verses.text: You should think of us as servants of Christ, the ones God has trusted to do the work of making known God’s secret truths.
Someone who does this work of God must always be faithful and do what God wants him to do.
But it is not important to me what people or a judge thinks of my work. I don’t even try to decide myself if I have done the right thing.
I don’t know of anything that I have done wrong, but I don’t say that I am innocent. God will say if I am guilty or not.

That is why you must not judge anyone now. The time for judging will be when the Lord comes. He will shine light on everything that is now hidden in darkness. He will make known the secret purposes of our hearts. Then the praise each person should get will come from God.

Brothers and sisters, I have used Apollos and myself as examples for you. I did this so that you could learn from us the meaning of the words, Follow what the Scriptures say. No one must say that one person is more important than another person.
Why do you think you are better than someone else? Everything that you have, God gave you. Why do you boast about the things that you did not work for?
You think you have everything you need. You think you are rich. You think you have become kings without us. I wish you really were kings. Then we could rule together with you

We are apostles, but I think God made us unimportant and He gave us the last place of all people. We are like prisoners on our way to die in a place where everyone can see us. And now everyone in the world, angels and people, can see how we die.

Everyone thinks that we, the apostles, are fools because we work for Christ. but you think you are so wise in Christ. You think we are weak but you are strong. You think you are important and we are not important.
Even now we still don’t have enough to eat or drink, and we don’t have enough clothes. We often get beatings. We have no homes.
We work very hard for food and clothes for ourselves. When people curse us, we pray that God will bless them. When they do bad things to us, we keep quiet and hold out.
When they say bad things about us, we try to say something good that will help them. We have become like the worst people in the world, like dirt. We are like dirty things that people throw away.
I am not trying to make you feel ashamed, but I am writing this to warn you as my own children.

You have ten thousand teachers to guide you in Christ, but you do not have many fathers who led you to Christ and assumed responsibility for you, for I became your father in Christ Jesus through the good news of salvation.
That is why I beg you to live as I live.

That is why I sent Timothy to you. I have helped Timothy to know the Lord Jesus and now he is like a son to me, and I love him very much and Timothy is faithful. He will help you to remember how I live in Christ Jesus. The way of life that I teach in every meeting of the church wherever I am.
Some of you thought I would never come to you again and they think they are very important now.
But I will soon come to you, if God wants me to come. I will find out if these people only talk or if they also do something.
Because where God is King, people don’t only talk about the things that God wants. They also do them with the power that God gives them.
You can choose what I must do when I come to you: Must I be angry, or must I be loving and friendly?

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: When one of you has a problem with another believer, why do you go to the law courts and let people who are not right with God decide it? Why not let God's people decide?
Don't you know that God's people will one day judge the world? And if you are going to judge the world, are you not able to decide small things?
Don't you know that we will judge angels? So of course we can decide about the things of this life.
So when you have these everyday problems, why do you take them to judges who mean nothing to the church?
I say this to make you ashamed. Is there really no one among you wise enough to settle a problem between two believers?
Instead, one believer takes another to court — and does it in front of people who do not believe!
The truth is, going to law against each other means you have already lost. Why not just accept the wrong? Why not let yourself be cheated?
But instead you do wrong and cheat others — and you do this to your own brothers and sisters.

Don't you know that people who do wrong will have no part in God's kingdom? Do not be fooled. People who sin sexually, or worship idols, or are unfaithful in marriage, or men who have sexual relations with other men,
or who steal, or are greedy, or get drunk, or say evil things about others, or cheat people — none of them will have a part in God's kingdom.
Some of you were like that. But you were washed clean. You were made holy. You were made right with God in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ and by the Spirit of our God.
Someone may say: I am allowed to do anything. But not everything is good for you. I am allowed to do anything, but I will not let anything control me.
Food is for the stomach, and the stomach is for food — but God will put an end to both. The body is not for sexual sin. The body is for the Lord, and the Lord is for the body.
By his power God raised the Lord from death, and he will raise us up too.
Don't you know that your bodies are parts of Christ himself? So should I take a part of Christ and join it to a prostitute? Never!
Don't you know that a man who joins himself to a prostitute becomes one body with her? For God says: The two will become one flesh.
But the one who joins himself to the Lord becomes one with him in spirit.
Run away from sexual sin. Every other sin a person does is outside the body, but the one who sins sexually sins against his own body.
Don't you know that your body is a temple for the Holy Spirit? The Spirit lives in you, and you received him from God. You do not belong to yourself.
God bought you for a price. So use your body to bring honor to God.

Now I will write about meat that is sacrificed to idols. It is certainly true that we all have knowledge. But this knowledge only fills people with pride. It is love that helps the church grow stronger.
Those who think they know something do not yet know anything as they should.
But whoever loves God is known by God.
Now I want to talk about meat that has been sacrificed to idols. We all know that no idol in the world is alive. We know that there is only one true living God.
Many people say there are gods in heaven or on earth and we know that people worship many gods and say they are lords

For us there is only one God, and he is our Father. All things came from the Father, and we live for him. And there is only one Lord, Jesus Christ. All things were made through him, and we also have life through him.

But not all the believers understand that there is only one God. They served these idols for many years before they became believers. They think that when they eat meat that was sacrificed to idols, they serve those idols. And because these believers feel guilty very easily, they think that they have sinned.
But food will not bring us closer to God. Refusing to eat does not make us less pleasing to God, and eating does not make us closer to him.
Those of you who think it is not wrong to eat meat that was sacrificed to idols must be careful because other believers may sin when they see what you eat.

If you think it is not wrong to eat meat that was sacrificed to idols and someone who does not have a strong faith sees you eating in a temple of an idol, what will he think? He will think that he too can eat there and maybe he will then start to serve those idols.
His faith is not strong and because you think it is not wrong to eat meat that was sacrificed to idols, he may be lost. But he is a believer, and Christ died for him.
When you do something that would make other believers feel guilty, then you sin against Christ.
That is why, if food makes someone sin, and that person believes in Christ like I do, then I will never eat meat again so that I will not make other believers sin.
Now, brothers and sisters, I want you to understand about spiritual gifts.
Remember the lives you lived before you were believers. You let yourselves be influenced and led away to worship idols, things that have no life.

That is why I tell you: If the Spirit of God is in someone’s heart, that person can not curse Jesus. And when someone believes that Jesus is the Lord, he can say that only because the Holy Spirit is in his heart.
There are different gifts, but it is the same Spirit who gives them.
And there are different ways to serve the Lord, but we serve the same Lord.
And there are different kinds of ways that God works in the congregation, but it is the same God who helps people do all these things.
The Spirit shows everyone what he must do, because the Spirit knows what is best for the congregation.
The Spirit gives one person the ability to speak with wisdom. And the same Spirit gives another person the ability to speak with knowledge.
The same Spirit gives faith to one person and to another he gives gifts of healing.

The Spirit gives to one person the power to do miracles, to another the ability to prophesy, and to another the ability to judge what is from the Spirit and what is not. The Spirit gives one person the ability to speak in different kinds of languages, and to another the ability to interpret those languages.
One Spirit, the same Spirit, does all these things. The Spirit decides what to give each one.
Every person has a body, but his body has many parts. Yes, there are many parts, but the many parts are one body. It is the same with the body of Christ. It has many parts.
When we were baptized, the Holy Spirit made us all part of one body. We are Jews, Greeks, slaves and free people but the same Spirit is in all of us.
Our bodies do not have only one part. They have many parts.
If the foot says: I am not part of the body because I am not the hand, is the foot then not part of the body? No, it is still part of the body.
If the ear says: I am not part of the body because I am not the eye, is the ear then not part of the body? No, it is still part of the body.
If the whole body were an eye, then the body would not have been able to hear. And if the whole body were an ear, then the body would not have been able to smell.
But now God has given different parts to the body. He gave every part as He wanted.
If the whole body were just one part, then it would not have been a body.
But now there are many parts, and there is only one body.
The eye can not say to the hand: I do not need you. The head can not say to the feet: I do not need you.
We think there are parts of the body that are weaker than other parts, but the body needs them.
And the parts that we think are not worth very much are the parts we give the most care to. And we give special care to the parts of the body that we don’t want to show.
The more beautiful parts don’t need this special care. But God put the body together and gave more honor to the parts that need it.
That is why the body does not fight against itself, because the different parts of the body care for each other.
When one part of the body suffers, then all the other parts also suffer. And when the people praise one part of the body, then all the other parts are also happy.
All of you together are the body of Christ. You all are a part of this body

God gives each one of us a place in the church. First there are the apostles, 2nd are the prophets, 3rd are those who teach other people, then the people who do miracles, then those who have the gift of healing people and then those who help other people. There are people who can speak in tongues.
Not all of them are apostles, not all are prophets, not everyone can teach other people and not everyone can do miracles.
Not all have the gift of healing other people, not everyone speaks in tongues, and not everyone can interpret what people say when they speak in tongues.
Continue to give your attention to the spiritual gifts you consider to be the greatest. But now I want to point out a way of life that is even greater
Now, brothers and sisters, I want you to remember the gospel I told you. You received that Good News, and you continue to base your life on it.
That Good News, the message you heard from me and believed, is God’s way to save you. Do not change the word which I preached to you, unless you believed in vain (without complete commitment].
I, Paul, gave you the message that I received. I told you the most important truths: that Christ died for our sins, as the Scriptures say.
Jesus was buried and Jesus was raised to life on the third day, as the Scriptures say;
Jesus appeared to Peter, then to all twelve apostles
Later Jesus appeared to more than 500 of his followers at once, most of whom are still alive, although some have died.
Then Jesus appeared to James and later to all the apostles.
Last of all, he appeared to me, Paul. I was different, like a baby born before the normal time.
All the other apostles are more important than I am. I am least worthy to be an apostle because I persecuted the people of the church of God.

Because of God’s grace, I am an apostle now. God’s grace that he gave me was not wasted. I worked harder than all the other apostles. (But I was not really the one working. It was God’s grace that was with me.)
It is not important who preaches to you, I or the other apostles. What is important is that we all preach the same message and that you believe it.

We tell everyone that Christ was raised from death. So why do some of you say that people will not be raised from death? 1 Corinthians 15: 13 If no one will ever be raised from death, then Christ has never been raised.
And if Christ has never been raised, then the message we tell is worth nothing. And your faith is worth nothing.

Then we have lied and we are false witnesses of God, because we said that God has raised Christ to live again. Because if the people who have died will not live again, then it is not true that God has raised Christ to live again. 1 Corinthians 15 16 If those who have died are not raised, then Christ has not been raised either.
And if God did not make Christ live again, then your faith is for nothing; you are still guilty of your sins
Then everyone who belonged to Christ and has died is lost.
If we hope that Christ saves us only for this life on earth, then everyone must feel very sorry for us.
But our message is true: God has raised Christ from death to live again, this is sure. Christ is the first One who died and was raised to live again.
We all die because of what one man, Adam, did. But all believers will be raised to live again because of what one Man, Christ, did.
We are all descendants of Adam. That is why we all die. But we also belong to Christ and that is why God will make us all live again.
But everyone will be raised to life in the right order. Christ was first to be raised. Then, when Christ comes again, those who belong to him will be raised to life.
Then the end will come. Christ will destroy all rulers, authorities, and powers. Then he will give the kingdom to God the Father
Christ will rule until God has defeated all enemies of Christ and put them under his feet.
The last enemy that Christ will defeat is death.

It is written in the Old Testament: God put everything under Christ control. When God says that everything is put under Christ, it is clear that this does not include God himself. God is the one putting everything under Christ’s control.

When God gives everything to Christ so that Christ can rule over it, then Christ, the Son of God, will also give Himself to God. Remember, God is the One who gave everything to Christ so that Christ can rule over it. That is why God will also rule over Christ in the end. Then God will rule over everything and everyone.
If no one will ever be raised from death, then what will the people do who are baptized for those who have died? If the dead are never raised, then why are people baptized for them?
30 And what about us? Why do we put ourselves in danger every hour if the dead will not live again?
I swear, dear brothers and sisters, that I face death daily, because as my pride in what Christ Jesus our Lord has done in you.
In Ephesus I fought wild animals. If I did that only for human reasons, then I have gained nothing. If we are not raised from death, Let us eat and drink, because tomorrow we die.
Do not let people deceive you, Bad friends will ruin good habits.
Come back to your right way of thinking and stop sinning. Some of you don’t know God. I say this to shame you.
Some people ask: What will it be like when the dead people live again? What will their bodies look like?
Why do they ask these foolish questions? When you plant something, it must die in the ground before it can live and grow.
And when you plant something, what you plant does not have the same body that it will have later. What you plant is only a seed, maybe wheat or something else
But God gives it the body that he has planned for it, and he gives each kind of seed its own body.
All things made of flesh are not the same: People have one kind of flesh, animals have another, birds have another, and fish have yet another kind.
Also there are heavenly bodies and earthly bodies. But the beauty of the heavenly bodies is one kind, and the beauty of the earthly bodies is another.
The sun has one kind of beauty, the moon has another kind, and the stars have another. And each star is different in its beauty.
It will be the same when those who have died are raised to life. The body that is buried in the grave will ruin and decay, but it will be raised to a life that cannot be destroyed.
When the body is buried it is without honor. But when it is raised, it will be great and glorious. When the body is buried, it is weak. But when it is raised, it will be full of power.
The body that is buried is a physical body. When it is raised, it will be a spiritual body. There is only a human body, there is also a spiritual body.
In the Old Testament it is written: The first man, Adam, became a living person. the last Adam, Christ, became a life-giving spirit to restoring the dead to life.
Remember, the spiritual body did not come first. The human body was first and after that, the spiritual body came.
God made the first man from the dust of the earth. But the 2nd Man is the Lord from heaven.

People who belong to this world are like the first man. They are like the man who came from. But people who belong to heaven are like the Man who came from heaven. Christ is the One who came from heaven.
Now we all are like the man of dust, but the time will come when we will be like the Man from heaven.
I tell you this, brothers and sisters: Our bodies of flesh and blood cannot have a part in God’s kingdom. Something that will ruin cannot have a part in something that never ruins.
Now I want to tell you a secret: Not all of us will die, but God will change us all.

It will only take the time of a second. We will be changed as quickly as an eye blink. This will happen when the last trumpet blows. The trumpet will blow and those who have died will be raised to live forever. And we will all be changed.
Because this body that we have now can become dust, God will give us a body that can not become dust. This body that we have now can die, but God will give us a body that will never die.
When God has given us a body that can not die or become dust, we will know that the words in the Old Testament are true where it is written: God has defeated death.
Death, where is your victory? Where is your power to hurt?
The power and sting of death come from sin, and the power of sin comes from the law.
But we thank God who gives us the victory through our Lord Jesus Christ!
So, my dear brothers and sisters, stand strong. Don’t let anything change you. Always give yourselves fully to the work of the Lord. You know that your work in the Lord is never wasted.

We want to tell you about the Word that gives life. The one who existed before the world began. This is the one we have heard and have seen with our own eyes. We saw what he did, and our hands touched him 1st John

Yes, Jesus, the one who is life was shown to us. We saw Jesus, and so we can tell others about Jesus. We now tell you about Jesus. Jesus is the eternal life that was with God the Father and was shown to us. 1st John
We are telling you about what we have seen and heard. We want you to have fellowship with us. The fellowship we share together is with God the Father and his Son Jesus Christ 1st John
We write these things to that your joy will be full of joy. 1st John
We heard the true teaching from God. Now we tell it to you: God is light, and in him there is no darkness. 1st John
So if we say that we share in life with God, but we continue living in darkness, we are liars, who don’t follow the truth. 1st John
But if we live in the light as God is in the light. We have fellowship with each other, and the blood of Jesus, God’s Son, washes away every sin and makes us clean. 1st John
If we say we do not have sin, then we deceive ourselves and we do not accept the truth. 1st John
If we say that we have sinned and we confess our sins, then we can be sure that God will forgive our sins and wash away everything that we have done wrong 1st John
If we say we have not sinned, then it means we say that God lies. Then we do not accept what God tells us.

My children, I write these things to you because I do not want you to sin. But if one of you does sin, he can be sure that we have Someone with the Father who asks God to forgive us, and that Person is Jesus Christ, the One who did not sin.
Jesus is the way our sins are taken away. Jesus did not take away only our sins. But Jesus also took away the sins of all the people in the world. 1st john
If we obey what God has told us to do, then we are sure that we know him. 1
If people say they know God, but they don’t do what God says, they lie and they do not accept the truth.
But if people do what God tells them to do, God love is truly working in the. This is how we know that we are living in him.

If people say they belong to God, we must live the way Jesus lived. 1 John 2;7 Dear friends, I am not writing a new command to you. It is the same command that you have had since the beginning. This command is the teaching that you have already heard.
But there is something new about this law. You can see this truth in Jesus and in yourselves. The darkness is passing away, and the true light is already shining.
If people say that they live in the light of God, but they hate other believers, then those people still live in the dark.
If people love other believers, then they live in the light and there is nothing in them that will make them do wrong.
If people hate other believers, then they live in the dark. They are like people who walk in the dark and they do not know where they are going because they cannot see in the dark.
I write to you, dear children, because your sins are forgiven through Christ.

I am writing to you fathers, those believers and are spiritually mature, because you know God who has existed from the beginning. I am writing to you, young people, those believers who are growing in spiritual maturity, because you have been victorious and have overcome the evil one. I have written to you, children, those who are new believers, those spiritually immature, because you have come to know the Father. 1 John 2: I have written to you fathers, the mature ones, because you know God who has existed from the beginning. I have written to you, young people, because you are strong, and the word of God remains in you, and you have been victorious over the evil one.
You must not love this sinful world or the things that are in this world. If you love this sinful world, then you do not love the Father.

This is all there is in the world: wanting to please our sinful selves, wanting the sinful things we see, and being too proud of what we have. But none of these comes from the Father. They come from the world.
The world is passing away, and all the things that people want in the world are passing away. But whoever does what God want will live forever. 1John
My dear children, the end is near! You have heard that the antichrist is coming. And now many antichrist are already here. So, we know that the end is near. 1John

These enemies were in our group, but they left us. They did not really belong with us. If they were really part of our group, they would have stayed with us. But they left. This shows that none of them really belonged with us. 1John
But you have an anointing from the Holy, and all of you know. 1John
I write this letter to you, not because you don’t know the truth, but because you know the truth and you know that something that is true now cannot later become a lie. 1John
So who is the liar? It is the one who says Jesus is not the Messiah. He the liar is the antichrist; he is the one who does not believe in the Father or in his Son. 1John
Whoever does not believe in the Son does not have the Father, but whoever accepts the Son has the Father too. 1John
As for you, let what you have heard from the beginning remain in you. If it does, you will also remain in the Son and in the Father. 1John
And this is what the Jesus has promised us, eternal life. 1John
I am writing this letter about those who are trying to lead you into the wrong way. 1John

Remember that Jesus has anointed you with the Holy Spirit and the Holy Spirit is in you. That is why you do not need other people to teach you. Jesus has anointed you with the Holy Spirit and the Holy Spirit teaches you everything that you must know. He speaks the truth, and He does not lie. He teaches you to live with Jesus and you must do that. 1John
Yes, my dear children, live in him. If we do this, we can be without fear on the day when Christ comes again. We will not need to hide and be ashamed when he comes. 1John
You know that Christ always did what was right. So you know that all those who do what is right are God’s children.
Dear friends, do not believe everyone who claims to speak by the Spirit. You must test them to see if the spirit they have comes from God. For there are many false prophets in the world.
This is how you can recognize God’s Spirit. When people say they believe that Jesus is the Christ who really came to the world and became a Man, then they have the Spirit of God in them

But people who say they don’t believe that Jesus Christ really became a Man do not have the Spirit of God in them. They have the spirit of the antichrist in them. They have told you that the antichrist will come. But I tell you: He is already here in the world.
My dear children, you belong to God, you have already defeated these false prophets. That’s because God is in you is greater than the one who is in the world.
The false prophets are people of this world. That is why the false prophets speak like the people of this world and the people of this world do what the false prophets tell them.

We are the children of God and everyone who knows God listen to what we say. Everyone who is not a child of God does not listen to what we say. This is how we know the Spirit that is true and the spirit that is false.
You that are loved of God, we must love each other because love comes from God. Everyone who loves other people is a child of God and knows God.
People who do not love other people do not know God, because God is love.
God showed us how much He loves us when God gave his only Son to come to the world. God’s Son came to give us eternal life.
True love is God’s love for us, not our love for God. He sent his Son as the way to take away our sins.
You that are loved of God, God has shown us how much He loves us, so now we must also love each other.
No person has ever seen God. If we love each other, then God lives with us and we are full of his love.
We know that we live with God and that He lives with us because He has given his Spirit to us.
We have seen and now we tell other people that the Father has sent his Son so that his Son can be the Saviour of the world.
If people say they believe that Jesus is the Son of God, then God is with them, and they are with God.
We have seen God’s love and we believe that God loves us. God is love and everyone who loves other people lives with God and God lives with him.
If God’s love is made perfect in us, we can be without fear on the day when God judges the world. We will be without fear, because in this world we are like Jesus
Where God’s love is, there is no fear, because God’s perfect love takes away fear. (Fear has to do with punishment) God’s love is not made perfect in the one who is afraid.
We love God, because God first loved us.
If we say we love God but hate any of our brothers or sisters in God’s family, we are liars. If we don’t love someone we have seen, how can we love God? We have never even seen him.
God has told us that if we love Him, we must also love the other believers.
Naboth answered, The Lord told me I should not give you my land. This land belongs to my family.
When Ahab went home, he was sad and angry because Naboth of Jezreel had told him he would not give him his family’s land. So, Ahab went to bed, turned away from everyone, and refused to eat.
His wife Jezebel came to him and asked him: Why are you sad and why don’t you eat?
He said to her: I talked to Naboth of Jezreel and I asked him to give me his vineyard. I said I would pay for it or give him another vineyard, but he said he would not give me his vineyard.
Ahab’s wife Jezebel said to him: You are the king of Israel. Get up and eat and be happy. I will get you the vineyard of Naboth of Jezreel.
Then Jezebel wrote some letters. She signed Ahab’s name to them and used his seal to seal the letters. Then she sent them to the elders and important men who lived in the same town as Naboth.
In the letters she wrote: Announce that there will be a day of fasting when the people will eat nothing. And sit Naboth in front of the people.
Find 2 men who will tell lies about Naboth. They should say that they heard Naboth cursing God and speak against the king. Then take Naboth out of the city and kill him with stones.
So the elders and important men of Jezreel obeyed the command that Jezebel had written in the letters
They called all the people to the fast and they told Naboth to come and sit in front of the people.

Then the 2 men who were going to tell lies about Naboth sat down in front of Naboth. Then the 2 men told the people that they heard Naboth cursing God and speaking against the king. Then they carried Naboth out of the city, and they killed him with stones.
Then they went to Jezebel and told her that they had killed Naboth with stones.
Then Jezebel heard that Naboth was stoned to death, she said to Ahab, Naboth is dead. Now you can go and take the vineyard that Naboth did not want to sell to you.
When Ahab heard that Naboth was dead, he went to the vineyard of Naboth of Jezreel to take it for himself.
The Lord said to Elijah, the prophet from Tishbe
Go meet Ahab, king of Israel in Samaria, he is in the vineyard of Naboth. He went there to take it for himself.

Tell Ahab that I, the Lord, say to him, Ahab! You killed the man Naboth and now you are taking his land. So I, the Lord, tell you this: Where the dogs licked up the blood of Naboth, the dogs will lick your blood as well.
So Elijah went to Ahab. When Ahab saw him, he said, my enemy has found me again! Elijah answered: ‘Yes, I have found you because you have again sold yourself out to do what the Lord says is evil

The Lord says: I will make you suffer. I will kill you and every man and boy in your family who lives in the land of Israel. I will kill every one of your male descendants, slave and free alike, anywhere in Israel!

I will do with your royal family what I did to the family of Jeroboam, son of Nebat, and the family of Baasha, son of Ahijah. I will do this to you because you have made me angry, and you have caused the Israelites to sin.

The Lord also talked about Jezebel. He said: The dogs will eat Jezebel at the wall of Jezreel. 1 Kings 21:24 If someone of Ahab’s family dies in a town, the dogs will eat him, and if one of Ahab’s family dies in the field, the birds will eat him. 1 Kings 21:25 So Ahab sold himself out to do what the Lord says is evil. There is no one who did as much evil as Ahab. His wife Jezebel caused him to do these things. 1 Kings 21:26 Ahab committed terrible sin of worshiping those filthy idols, just as the Amorites did. And that is why the Lord took the land from them and gave it to the Israelites. 1 Kings 21:27 When Ahab heard what Elijah said, he tore his clothes (to show how sad he was). Then he put on sackcloth and refused to eat. He even slept in these clothes. He was very sad and upset and he did not speak to anyone. 1 Kings 21:28 The Lord said to Elijah from Tishbe: 1 Kings 21:29 “Look, Ahab has humbled himself before me. I will not make all those bad things happen while he is
In the year that King Uzziah died I, Isaiah, saw the Lord sitting on a very high and wonderful throne. His long robe filled the Temple. Isaiah
Above God were Seraphims, special angels. Each angel had six wings. They used two wings to cover their faces, two wings to cover their feet, and two wings to fly. Isaiah
The angels were calling to each other said: Holy, holy, holy is the Lord who rules over all. His glory is everywhere on earth. Isaiah
The angels talked so loudly that it caused the frame around the door to shake, and the Temple was filled with smoke Isaiah

I, Isaiah, was frightened and said I said: Oh, no! I will be destroyed. I am not pure enough to speak to God, and I live among people who are not pure enough to speak to God. But I have seen the King, the Lord All-Powerful. Isaiah
There was a fire on the altar. One of the angels used a pair of tongs to take a hot coal from the fire. Then the angel flew to me with it in his hand. Isaiah
Then he touched my mouth with the hot coal and said, “When this hot coal touched your lips, your sin was taken away, your sins were erased Isaiah
Then the Lord asked: Is there anyone who I can send? Who will take a message to the people? Then I, Isaiah, said: Here I am. Send me. Isaiah
The Lord said to me: Go and tell the people: You will hear and listen to what I say, but you will not understand what I say. You will look, but you will not know what you see. Isaiah

Make the heart of this people insensitive, Their ears dull, And their eyes dim, Otherwise they might see with their eyes, Hear with their ears, Understand with their hearts, And return and be healed. Isaiah

Then I, Isaiah, asked: Lord, for how long must I do this? And the Lord answered, Do this until the cities are destroyed and all the people are gone. Do this until there is no one left living in the houses and the land is destroyed and empty. Isaiah
The Lord will make the people go far away, and there will be large areas of empty land in the country. Isaiah

A tenth of the people will be allowed to stay in the land, but it will be destroyed again. They will be like an oak tree. When the tree is chopped down, a stump is left. This stump will be a very special seed that will grow again.
The Lord commanded a man of God from Judah to go to the city of Bethel. King Jeroboam was standing at the altar offering incense when the man of God arrived.

The Lord had commanded the man of God to speak against the altar. He said, Altar, the Lord says to you: David’s family will have a son. His name will be Josiah. The priests of the high places are now burning incense on you, but Josiah will offer the priests on you and burn human bones on you, so you can never be used again!
On that day the man of God also said: The Lord will give this sign: This altar will break apart, and the ashes on it will fall onto the ground

When King Jeroboam heard the message from the man of God about the altar in Bethel, he took his hand off the altar and pointed at the man. He said, Arrest that man! But when the king said this, his hand became paralyzed. He could not move it.
Then the altar broke into pieces, and all its ashes fell onto the ground. This proved that what the man of God had said came from the Lord

The king asked the man of God: Please talk to the Lord your God and pray for me and ask Him to heal my hand. The man of God prayed, and the king’s hand was right again. It was the same as it had been before.
The king said to the man of God: Come home with me and eat some food. I want to give you a gift.
But the man of God said to the king: I will not come with you, not even if you give me half of your kingdom. I will not eat bread or drink water in this place.
because the Lord told me: Do not eat bread or drink no water there and God also commanded me not to go back the same way I came.
Then the man of God went home by another road. He did not go back the same way as he had come to Bethel.
There was an old prophet living in Bethel. His sons came home and told their father what the man of God had done in Bethel and what he had said to the king.
The father asked his sons: Which way did he go? And his sons showed their father which road the man of God from Judah had taken.
The father told his sons to get his donkey and to put a saddle on it, and then he got onto the donkey

The father rode and started to look for the man of God. He found the man of God sitting under an oak tree. The old prophet asked him: Are you the man of God who came from Judah? The man of God said: Yes, I am.
The old prophet said to him: Come home with me and eat some bread.
The man of God told the old prophet: No, I may not go back with you to your home. I may not eat bread or drink water in this place.
because the Lord said to me: Do not eat bread or drink water in this place and do not go back on the road that you came by.

The old prophet said to the man of God: I am also a prophet like you. An angel gave me a message from the Lord, and he said: You must bring the man of God to your house, and you must give him bread to eat and water to drink. But the old prophet lied to the man of God.
Then the man of God went with the old prophet to his home, and he eat bread and drink water.
While they sat at the table, the Lord talked to the old prophet who had told the man of God to come to his home.
The old prophet spoke to the man of God from Judah. He said, The Lord said that you did not obey him! You did not do what he commanded.

He said you must not come back and eat bread and drink water in this place, but you came back. You did what He said you must not do. That is why they will not bury you in the tomb of your grandfathers.
After the man of God had finished eating bread and finished drinking water the old prophet who had told him to come back put a saddle on his donkey.
The man of God went away and on his way a lion came and killed him. The man’s body lay on the road, and the donkey and the lion stood next to his body.

Some people walked by, and they saw that the body of the man of God was lying on the road and that the lion was standing next to the body. They went and told it to the people in the town where the old prophet lived.

The old prophet who had told the man of God to come back heard people talking about it and he said: It is the man of God who did not listen to the Lord. The Lord sent the lion to attack and kill him. It happened as the Lord told him.
The old prophet told his sons to put a saddle on his donkey and they did it.

He went, and he found the body of the man of God where it lay on the road. The donkey and the lion were still standing next to the body of the man of God. The lion had not eaten the body and it had not killed the donkey.
The old prophet took the body of the man of God, and he put it on his donkey. He took the body back to his town to mourn for the man of God and to bury him.
He put the body in his own grave and he mourned for him and said: Oh, my brother, I am sad.
After he had buried the man of God, the old prophet said to his sons: When I die, you must bury me in this grave where I have buried the man of God. You must put my body next to his body
because the Lord sent him and everything will happen that he said against the altar in Bethel and against all the places of sacrifice in the towns of Samaria, this is sure.

After this, Jeroboam did not stop doing the bad things that he was doing. He continued to choose priests from other tribes, and not from the Levi tribe. Anyone who wanted to become a priest could be a priest at the altars that he had built.
This was the sin that Jeroboam and his family did, and that is why there is not one of his family that is still a king.

Elijah was a prophet from the town of Tishbe in Gilead. He said to King Ahab, I serve the Lord, the God of Israel. By his power, I promise that no dew or rain will fall for 3 years. The rain will fall only when I command it to fall.
Then the Lord said to Elijah:
You must go away. Go east over the Jordan River and hide in the Kerith Valley.
You can drink water from the stream in the valley and I have told the ravens to bring you food.
Elijah did what the Lord told him. He went to the brook of Cherith, which is on the other side of the Jordan River.
Every morning and every evening the ravens brought him bread and meat and he had water from the stream in the valley.
It did not rain and later there was no more water in the Kerith Valley.
Then the Lord said to Elijah:
You must go to the town of Zarephath in the land of Zidon. Go and stay there. I have told a widow who lives there to give you food.
Elijah went to Zarephath and when he came to the gate of the town, a widow was there getting wood to make a fire. He called her and said: Please give me a jar of water to drink.
She went to get the water and Elijah said: Please bring me a piece of bread too.

She said: I do not have any bread. That is as true as the Lord your God lives. I have only a handful of flour in a jar and a little oil in a bottle. I am getting wood to make a fire to make the last meal for my son and myself, and then we will have no food and we will die.
Elijah said to her: ‘Do not fear. Go and do what you have said, but first make me a small loaf of bread and bring it to me, and then you can make bread for yourself and your son.
The Lord God of Israel says the jar of flour will not become empty and there will always be enough oil in the bottle until the day when the Lord makes it rain again in the land.
The widow went home and she did what Elijah told her and every day she had food to eat, she and Elijah and her family.
The jar of flour did not become empty and there was always oil in the bottle, as the Lord had told Elijah to tell her.
The Lord said to Elijah in the third year of no rain, Go meet with King Ahab, and tell him I the LORD will make it rain again.
Elijah went to meet King Ahab. Because of no rain for 3 years there was a famine in Samaria.
So King Ahab told Obadiah to come to him. Obadiah was the man in charge of the king’s palace. (Obadiah was a true follower of the Lord.

When Jezebel killed the prophets of God, Obadiah hid 100 prophets in 2 caves, 50 prophets in each cave, and he gave them food and water. 1 Kings 18: King Ahab said to Obadiah, Come with me. We will look at every spring and every stream in the land. We will see if we can find enough grass to keep our horses and mules alive.
Ahab and Obadiah divided the land and Ahab went one way by himself and Obadiah went another way by himself.
On his way Obadiah met Elijah and when Obadiah saw it was Elijah, he bowed down with his face to the ground and he asked: Elijah, is that really you?
Elijah answered Obadiah and said: Yes, it is I. Go and tell your king that I am here.
Obadiah asked: What sin have I done? Why do you want King Ahab to kill me?

Obadiah said, I promise by the LORD your God that the king has searched every nation and kingdom on earth from end to end to find you. And each time he was told, Elijah isn’t here, King Ahab made the king of that nation to promise to the truth of his claim. That you were not there in their country
And now you say: Go and tell your king that Elijah is here.

If I go away from you now, and the Spirit of God takes you away to another place and I do not know where you are, what will happen to me? If I tell Ahab you are here, and you are not here when he comes, he will kill me. You must know that I have served the Lord from the time I was young.
Did you not heard what I did when Jezebel started to kill the prophets of the Lord? I hid 50 prophets in one cave and 50 prophets in another cave and I gave them food and water.
And now you say I must go to my king and tell him that Elijah is here. Do you want him to kill me?’
Elijah said to Obadiah: As sure as the Lord lives, in whose presence I stand, Obadiah don’t have to worry. Ahab will see me today.
Obadiah went to Ahab and he told him, and Ahab came to meet Elijah.
When the king saw Elijah, he said: Elijah Is that you, the one who brings trouble and death to Israel?

Elijah said to Ahab: It is not I who bring trouble and death to Israel. It is you and your family who did it when you stopped listening to the laws of God and when you started to serve the Baal idols.
Now tell all the people of Israel to meet me at Mount Carmel. Also bring the 450 prophets of Baal and the 400 prophets of the goddess Asherah, who eat at Jezebel’s table must also come
King Ahab sent messengers to all the children of Israel, and he told those prophets to come to Mount Carmel.

Elijah came and stood in front of all the people and Elijah asked them: How long will you keep jumping from one decision to serve God to the other decision to serve Baal. If the Lord is God, then you must serve Him. Or, if Baal is god, then you must serve him. But the people did not answer him. They kept quiet.
Then Elijah said to the people: ‘I am the only prophet of God that is still here, but there are 450 prophets of Baal.

Bring us 2 bulls. The prophets of Baal must choose one bull. They must cut the bull into pieces and put it on the wood on the altar, but they must not make fire on the altar. I will do the same with the other bull. I will put it on the wood, and I will not make fire on the altar.
Prophets of Baal, pray to your god, and I will pray to the Lord. Whichever god answers the prayer and starts the fire is the true God. All the people agreed that this was a good idea.
Then Elijah said to the prophets of Baal: You can choose one bull and prepare it first, because there are many of you. You must pray to your god, but you must not start a fire on your altar.

The prophets of Baal took the bull that Elijah gave them, they prepared it and they prayed to Baal. They kept on praying from morning to noon and asked: Baal, help us. But nothing happened, and no one answered. The prophets of Baal started jumping around on the altar they had built.

At noon Elijah began to make fun of them. He said, If Baal really is a god, maybe you should pray louder! Maybe he is busy. Maybe he is thinking about something, or maybe he stepped out for a moment! He could be sleeping! Maybe you should pray louder and wake him up!
So the prophets prayed louder. They cut themselves with swords and spears. (This was the way they worshiped.) They cut themselves until they were bleeding all over

The afternoon went by, and they continued praying until that evening, but nothing happened. No one answered them and Baal did not listen to them. Now it is time that the priest always sacrificed the evening offering,
Elijah said to all the people: Come here. Come to me. They came and Elijah built the altar of the Lord again. It was the altar that had been broken.
He took 12 stones, one stone for each tribe, the descendants of the sons of Jacob, the man to whom the Lord had said: Your name will be Israel.
Elijah built the stones into the altar of the Lord, then he dug a ditch around the altar. The ditch was so deep that 2 measure of seeds could go into it (around 4 gallons of water)
Then he put the wood on the altar, and he cut the bull into pieces and put it on top of the wood. Then Elijah said: You must fill 4 barrels of water and pour it over the offering and over the wood
Elijah told them to do it again, and he said unto them again do it a 3rd time.
The water ran down the altar and it filled the ditch.

Late that afternoon when it was time for the evening-offering, the prophet Elijah stood next to the altar and he said: Lord, God of Abraham, Isaac and Israel, let everyone see today that You are the God in Israel. Let everyone see that I am your servant and that it is You who have told me to do all these things.
Answer me, O Lord, answer me, so that all these people will know that You, Lord, are God and that it is You who change them and turn their hearts back to You.
The Lord then sent fire down and the fire burnt the offering and the wood and the stones and the ground and everything. There was no more water in the ditch, and the ditch was dry.
When the people saw this, they all bowed down with their faces to the ground. They said: The Lord, He is God. The Lord, He is God.
Elijah said to them: Catch the prophets of Baal. Not one of them must escape. They caught the prophets and Elijah took them to the Kishon River and he killed them there.
Then Elijah said to Ahab: Go, eat and drink because I can see a lot of rain coming.
Ahab went home to eat, and Elijah went to Mount Carmel. There he bowed down to the ground and he put his head between his knees.

He told the young man with him: Go up the mountain and look at the sea. The young man went up higher and he looked and said: I see nothing. When he came back Elijah told the young man to go and look again. He did this 7 times.

When the young man went for the 7th time, he came back and said: There is a small cloud coming from the sea. The cloud is as big as my hand. Then Elijah said: Go and tell Ahab he must get his horses and chariot ready and go, because later the rain will stop him, and he will not get away.
While Elijah was saying that, the sky became dark with black clouds. There were strong winds and a lot of rain. Ahab got into his chariot, and he went to the town of Jezreel.
The power of the Lord came to Elijah. He used his belt to hold up the bottom of his robe away from his feet. Then he ran ahead of King Ahab all the way to Jezreel.
Ahab told Jezebel everything that Elijah had done, and also that he had killed all the prophets of Baal with a sword.
Then Jezebel sent a message to Elijah and said: I promise that by this time tomorrow, you will be just as dead as those prophets. If I don’t succeed, may the gods do the same or worse to me.
When Elijah heard this, he was afraid. So he ran away to save his life. He took his servant with him, and they went to Beersheba in Judah. Then Elijah left his servant in Beersheba

Then Elijah went on alone. He walked for a day into the desert. He stopped and sat down in the shade of a juniper tree, and he wished that he was dead. He said: Lord, this is enough. Let me die because I am not better than my grandfathers.
Then Elijah lay down under the juniper tree and he slept there. Later an angel came to him and touched him and said: Get up and eat.
Elijah looked around, and by his head there was a cake that had been baked over coals and a jar of water. He ate and drank and then went back to sleep.
Later the angel of the Lord touched him again and said: Get up and eat. If you do not eat, you will not be strong enough to go all the way.
Elijah got up and he started to eat and drink. And that food made him so strong that he walked for 40 days and 40 nights to Mount Horeb, the mountain of God.
There he went into a cave, to sleep. Elijah heard the Lord talking to him. The Lord asked him: Elijah, what are you doing here?

Elijah answered, Lord God, I have always served you the best I can, but the Israelites have broken their agreement with you. They destroyed your altars and killed your prophets with the sword. I am the only prophet left alive, and now they are trying to kill me!

Then the Lord said to Elijah: Go out and stand before Me on the mountain. Then the Lord went past him. There was a very strong wind, so strong that the mountain shook and the rocks broke off before the Lord. But the Lord was not in the wind. After the wind there was an earthquake. But the Lord was not in the earthquake.
And after the earthquake there was a fire. But the Lord was not in the fire. Then after the fire Elijah heard something. It was like someone talking very softly.
When he heard this, he covered his face with his robe. He went out of the cave and he stood there. Then he heard the voice of God saying: Elijah, what are you doing here?

Elijah said, Lord God I have always served you the best that I can, but the Israelites broke their agreement with you. They destroyed your altars and killed your prophets. I am the only prophet left alive, and now they are trying to kill me.
Then the Lord said to Elijah: Go back the same way that you came. You must go into the desert of Damascus, and when you get there you must anoint Hazael to be king of the Syrian people.
And you must anoint Jehu, son of Nimshi, to be king of Israel. And you must anoint Elisha, son of Shaphat, from the town of AbelMeholah, to be a prophet after you.
Hazael will start killing people and if there are some people who flee from Hazael, then Jehu will kill them. And if there are some people who flee away from Jehu, then Elisha will kill them.
But there are still 7 000 people left in the land of Israel who did not bow before Baal. They have not worshipped or kissed him.

Elijah went from Mount Horeb to look for Elisha, son of Shaphat. Elisha was busy ploughing with 12 pairs of oxen, and Elisha was with the last pair, at the back. When Elijah came to him, he threw his robe over Elisha.

Elisha left the oxen, and he ran after Elijah and said to him: I first want to go to my father and mother and say goodbye, then I will go with you. Elijah said to him: You may go. I will not stop you.

When Elisha came back, he killed 2 oxen, and He took the wooden yokes and made a fire and then he cooked the meat and gave the meat to his people. When they had finished eating Elisha went with Elijah. Elisha became Elijah’s helper.

There was a man named Elkanah from the Zuph family who lived in Ramathaimzophim, in the hill country of Ephraim. Elkanah was the son of Jeroham, the son of Elihu, the son of Tohu, the son of Zuph from the tribe of Ephraim. 1st Samuel
Elkanah had 2 wives. The name of one wife was Hannah and the other wife was Peninnah. Peninnah had children but Hannah did not have any children. 1st Samuel

Every year Elkanah left his town of Ramathaimzophim and went to Shiloh. Elkanah worshiped the Lord the All-Powerful. There in Shiloh Elkanah offered sacrifices to the Lord. The 2 sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, were the priests of the Lord in the town of Shiloh. 1st Samuel
When Elkanah gave an offering to the Lord, he gave pieces of the meat offering to his wife Peninnah and to each of her sons and daughters. 1st Samuel
But to Hannah, Elkanah gave a double portion because he loved her, and the LORD had closed her womb. 1st Samuel
Peninnah would always make Hannah angry and humiliate because the Lord did not give Hannah any children. 1st Samuel
This happened every year when their family went to the Lord’s house at Shiloh. So, every year Peninnah would upset Hannah so much that she would begin to cry and would not eat anything.
Then Hannah’s husband, Elkanah, said to her: Hannah, why are you crying? Why don’t you eat? Why are you sad? You know you have me and that is better than having 10 sons.
After eating and drinking in Shiloh, Hannah went to the temple of the Lord. The priest Eli was sitting on a chair at the door of the tent.
Hannah was very sad. She prayed to the Lord and cried for a long time.

Hannah made a special promise to God and said, Lord, you are the one who rule everything, I am your servant, please look at me and see how sad I am. Remember me. Don’t forget me. If you will give me a son, I will bring him here to belong to You for as long as he shall lives and no one will shave off the hair on his head.
Hannah kept on praying to God for a long time and Eli saw her talking.
But Hannah did not speak loudly, Hannah was praying in her heart. Her lips were moving, but since she did not say the words out loud, Eli thought she was drunk.
Then Eli said to Hannah: You have had too much to drink. It is time to put away the wine.
But Hannah said to Eli: No, sir, I have not drunk wine or strong alcohol. I am very sad and I told the Lord how I feel.
Hannah said to Eli, Do not think I am a bad woman. I have talked to the Lord about all my problems and my pain.
Eli said to Hannah: Go in peace. The God of Israel will give to you what you have asked Him for.
Hannah answered: I hope that you will think well of me. Hannah went away and eating something, she was not sad anymore.
The next morning Elkanah and his family got up, prayed to the Lord and then went back to their home in the town of Ramah. Elkanah had sex with Hannah and the Lord remembered what Hannah had asked.
It happen that Hannah became with child and gave birth to a son. Hannah named him Samuel. She said: His name is Samuel because I asked the Lord to give him to me.
Elkanah went to Shiloh to offer the sacrifice for that year and the gift he had promised to the Lord. He took his family with him.

Hannah did not go with them. She said to her husband, Elkanah: When the boy is old enough to eat solid food, I will take him to Shiloh. I will take Samuel and I will give him to the Lord to work for Him. Then Samuel can always stay there.

Her husband Elkanah said to Hannah: You must do what you think is good. You may stay home until the Samuel is old enough to eat solid food. I pray that God will do everything that He has promised you. Hannah stayed at home until her son, Samuel, stopped drinking milk from her.
When the boy ,Samuel, was old enough to eat solid food, Hannah took Samuel to the Lord’s house at Shiloh. She also took 3 bull, 10 pounds of flour, and a bottle of wine.
They sacrificed a bull and then they took the Samuel to Eli the priest.
Hannah said to Eli: Sir, do you remember me? I am the woman who was here with you and prayed to the Lord.
I prayed to have this child and the Lord has given me what I asked Him for.
And now I give this child to the Lord. He will serve the Lord all his life. Samuel worship the Lord there.
The Philistines took The Holy Box of God, from Ebenezer to Ashdod.
The Philistines took The Holy Box of God to the temple of Dagon and they put it down next to the idol of Dagon.
When the people of Ashdod got up the next morning, they saw Dagon lying there on his face to the ground before the Holy Box of the Lord. They took Dagon and they put him back where he had been.

But when they got up the next morning, they saw that Dagon was lying down on the ground again before the Holy Box of the Lord. Dagon’s head and his hands were broken and they were lying on the doorstep. Only Dagon’s body was not broken.
This is why the priests of Dagon, and everyone else who comes into the temple of Dagon, never step on the doorstep of the temple, even until today.

The Lord punished the people of Ashdod. He made them afraid by destroyed their lands and He made sores come out on their bodies. The sores were on all the people of Ashdod and also on all the other people in their land.
When the people of Ashdod saw what was happening, they said: The Holy Box of the God of Israel must not stay with us, because He is the One who punishes us and our god Dagon.

They called all the leaders of the Philistines together and they asked them: What must we do with the Holy Box of the God of Israel? The leaders answered: You must take the Holy Box of the God of Israel to the town Gath. So, they took the covenant box of the God of Israel to Gath.

But after they had taken it there, the Lord made all the people of Gath very afraid. Destroyed the town and He made all people, the children and the old, very sick. They had sores in their bottom parts.
They then sent the Holy Box of God to the town of Ekron. When it came into the town, the people of Ekron shouted and said: They have sent the Holy Box of the God of Israel here to kill our people.

Then they called all the leaders of the Philistines together and said to them: You must send the Holy Box of the God of Israel back to its own place. If it stays here, it will kill all of us. The people of Ekron said this because everyone in the town was afraid from the destruction and sickness. They thought they would die. God made them suffer a lot.
Those who didn’t die were punished with sores all over their bodies. The people of Ekron cried loudly to heaven.
We could not come to you, but it was very hard to wait any longer. So we decided that Silas and I, Paul would stay in Athens.
Timothy will go to you. Timothy is a believer who works for God, preaching the Good News of Christ with us. Silas and I are sent Timothy to strengthen and encourage you in your faith.
Silas and I are sending him because we did not want you to start doubting because of the troubles you are having now. You know that believers will suffer in this world.
When we were with you, we told you that we would all have to suffer. And you know that it happened just as we said.

I, Paul, could not wait any longer. I wanted to know how you were doing. That why I told Timothy to go and find out if your faith in Christ was still strong. I was afraid that the devil who tempts people might have defeated you with troubles. Then our hard work would have been wasted.

Timothy has already come back from you and Timothy has given us the good news that your faith in God is good and your love for each other is strong. Timothy also said that you think of the good things when you remember us and you long to see us just as we long to see you.
For this reason, brothers and sisters, during all our trouble and suffering we have been comforted and greatly encouraged about you because of your faith.
Your faith in the Lord is strong and that makes us feel good and alive.
When we think of you, there are so many things that make us happy before our God. When we pray, we can not thank Him enough for what He has done for you.
Night and day we continue praying with all our heart that we can come there and see you again. We want to give you everything you need to make your faith strong.
We pray that our God and Father and our Lord Jesus will prepare the way for us to come to you.
And pray that Lord will make your love to increase and overflow for one another, and for all people, just as we also do for you;
This will strengthen your desire to do what is right, and you will be holy and without fault before our God and Father when our Lord Jesus comes with all his holy people.
Sometime later the Moabite and Ammonite soldiers came to make war against King Jehoshaphat of Judah. Also other people also came with the Ammonites.

Some people came to Jehoshaphat and said: There are a lot of men who have come here to attack you. They have come from the other side of the Dead Sea, out of Syria; and behold, they are in Hazazontamar (that is, Engedi) 2 Chronicles 20: 3 Jehoshaphat was afraid, and he asked the Lord what he should do. Jehoshaphat told all the people in Judah to fast.
The people of Judah came together to ask the Lord what they must do. They came from all the towns of Judah to ask the Lord to help them.
Jehoshaphat went and stood in the new courtyard of the temple in front of the congregation of Judah and Jerusalem.
Jehoshaphat prayed and said: Lord, God of our grandfathers, You are the God in heaven. You rule over all the lands and all the nations. You are strong and powerful, and no one can stand against You.
You are our God, You chased away the people who lived in this land before and You gave this land to your people of Israel. You gave this land to the family of your friend Abraham.
We came to live here and we built a temple for You and we said:

When we suffer or trouble comes, the sword, punishment, sicknesses, or famine, we will stand in front of this Temple and in front of God. Your name is on this Temple. We will pray to you when we are in trouble. Then you will hear and save us.

Now the people from Ammon and Moab and the people from Mount Seir have come to attack us. When the people of Israel came out of Egypt, You did not let the people of Israel go into these lands to defeat them. You said the Israelites must turn away from them and the Israelites did not kill those people.
But now the people from Ammon and Moab and the people from Mount Seir have come to attack us. They want to chase us away from this land that You gave us.
You are our God, please punish them, We don’t have the strength to stop this large army that is coming against us. We do not know what to do, and that is why we are asking You to help us.
All the men of Judah were standing there before the Lord, with their babies, their wives and their children.
Then the Spirit of the Lord went into Jahaziel in front of the congregation. Jahaziel was the son of Zechariah, son of Benaiah, son of Jeiel, son of Mattaniah, a Levite and a descendant of Asaph.

Then Jahaziel said, Listen to me King Jehoshaphat and everyone living in Judah and Jerusalem! The Lord says this to you: Don’t be afraid or discourage about this large army, because the battle is not your battle. It is God’s battle!
Tomorrow morning you must go down to them. They will climb up the cliff of Ziz. When you get there, they will be at the end of the valley near the side of the Jeruel Desert.

You will not have to make war yourselves. You can just stand and look how the Lord will save you. People of Judah and Jerusalem, do not be discourage, and do not be afraid. Go to them tomorrow. The Lord will be with you.
Then Jehoshaphat bowed down with his face to the ground and all the people of Judah and Jerusalem bowed down before the Lord and worship the Lord.
Then the Levites from the Kohath and Korah families stood up and started to praise the Lord, the God of Israel. They sang very loudly.

Early the next morning, Jehoshaphat’s army went out into the desert of Tekoa. As they marched out, Jehoshaphat stood there saying, Listen to me, men of Judah and Jerusalem. Have faith in the Lord your God, and you will stand strong! Have faith in his prophets, and you will succeed!

Jehoshaphat encouraged the men and gave them instructions, and then Jehoshaphat appointed those who sang to the LORD and those who praised Him in their holy priestly attire, as they went out before the army and said, Praise and give thanks to the LORD, for His mercy and lovingkindness endure forever.
As they began to sing and to praise God, the Lord set an ambush for the army from Ammon, Moab, and Mount Seir who had come to attack Judah. The enemy was defeated!
Then the people of Ammon and Moab started to attack the people from mount Seir and they killed all of them. And then people from Ammon and Moab started to kill each other.
The men from Judah arrived at the lookout point in the desert. They looked for the enemy’s large army, but all they saw were dead bodies lying on the ground. There were no alived.

Jehoshaphat and his men came and took things from the dead bodies of the enemy. They found many animals, riches, clothes, and other valuable things. It was more than Jehoshaphat and his men could carry away. There was so much that they spent three days taking everything from the dead bodies.
After they gather for three days the next day they came together in the Berachah Valley and they praised the Lord. This is why people today still say that is the ‘Berachah Valley.

Then Jehoshaphat walked in front of the men of Judah and Jerusalem and they all went back to Jerusalem. All of them were very happy because the Lord had made them happy when He defeated their enemies.
Then Jehoshaphat and the men returned they went to the temple and they played their harps, viol and trumpets.
People in all the surrounding kingdoms became afraid of God when they heard that the Lord fought against the enemies of Israel
That is why there was peace for Jehoshaphat’s kingdom. God brought him rest from the enemies that were all around him.
Jehoshaphat was 35 years old when he became king of Judah. Jehoshaphat ruled in Jerusalem for 25 years. His mother’s name was Azubah, daughter of Shilhi.
Jehoshaphat listened to the Lord and he lived in the right way like his father Asa had done and he did not stop serving the Lord. He did what the Lord said was right.
Only the high places for pagan sacrifices were not removed, for the people did not listen to the God of their grandfathers.
The other things that Jehoshaphat did, from the time when he became king to the end, are written in the history of Jehu, son of Hanani, in the book of the kings of Israel.
King Jehoshaphat of Judah later made an agreement with Ahaziah, king of Israel. Ahaziah was a bad man who did evil things.
Jehoshaphat made an agreement with Ahaziah to build boats for him that could go to Tarshish. They built the boats in Ezion-Geber.

But a man of God, Eliezer, son of Dodavahu from the town of Mareshah, came to Jehoshaphat and gave him a message from God. Eliezer said, Jehoshaphat, since you have joined with Ahaziah, the Lord will destroy what you have built. The ships were wrecked, so Jehoshaphat and Ahaziah were not able to send them to Tarshish.
Josiah was 8 years old when he became king, and he was king in Jerusalem for 31 years.
Josiah lived in a way that pleased the Lord, as his grandfather David had done. Josiah always doing what was right, and never changed his way of life.

In the 8th year after Josiah had become king, Josiah was still a young man then, Josiah started serving the God of his grandfather David. In the 12th year after Josiah had become king, he started to destroy the places of sacrifice, the sacred pillars and the images of wood and metal. Josiah destroyed all of them.

Josiah told the people to break down the altars of Baal. They also cut down the incense altars that stood high above the people. They broke the idols that were carved and the idols that were made from molds. Josiah beat the idols into powder and sprinkled the powder on the graves of the people who had offered sacrifices to the Baal.
Josiah took the bones of the Baal priests, and Josiah burnt them on their altars. Josiah also removed the idles from Judah and Jerusalem
Josiah did the same in the towns of the tribes of Manasseh, Ephraim, Simeon to Naphtali and all area around them, with pickaxe.

Josiah told the people to break down all the altars and the sacred pillars, and to beat the images and the incense altars into small pieces. Josiah did this in all the places of the land of Israel and then Josiah came back to Jerusalem.

In the 18th year after Josiah had become king and after he had purified the land and the temple, he sent Shaphan, son of Azaliah, and Maaseiah, the chief of the city, with Joah, son of Joahaz the secretary, to repair the temple of the Lord his God.

These men came to Hilkiah the high priest, they gave the money that had been brought into the house of God, which the Levites, who guarded the doors, had collected from Manasseh and Ephraim, and from all the people of Israel, and from all Judah and Benjamin, and the people of Jerusalem.
Then the Levites paid the men who supervised the work on the Lord’s Temple. And the supervisors paid the workers who repaired and fixed up the Lord’s Temple.

The supervisors gave the money to carpenters and builders to buy large rocks that were already cut, and to buy wood to make beams for the buildings. They bought wood to make the floor because the kings of Judah had destroyed them.

The men worked faithfully. Their supervisors were Jahath and Obadiah, the sons of Merari, and Zechariah and Meshullam, the sons of Kohath, who made sure the work was done right. All the Levites who played the musical instruments
The supervisors were in charge of the men who had to carry the heavy loads. The supervisors also told the workers what to do. Some of the Levites were secretaries, managers, and guards at the doors.
When they went into the temple to get the money that the people had given, the high priest Hilkiah found the book of the laws that God had given to Moses.
Hilkiah said to Shaphan, the secretary: I have found the book of the laws in the temple. Then Hilkiah gave the book to Shaphan.
Shaphan took the book to King Josiah. Shaphan reported to the king, Your servants are doing everything you told them to do.
They took the money that they found in the temple of the God, and they gave it to the supervisors and workers.
Then Shaphan, the secretary, told the king about the book that the priest Hilkiah had given him, and Stephan started to read to king Josiah what was written in the book.
When Shaphan they told king Josiah what was written in the laws, King Josiah tore his clothes
King Josiah said to Hilkiah and Ahikam, son of Shaphan, to Abdon, son of Micah, and Shaphan, the secretary, and Asaiah the servant:

Go and ask the Lord to tell me and everyone who is still alive in Israel and Judah what will happen. Ask about the words in the book that was found. The Lord is very angry with us because our grandfathers did not obey the Lord’s word. They did not do everything this book says to do.

Hilkiah and the other men with him went to the prophetess Huldah, the wife of Shallum the son of Tikvath, the son of Hasrah. Huldah’s husband had to guard the clothes of the king and they lived in the lower part of Jerusalem. The me told her what the king had said.
The prophetess Huldah said to them: The Lord, the God of Israel, Tell King Josiah the man who has you to Me:
The Lord says: I will bring trouble to this place and to the people living here. I will bring all the terrible things that are written in the book that was read in front of the king of Judah.

I will do it because they have turned away from Me and they have served other gods. They have made Me angry because of all the bad things that they have done. I will punish this place and I will not stop.
Go back to King Josiah of Judah, the one who sent you to ask what the Lord wants. Tell him, This is what the Lord, the God of Israel, says about the words you heard being read:
Josiah, I, the Lord, saw that you humbled yourself when you heard what I had said about this place. I, the Lord, saw when you tore your clothes and I listened to you when you cried.

That is why I, the Lord, will be with you. I will not punish this place while you are alive, I will wait until you have died, and you are buried. You will not see how these people will suffer. Hilkiah and the other men went to the king and told him what the prophetess had said.
Then king Josiah told all the leaders of Judah and Jerusalem to come to him.

king Josiah went to the temple of the Lord and all the people of Judah and Jerusalem went with him, as well as the priests and Levites, all the adults and the children. Then King Josiah read to them so that they could all hear. He read what was written in the book of the covenant, that they had found in the temple of the Lord.

King Josiah stood in the place where the king always stood, and he made a covenant before the Lord. He promised to do what the Lord said and to obey everything in the laws of the Lord. He promised to do what the laws said with all his heart and soul, and he would do everything that was written in that book.

Then king Josiah asked every person in Jerusalem and in the tribe of Benjamin to promise that they would also do it. And the people of Jerusalem did what the covenant of the God of their grandfathers said.

Josiah took away all the things which the Lord hates. He took them away from the homes of the children of Israel. He said that everyone in the land of Israel must serve the Lord their God. And the people served the Lord, the God of their forefathers. They did not stop doing it as long as Josiah lived.

Moses’ father in law was named Jethro. Jethro was a priest of Midian. Moses took care of Jethro’s sheep. Moses led the sheep to the backside of the desert. He went to a mountain called Horeb, the mountain of God.
On that mountain, Moses saw the angel of the Lord in a burning bush. Moses saw that the bush was on fire, but it was not being destroyed.
Moses said to himself: I will go closer to the bush and see why the bush could continue burning without being burned up.
When the Lord saw Moses coming to the bush, He called him from inside the bush and said: Moses, Moses. And Moses said: Here I am.
The Lord said to Moses: Do not come closer. Take off your shoes because the place that you are standing on is holy ground.
I am the God of your fathers, the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob. Moses covered his face because he was afraid to look at God.

Then the Lord said to Moses: I have seen the troubles my people have suffered in Egypt. I have heard them crying, asking Me to save them from the Egyptians who force them to work. I know they suffer and I, God, feel sorry for them.

I, God, want to save them from the Egyptians. I, God, want to bring them out of this land and take them to a good and great land, a land where there is a lot of food, milk and honey. It is the land where the Canaanites are living now with the Hittites, the Amorites, the Perizzites, the Hivites and the Jebusites.
I, God, have heard the Israelites pray, and they have asked Me to help them. I, God, have seen the way the Egyptians have made life hard for them.
I, God, want you, Moses, to go to Pharaoh and to take my people, the Israelites, out of Egypt.
Moses said to God: I am not an important man. I can not go to Pharaoh and bring the children of Israel out of Egypt.

God answered Moses: I, God, will be with you. I, God, will give you a sign that will prove to you that I, God, have sent you. When you bring the people of Israel out of Egypt, you will worship God on this mountain.
Then Moses said to God: When I go to the children of Israel and I tell them: The God of your grandfathers has sent me to you, they will ask me, Moses: What is his name? What must I, Moses, tell them?
Then God said to Moses: My name is: I Am Who, I Am. You must tell the children of Israel: I Am has sent me, Moses, to you.

God also said to Moses: You must tell the children of Israel: The Lord God of your grandfathers has sent me, Moses, to you. He is the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob. This will always be my name. It is how I want the children of Israel to remember me from now on.

Go and call the leaders of Israel together and tell them that the Lord, the God of their grandfathers, the God of Abraham, Isaac and Jacob, appeared to you, Moses, and gave you, Moses, this is a message from me, God: I have been watching over you and have seen what people in Egypt have done to you.

God said He has decided that to take you away from the troubles the children of Israel are suffering in Egypt. God will take you, the children of Israel, into the land of the Canaanites and the Hittites and the Amorites and the Perizzites and the Hivites and the Jebusites. It is a land that has a lot of food, milk and honey.
