The leaders of Israel will listen to you, Moses. Then you, Moses and the leaders must go to the king of Egypt and tell him: The Lord, the God of the Hebrews, has talked to us. We beg you, King, let us go into the desert 3 days journey. We want to sacrifice offerings to the Lord our God.
I know that the king of Egypt will not let you go. He will not do it even if I punish him.
I, God, will use my great power against Egypt. I will cause amazing things to happen in Egypt. After I do this, The King of Egypt will let you go.
I will make the people of Egypt friendly towards children of Israel, and the people of Egypt will give the children of Israel many gifts when you go.

All the children of women Israel will ask their Egyptian neighbors and any woman living in her house for gifts of silver, gold, and fine clothing. Then you will put those gifts on your sons and on your daughters. The children of Israel shall plunder the Egyptians.
Then the Lord said to Moses,
Tell the Israelites to go back to and go back to the town of Pi-Hahiroth and put up their tents there, between Migdol and the sea. They must camp near the sea and near Baal-Zephon.
Then Pharaoh will think that the Israelites do not know where to go. He will say they are lost in the desert.

Pharaoh is stubborn, and he will go after the Israelites and chase them. But I will defeat Pharaoh and his army. This will bring honor to me. Then the Egyptians will know that I am the Lord. The Israelites did what Moses told them.
When they told Pharaoh, the king of Egypt, that the Israelites had fled, he and his officials said: We have made a mistake. We let the Israelites go and now they are not our slaves anymore.
So Pharaoh prepared his chariot and took his men with him.
Pharaoh took 600 of his best men and all of his chariots. There was an officer in each chariot.
The Lord made Pharaoh, king of Egypt, stubborn. He followed and chased the Israelites, but The Israelites were leaving with boldness.
The Egyptians with their war-chariots and the soldiers came near the Israelites, who were camping at the sea at PiHahiroth near BaalZephon.
When the Israelites saw Pharaoh and his army coming toward them, they were very afraid and cried to the Lord for help.
They said to Moses, Was it because there were no graves in Egypt that you brought us to the desert to die? What have you done to us by bringing us out of Egypt?

We told you this would happen! In Egypt we said, Please don’t bother us. Let us stay and serve the Egyptians. It would have been better for us to stay and be slaves than to come out here and die in the desert.
But Moses answered the children of Israel, Don’t be afraid! Don’t run away! Stand where you are and watch the Lord save you today. You will never see these Egyptians again.
You will not have to do anything but stay calm. The Lord will do the fighting for you.”
Then the Lord said to Moses, Why are you still crying to me? Tell the Israelites to start moving.
You must lift up your staff and hold it out over the sea. The water will separate and there will be dry ground for the Israelites to walk on.
I will make the Egyptians stubborn and they will go after you. I will show that I am God who can do what I want to do with Pharaoh and his army and his war-chariots and all his men.
Then Egypt will know that I am the Lord. They will honor me when I defeat Pharaoh and his horse soldiers and chariots.

Then the angel of God moved to the back of the people. (The angel was usually in front of the people, leading them.) So the tall cloud moved from in front of the people and went to the back of the people.

In this way the cloud stood between the Egyptians and the Israelites. There was light for the Israelites. But there was darkness for the Egyptians. So the Egyptians did not come any closer to the Israelites that night.
Moses raised his hand over the Red Sea, and the Lord caused a strong wind to blow from the east. The wind blew all night long. The sea split, and the wind made the ground dry.
The children of Israelites went through the sea on dry land. The water was like a wall on their right and on their left.
Then all of Pharaoh’s chariots and horse soldiers followed them into the sea.
Early that morning the Lord looked down from the tall cloud and column of fire at the Egyptian army. Then he made them panic.
The wheels of the chariots became stuck. It was very hard to control the chariots. The Egyptians shouted, Let’s get out of here! The Lord is fighting against us. He is fighting for the Israelites.
Then the Lord told Moses, Raise your hand over the sea to make the water fall and cover the Egyptian chariots and horse soldiers.

Then Moses put up his hand over the sea and the water of the sea flowed back to where it had been before. The Egyptians wanted to run away but the flood of water went over them and that is how God drowned the Egyptians in the sea
The water returned to its normal level and covered the chariots and horse soldiers. Pharaoh’s army had been chasing the Israelites, but that army was destroyed. None of them survived!
But the Israelites crossed the sea on dry land. The water was like a wall on their right and on their left.
So that day the Lord saved the Israelites from the Egyptians. Later, the Israelites saw the dead bodies of the Egyptians on the shore of the Red Sea.
The Israelites saw the great power of the Lord when he defeated the Egyptians. So, the people feared and respected the Lord, and they began to trust the Lord and his servant Moses.

1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: From the elder. To the chosen lady and her children, whom I love in the truth. And not only I, but also everyone who knows the truth loves you,
because of the truth that lives in us and will be with us forever.
Grace, mercy, and peace will be with us from God the Father and from Jesus Christ, the Father's Son, in truth and love.
It made me very glad to find some of your children living in the truth, just as the Father commanded us.
And now, dear lady, I ask you: let us love one another. This is not a new command; it is the one we have had from the beginning.
And love means this: that we live by his commands. This is the command you heard from the beginning: that you should live in love.
Many liars have gone out into the world. They do not say that Jesus Christ came as a real human. Anyone like this is a liar and an enemy of Christ.
Be careful, so that you do not lose what we have worked for, but receive your full reward.
Anyone who goes too far and does not stay in the teaching of Christ does not have God. But whoever stays in the teaching of Christ has both the Father and the Son.
If anyone comes to you and does not bring this teaching, do not welcome him into your house or even greet him.
Anyone who greets him shares in his evil work.
I have much to write to you, but I do not want to use paper and ink. Instead, I hope to come and talk with you face to face, so that our joy may be full.
The children of your chosen sister send you their greetings. Amen.
Solomon’s Most Wonderful Song.
She says: Cover me with kisses, for your love is better than wine.
Your perfume smells wonderful, but your name is sweeter than the best perfume. That is why the virgins love you.
Take me with you. Let’s run away. The king took me into his room. We will rejoice and be happy for you. Remember, your love is better than wine. With good reason, the virgins love you.
She says: Girls of Jerusalem, I am black, but I am beautiful. I am as beautiful as the tents in the land of Kedar, like the curtains of Solomon.

Don’t look at me because I am black, because the sun has burnt me. My brothers were angry with me. They forced me to take care of their vineyards, and that is why I could not take care of my vineyard.

She says: I love you with all my soul! Tell me, where do you feed your sheep? Where do you lay them down at noon? I should come to be with you, or I will be like a hired woman caring for the sheep of your friends.
He says: You are such a beautiful woman. If you don’t know the way, you must follow the path of the sheep. Feed your young goats near the shepherds’ tents.
He says: My love, you are beautiful to me, as beautiful as a horse in front of Pharaoh’s war-chariot.
Your cheeks are so beautiful with those ornaments hanging beside them. Your neck is so lovely under that neckless of gold.
I will make you some more gold jewelry and decorate it with silver.
She says: When the king was at his table, my perfume spread its fragrance.
My lover is like the small bag of myrrh around my neck, lying all night between my breasts.
My lover is like a bunch of henna flowers near the vineyards of Engedi.
He says love, you are so beautiful! Oh, you are beautiful! Your eyes are like doves.
She says: You are so handsome, my lover! also our bed is green.
The beams of our house are cedar. The rafters are from cypress trees.
I am a rose on the plain of Sharon, a lily in the valleys.
He says, My darling, among other women, you are like a lily among thorns!
She says, My lover, among other men, you are an apple tree among the wild trees in the forest! I enjoy sitting in my lover’s shadow; his fruit is so sweet to my taste.
She said, My lover took me to a place where we can celebrate, and he shown me that he loves me.
Strengthen me with raisins; refresh me with apples, because I am weak with love.
My lover’s left arm is under my head, and his right arm holds me.
Women of Jerusalem, promise me by the gazelles and wild deer, don’t awaken love, don’t arouse love, until I he is ready.
She say, I hear my lover’s voice. Here it comes, jumping over the mountains, skipping over the hills.
She say, My lover is like a gazelle or a young deer. Look at him standing behind our wall, staring out the window, looking through the lattice.
She say, My lover speaks to me, Get up, my darling, my beautiful one. Let’s go away!
Look, winter is past, the rains have come and gone.
The flowers are blooming in the fields. It is time for the birds to sing. I can hear the doves coo in our land.
Young figs are growing on the fig trees. Smell the vines of gapes in bloom. Get up, my darling, my beautiful one. Let’s go away!
He says, You are like a dove among the rocks, a dove that is hiding behind high rocks. I want to see you, and I want to listen to you. Your voice is beautiful and you are beautiful.
She says, Catch the foxes for us even the little foxes that spoil the vineyard. Our vineyard is now in bloom.
She says, My lover is mine, and I am his! My lover feeds among the lilies,
Until the day is over and the shadows are gone. my lover, you must come back. You must be like a gazelle, like a young deer on the Bether Mountains.
She says, At night on my bed, I look for the man I love. I looked for him, but I could not find him.
She said, I will get up now! I will go around the city. In the streets and squares, I will look for the man I love. I looked for him, but I could not find him.
The guards patrolling the city found me. I asked them, Have you seen the man I love?
I had just left the guards when I found the man I love! I held him and would not let him go, while I took him to my mother’s house, to the room of one who bore me.
She said, Women of Jerusalem, promise me by the gazelles and wild deer, don’t awaken love, don’t arouse love, until he is ready.
The Women of Jerusalem say, Who is that woman who come out of the desert like a cloud of smoke? I can smell the lovely myrrh and incense and the perfumes that come from the merchants.
Look, Solomon’s traveling chair. There are 60 soldiers guarding it, strong soldiers of Israel.
All of them are trained fighting men with their swords at their side, ready for any danger of the night.
King Solomon made a traveling chair for himself. The wood came from Lebanon.
The poles were made from silver, and the supports were made from gold. The seat was covered with purple cloth. It was inlaid with love by the women of Jerusalem.
Women of Zion, come out and see King Solomon. See the crown his mother put on him the day he was married, the day he was so happy!
He says: My love, you are beautiful. Listen, you are good to look at. Your eyes are like doves behind your veil. Your hair is like a flock of goats coming down from Mount Gilead.
Your teeth are white like a flock of sheep, sheep that are washed and sheared. Each sheep has twin babies. All of them have lambs.
Your lips are like a red silk thread. Your words are beautiful. Your temples under your veil are like two slices of pomegranate.
Your neck is like David’s tower. That tower was built to be decorated with a thousand shields on its walls, with the shields of powerful soldiers.
Your breasts are like twin fawns, feeding among the lilies.
I will go to the mountain of myrrh, to the hill of incense, and I will stay there until morning comes and the darkness disappears.
My love, you are beautiful all over. Every part of you is perfect.
Come with me, my bride, from Lebanon. Come with me from Lebanon. Come from the peak of Amana, from the top of Shenir and Hermon from the lion’s caves, from the mountain of the leopards.
My love, my bride, you made my heartbeat faster when you looked at me. It took only one piece of the chain around your neck to make me breathe faster.
Your love is so beautiful, my love, my bride! Your love is better than wine. The smell of your perfume is better than any kind of spice!
My bride, your lips are like a honeycomb, and there is honey and milk under your tongue. Your clothes smell like they come from the Lebanon Mountain.’
He says: You are like a garden with a wall around it. My bride is like a garden with a wall around it, and like a fountain that is closed.
Your arms and legs are like a garden filled with pomegranates and other pleasant fruit, with all the best spices:
Spikenard, saffron, calamus, and cinnamon. with all the incense trees, myrrh and aloes with all the best herbs.
You are like a garden fountain, a well of fresh water, flowing down from the mountains of Lebanon.
She says, Wake up, north wind. Come, south wind. Blow on my garden. Spread its sweet smell. Let my lover enter his garden and eat its pleasant fruit.

He said, My darling my bride, I have entered my garden. I have gathered my myrrh and spice. I have eaten my honey and honeycomb. I drank my wine and milk. Bride says, Dearest friends, eat, drink! Be drunk with love!

Bride says, I am asleep, but my heart is awake. I hear my lover knocking, saying, Open to me, my darling, my love, my dove, my perfect one! My head is soaked with dew. My hair is wet with the mist of the night.
I have taken off my robe. I don’t want to put it on again. I have washed my feet. I don’t want to get them dirty again.
But my lover put his hand through the opening, and I felt sorry for him.
I got up to open for my lover, myrrh dripping from my hands, myrrh scented lotion dripped from my fingers onto the handles of the lock.
I opened for my lover, but my lover had turned away and was gone! I nearly died when he came and went. I looked for him, but I couldn’t find him. I called for him, but he didn’t answer me.
The guards patrolling the city found me. They hit me. They hurt me. The guards on the wall took off my veil.
She said, I tell you women of Jerusalem, if you find my lover, tell him I am weak with love.
The Women of Jerusalem Answer, Beautiful woman, how is your lover different from other lovers? Is your lover better than other lovers? Is that why you ask us to make this promise?
My lover is white and blood red. He would stand out among 10,000 men.
His head is like the purest gold. His hair is curly and as black as a raven.
His eyes are like doves by a stream, like doves in a pool of milk, Like a jewel in its setting.
His cheeks are like a garden of spices, like flowers used for perfume. His lips are like lilies, dripping with liquid myrrh.
His arms are like gold rods, filled with jewels. His body is like smooth ivory with sapphires set in it.
His legs are like marble pillars on bases of fine gold. He stands tall like the finest cedar tree in Lebanon!
His mouth tastes sweet, and he is beautiful in every way. Women of Jerusalem, this is the man that I love. This is my friend.
The Women of Jerusalem said, Beautiful woman, where has your lover gone? Which way did your lover go? Tell us so that we can help you look for him.
She said, My lover has gone down to his garden, where sweet-smelling spices grow. There, he will eat, and he will enjoy the lilies.
I belong to my lover, and my lover belongs to me. He is the one feeding among the lilies.
He says, My darling, you are as beautiful as Tirzah, as pleasant as Jerusalem, You are inspiring as an army with flags.
Don’t look at me! Your eyes make me tremble! Your hair is like a flock of goats running down Mount Gilead.
Your teeth are white like sheep just coming from their bath. They all give birth to twins. Not one of them has lost a baby.
Your temples under your veil are like slices of pomegranate.
There might be 60 queens and 80 slave women, and virgins too many to count,

but there is only one woman for me, my dove, my perfect one. She is the favorite of her mother, her mother’s favorite child. The young women see her and praise her. Even the queens and slave women praise her.
The woman say, Who is that coming to us like a new day? She is as beautiful as the moon, as bright as the sun. She is so beautiful that we start to tremble.
She said, I went down to the grove of walnut trees, to see the fruit of the valley, to see if the vines were in bloom, to see if the pomegranates had budded.
I do not know what happened to me. It was as if she made me ride on the best war-chariots of my people.’

The women say: Shulammite woman, you must come back, you must come back, you must come back. We want to look at you. She says: Why do you want to look at me? I am the Shulammite woman. Why do you want to look at me when I dance in the camp of the soldiers?’
Princess, your feet are beautiful in those sandals. The curves of your thighs are like jewelry made by an artist.
Your navel is like a round cup: may it never be without wine. Your belly is like a pile of wheat surrounded by lilies.
Your breasts are like twin fawns.
Your neck is like an ivory tower. Your eyes are like the fishpoles in Heshbon near the gate of Bathrabbim. Your nose is like the tower of Lebanon that looks toward Damascus.
Your head is like Carmel, and the hair on your head is like purple silk. Your long flowing hair captures even a king.
You are so beautiful and so pleasant, a lovely, delightful woman!
You are tall as tall as a palm tree. And your breasts are like clusters of grapes.
I would love to climb that tree and take hold of its branches. May your breasts be like clusters of grapes and your fragrance like apples.
May your mouth be like the best wine, flowing straight to my love, flowing gently to the sleeper’s lips.
She says, I belong to my lover, and he wants me.
Come, my lover, let’s go out into the field; let’s spend the night in the villages.
Let’s get up early and go to the vineyards. Let’s see if the vines are in bloom. Let’s see if the blossoms have opened and if the pomegranates are in bloom. There I will give you my love.
Smell the mandrakes and all the pleasant flowers by our door. I have saved many pleasant things for you, my lover, pleasant things, new and old.
The Lord is my shepherd. I will always have everything I need.
He makes me lie down and rest in green fields where the water is calm.
The Lord gives me new strength. He teaches me to live in the right way because I belong to Him.
When I get ready to die, I will not be afraid, because You, O Lord, are with me. You protect me like a shepherd protects his sheep.
You invite me to sit at your table in front of my enemies. You honor me by anointing my head with oil. My cup is full of blessings and spilling over.
Your goodness and mercy will be with me all my life, and I will live in the Lord’s house for ever.
I said, I will be careful about what I say. I will not let my words cause me to sin. I will keep my mouth closed when I am around wicked people.
So I did not speak. I kept quiet, I did not even speak good, and it made me suffer even more.
I was very angry, and the more I thought about it, the angrier I became. So, I said something.
Lord, please tell me when I will die. For how long will I still live? Tell me how soon my life will be over.
You have made my life very short, and all the days of my life are like nothing to You. Surely every person at their best is a puff of wind. Pause and think
Our life is like an image in a mirror. We rush through life collecting things, but we don’t know who will get them after we die.
O Lord, what am I relying on? You are my only hope!
Save me from all my sins, and don’t let fools make fun of me.
I will not speak, and I will not say a word because my punishment is from you. Psalms 39: 10 Please stop punishing me. You will destroy me if you do not stop.
You punish people for doing wrong to teach them the right way to live. As a moth destroys cloth, you destroy what people love. Yes, our lives are nothing. Pause and Think

Lord, please listen to my prayer and my crying. Look at my tears, and don’t look away from me because I am like a stranger to You, or like a visitor, like my forefathers who came to You for a short while.
Leave me alone so I can smile again before I am gone and exist no more.
I waited for the Lord to help me, and then He bowed down and heard me crying. He listened to me.
He lifted me up out of the deep hole that was full of mud. He put my feet on a rock on solid ground and kept my feet from slipping.
He gave me a new song to sing, a song of praise to our God. Many will see what he did and worship him. They will put their trust in the Lord.
All will go well with someone who trusts in the Lord, who not trust in the proud or in those who worship idols.
The Lord is my God, you have done many amazing things! You have made great plans for us that they are too many to list. I could talk on and on about them, because there are too many to count.
Lord, you made me understand this: You don’t really want sacrifices and offerings. You don’t want burnt offerings and sin offerings.
Then said The Lord: here I am. I am here to do what is written about me in the book of laws.
My God, I want to do what You want. I always think about what is written in your laws.
I told the good news of victory to the people in the great assembly. And, Lord, you know that I will never stop telling that good news.

I have talked about the good things you did. I did not hide these things in my heart. I spoke of how you can be trusted to save us. I did not hide your love and loyalty from those in the great assembly.
Lord, do not hide your mercy from me. Let your love and truth always protect me.
Troubles have surrounded me. They are too many to count! My sins have caught me, and I cannot escape them. They are more than the hairs on my head. I have lost my courage.
LORD, be pleased to save me; hurry to help me, LORD.
I pray that you will punish the people who want to take away my life and make them ashamed. Make them turn around and go away,
May those who say to me, “Aha! Aha!” be amazed at their own shame.
May all who seek You rejoice and be glad in You; may those who love Your salvation always say, Great is the LORD!
I am poor, and I cannot help myself, but the Lord will never forget me. Lord, you are my Helper and my Saviour. Please do not wait too long
Beautiful words stir my heart. I will recite a lovely poem about the king, for my tongue is like the pen of a skillful poet.
You are better-looking than all the other men and you say beautiful things to other people. That is why God was good to you and blessed you forever.
Put on your sword. You are a strong leader, the great king.
Go out in your greatness to win the victory for what is true and right. Let us see the amazing things you can do with your powerful right arm.
Your arrows are sharp, and they will pierce the hearts of the enemies of the king. Nations will fall before you.
God has made you king forever, and you will rule your people fairly and righteously.
You love what is right and you hate anything that is wrong and sinful. That is why God, your God, has anointed you and given you more joy than He has given any other.
Your clothes smell like myrrh, aloe and cassia, thou art made glad by the sweet smell of thy garments out of those ivory palaces
Kings’ daughters are among your noble women. At your right side stands the queen, wearing jewelry of finest gold from Ophir!
Hear, O daughter, and take to heart what I say: forget your people and your father’s family,
Your beauty will make the king desire you; he is your Lord, so you must worship him.
A princess is beautiful when she comes in from her room. Her clothes are made of golden threads.
Clothed in beauty, she is led to the king, followed by her bridesmaids.
Your sons will become kings like their father. You will make them rulers over many lands.
You will be famous for generations. People will praise you forever and ever.
God protects us and makes us strong. He is ready to help us in times of trouble.
We will not be afraid, even if earthquakes come and the mountains fall into the sea.
Let the waters be load and foam, and its raging waves shake the mountains.
There is a river which makes the people happy in the city of God, the holy place of the Highest God.
God is in that city, so it will never be destroyed. He is there to help even before sunrise.
People will make noises, and kingdoms and lands are destroyed. When God speaks, the earth melts away.
The Lord who rules over all people is with us. The God of Jacob protects us. Stop and Think
Come and see what the Lord has done. who has done fearsome deeds on earth.
He stops wars all over the world. He breaks all the bows and arrows and spears. He burns all the war-chariots with fire.
Stop doing what you are doing and know that that I am God, I am high above all people, and high above the earth.’
The Lord who rules over all people is with us. The God of Jacob protects us. Stop and Think
Everyone, clap your hands. Shout with joy to God!
Because the Lord is the Highest God, and everyone must fear Him. He is the Great King who rules all the earth.
He helped us defeat other nations. He put those people under our control.
He chose the land that He wanted to give us, the excellent land of Jacob whom He loved. Stop and Think
God has ascended with shouting, The LORD with the sound of a trumpet.
Sing praises to God, sing praises! Sing praises to our King, sing praises!
God is the King of all the earth. sing ye praises.
God rules over all the nations. God sits on his holy throne.
The leaders of the nations have come together with the people of the God of Abraham. All the rulers of the world belong to God. He is over them all!
The Lord is great! He is praised throughout the city of our God, his holy mountain.
His city is such a pleasant place. It brings joy to people from around the world. Mount Zion is the city of the great King.
God has shown that He protects his people within the strong walls of the city.
The Kings came together because they wanted to attack the city,
But when they saw God protecting the city, they became afraid and ran away.
Fear and pain grabbed them; like a woman giving birth.
God, with a strong east wind, you wrecked the ships of Tarshish.
Yes, we heard the stories about your power. But we also saw it in the city of our God, the city of God. God makes that city strong forever. Pulse and Think
God, in your Temple we remember your loving kindness.
Your name is known everywhere, God, and people throughout the earth praise you. You have shown that you do what is right.
The people in Zion rejoice. and the towns of Judah rejoice, because your decisions are fair.
Walk around Jerusalem, walk around the city, and count the towers in the walls.
Look how strong the walls around the city are. Look at the walls inside the city and tell this to your children.
“This God is our God for ever and ever; He will lead us from now until we die!
Listen to this, all you nations. Pay attention, all you people on earth.
Everyone, the humble and those of exalted rank, rich and poor people.
I have some very wise words for you. My thoughts will give you understanding.
I will incline my ear to a proverb; I will express my riddle on the harp.
I will not become afraid when things go wrong or when sinful people stand around me.
They think their wealth will protect them. They brag about how rich they are.
No one can save the life of another person, and no one can give to God the price of his life.
For it would cost too much to redeem his life, and the payments would go on forever.
So that he may live forever and not see the grave.
Anyone can see that wise people die. Foolish and ignorant people also die, and they leave their things to another.
But their inward thought is that their houses will continue forever, and their living places passed down to all generations. They name their lands after themselves.
A man’s fame will not last. He will die, just like the animals.
This is the fate of those who trust in themselves, and of their followers, who approve their sayings. Pulse and Think
Like sheep, they are led to the grave, where death will be their shepherd. In the morning the godly will rule over them. their bodies quickly decay in the grave far from their homes.
But God will pay the price to save me from the grave. He will take me to be with him. Pulse and Think
Don’t be afraid of people just because they are rich. Don’t be afraid of people just because they have big, fancy houses.
Because when he dies, he will not be able to take anything with him. His honor and praise will not follow him.
Yes, people said they were blessed when they were alive and other people might praise him.
But they will die like all before them and never again see the light of day.
The mighty God, the Lord God, who speaks. God calls all the people on earth. God calls them from where the sun comes up to where it goes down.
From Zion, perfect in beauty, God shines forth.
Our God is coming and will not keep quiet. Fire burns in front of him. There is a great storm around him.
God calls the heaven above and the earth to be witnesses when God judges his people
God says: Come here, all of you who believe in Me, all those who sacrificed offerings when they made a covenant with Me.
God is the Judge and everyone in heaven says that He is right and fair. Paulse and think
God says, My people, listen to me! People of Israel, God will show my evidence against you. I am God, your God.
The problem God has with you is not your sacrifices or the burnt offerings you bring to me everyday.
Why would God want more bulls from your barns or goats from your pens?
I, God, already own all the animals in the forest. I own all the animals on a thousand hills.
God know every bird in the mountains. Everything that moves in the fields is mine.
If God was hungry, God would not ask you for food. God already own the world and everything in it.
Does God eat the meat of bulls? Do God drink the blood of goats?
Give God thank for everything, and if you have promised to give something to the Highest God, then you must do it.
God says, pray to me when trouble comes. God will help you, and you will glorify my name.
But to the sinful people God said: What right have you to talk about My law or to take My promise on your lips?
You hate for someone to tell you what to do. and you ignore what I say
You are friends with every thief that you see and when you see someone who commits adultery, you go with him and do the same.
You always want to say something bad, and you continue to tell lies.
You sit around talking about people, you also find fault with your own brothers.
I have kept quiet while you have done all these things, so you thought I was just like you. But I will not be quiet any longer. I will correct you and tell you what you are doing wrong.
You who have forgotten God. Understand what I am telling you, or I will attack you like a lion, and no one will be able to save you.
He who offers a sacrifice of praise and thanksgiving honors God; And to him who follows the way that I show him, I shall show the salvation of God.
O God, please have mercy on me. You are faithful and always love us. You are always merciful. Because of your great love, erase all the wrongs I have done.
Wash away all my sins and clean me from my sin.
I know I have done wrong: and I remember my sin all the time.
I have sinned against You, only against You, and I did what You hate. You were right and fair when You said that I am guilty. You did not make a mistake when You judged me.
When I came into this world, I was full of sin. I was a sinner before I was born.
You want me to faithful to you deep in my heart and mind. So put true wisdom deep inside of me.
Remove my sin and make me pure. Wash me until I am whiter than snow.
Don’t look at my sins. Erase all my sins.
O God, please give me a clean mind, and make my spirit faithful to You.
Don’t send me away from You or take your Holy Spirit away from me.
Make me as happy as You did when You saved me. Let your spirit strengthen me
I will teach the guilty how you want them to live, and the sinners will come back to you.
O God, save me from the guilt of murdering someone. You are my Saviour. I will sing and tell everyone that You are good and righteous.
My Lord, I will open my mouth and sing your praises!
Sacrificing offerings or burnt offerings is not what you desire. If that was want you wanted, I would give it.
The sacrifice that God wants is a humble spirit. God, you will not turn away someone who comes with a humble and broken heart and is willing to obey you.
Please be good to Zion again. Build the walls of Jerusalem and make the city strong again.
Then you can enjoy the kind of sacrifices you want. You will receive whole burnt offerings, and people will again offer bulls on your altar.
Strong man, why are you bragging about the evil you did? Do you not know that God’s goodness continues forever?
Your tongue, like a sharp razor, You say things that hurt and wound people. You also try to deceive everyone.
You love doing bad things more than good things. You love telling lies more than telling the truth. Paulse and think
You with your lying tongue love to hurt and destroy people.
So God will defeat you forever! He will grab you and pull you from your home. God will make you to die like someone pulling up a plant by the roots. Paulse and think
The people who live in the right way will see it and they will fear God: They will laugh at you:
Look what happened to the man who did not believe in God, He trusted his money and became rich by destroying other people.
But I am like a green olive tree growing in God’s Temple. I will trust God’s faithful love forever and ever.
Lord, I will thank You forever for all the wonderful things that You have done to me. I will trust in your name and tell people how good You are. I will say it where all the believers are together.
Only foolish people think there is no God. They sin and do evil things; none of them does what is good
God looks down from heaven to see if there is anyone who is wise, anyone who looks to God and asks what God wants them to do.
But they have all turned away from God, Everyone has become evil. No one does anything good. No, not one person!
Those who are evil, do they not understand what they are doing? They are treating my people like bread to be eaten. They never ask for God’s help.
They will be filled with lots of fear like you have never feared before, because God will destroy the enemies of his people. They will suffer and become ashamed because God has left them alone.

I pray that someone from Mount Zion would bring deliverance to Israel! I pray that God will help his people return from being slaves, the people of Jacob will be very happy; the people of Israel will be glad.
God, by your name deliver me, and by your power judge me.
God, listen to my prayer. Listen to what I say.
Strangers who don’t even think about God have turned against me. Those cruel men are trying to kill me. Paulse and Think
Look, my God will help me. My Lord will support me, protect me, and save my life.
May the evil plans of my enemies be turned against them. Do as you promised and put an end to them.
I will happily give you a sacrifice. I will praise your name Lord, because You are good.
Because You have saved me from all my trouble and my eyes have seen God’s desires on my enemies.
O God, please listen to my prayer and don’t look away when I am begging You for help.
Please listen to me and answer me. Let me speak to you and tell you what upsets me.
My enemies shout at me and threaten me. In their anger they attack me. They bring troubles crashing down on me.
My heart beats very fast, and I am afraid that I will die.
I am trembling with fear. I am terrified!
I wish that I had wings like a dove, and then I would fly away to a safe place to rest.
Yes, I would fly away to the desert and stay there. Paulse and Think
I would hurry to go and find a place to hide from the wind and the storm.
My Lord, confuse their words and stop their plans. I see so much cruelty and fighting in this city.
Day and night they walk on the walls around the city. They sin and bring trouble to the people and make them suffer.
There is so much bad in the streets. People who hurt and cheat others are everywhere.

But it was not my enemies who falsely accused me. If it were my enemies, I would understand it. It was not the people who always hated me and who think they are more important than I am. If it were they, I would have hid from them.
But it was someone like me who accused me, the one so close to me, my companion, my good friend, who does this.
We used to share our secrets with one another, as we walked through the crowds together in God’s Temple.
I wish death would take my enemies by surprise! I wish the earth would open up and swallow them alive, because they plan such terrible things together.
But I will call God and ask Him to help me. I know that the Lord will save me.
I cry in the morning, at noon and at night. I pray to God, and He listens to me.
I have fought in many battles, but God has always rescued me and brought me back safely.
God will listen to my prayers and punish my enemies. Paulse and Think They will never change. They don’t fear and respect God.
The person who was my friend has turned against me and betrayed me. He did not keep his promise to me. He broke it.

He said beautiful things, and his words were as smooth as butter, but there was war in his heart. He wanted to do something bad to me. His words were softer than oil but they hurt me like sharp swords.
Give your worries to the Lord and he will care for you. He will never let those who are good be defeated.
But, God, you will send those liars and murderers to the grave. They will die before their life is half finished! As for me, I will put my trust in you.
O God, please have mercy on me, and be gracious because I ask You to protect me. I come and hide with You like chicks that hide under a hen’s wings until the danger is gone.
I call and ask You to help me. You are the Most High God, the God who does everything for me.
From heaven he helps me and saves me. He will punish the one who attacks me. Paulse and think. God will send forth his unfailing love and truth.
My enemies are like lions all around me lions that are hungry and want to kill. Their teeth are like spears and arrows. Their tongues and the things they say are like sharp swords.
O God, please show everyone that You are the Great King in heaven and on earth. Let them see your glory.
My enemies set a trap for my feet to bring me down. They dug a deep pit to catch me, but my enemies have fallen into it. Paulse and think
My heart is yours, O God, my heart is yours: I will sing and give praise you.
Wake up, my soul! Harps and psaltery, wake up, and let’s wake early.
My Lord, I will praise you before all people. I will sing praises about you to every nation.
Because your faithful love is higher than heaven, Your truths goes so far that it touches the clouds.
Rise above the heavens, God. Let all the world see your glory.
Do you speak righteousness, you silent ones? Do you judge uprightly, you sons of men?
No, you only think of evil things to do. You do violent crimes in this country.
Those wicked people started doing wrong as soon as they were born. They have been liars from birth.
Their anger is as deadly as the poison of a snake. They shut their ears like a deaf adder
The deaf adder does not listen to the music of the snake charmers, no matter how well they play.
O God, break the teeth in the mouths of those sinners. Lord, pull out the sharp teeth of those lions.
Let them disappear like water that flows away into the ground. When they want to shoot their arrows, let them miss and fail.

Let them be like snail that melt and disappear on the road when they move. Let them be like a child who dies before he is born, who never sees the sun. Psalm 58: 9 The Lord will make them disappear faster than thorn bushes that burn under a pot of water, where even the green branches burn out quickly.
People who live in the right way will be very happy when they see how the Lord punishes those who do wrong. They will wash their feet in the blood of the sinners.
Then people will say: Surely, the Lord rewards people who are righteous. There really is a God who judges all the people on earth.
My God, please save me From my enemies, And protect me from the people who want to attack me.
Save me from those evil people, and protect me from those who are after my blood.

God, you have forgotten us, and you have driven us out because you were angry with us. Now restore us to your favor.

You have set up a banner for those who fear God. A banner that may be displayed because of the truth. Paulse and think.
Save us and help us with your right hand, that those you love may be delivered.
God has spoken from his sanctuary: In triumph I will divide the land of Shechem and I will give them Succoth Valley.
Gilead and Manasseh will be mine. Ephraim will be my helmet Judah will be my lawgiver.
The land of Moab is like a bowl where I wash my feet, and the land of Edom is the place where I throw down my shoes. Philistia, shout in triumph because of Me.
Who will bring me to the city with strong walls? Who will lead me to Edom?
O God, You have pushed us away. O God You don’t go with our soldiers when we go to war.
Help us defeat the enemy! No one on earth can rescue us.
But if God is with us, then we will be strong. He will defeat and trample our enemies.
O 1 God, hear my cry for help. Listen to my prayer.

I ask this because You have always protected me. You were like a tower where my enemies could not come.
Let me live in your tent forever and protect me like a hen protects her chicks under her wings. Paulse and Think

Then I will praise your name forever. Every day I will do what I promised.
I wait patiently for God to help me. God is the only One who can save me.
Only God is my Rock and my Saviour. God protects me, my faith shall not be shaken.

Your love that will never change is better than life itself. That is why with my lips I will praise you.

My soul will be satisfied as with fat and rich food, and my mouth will praise you with joyful lips, Ps
When I lie down on my bed and remember You, when I think of You all night long,
I remember how You God has always helped and protected me like a hen protects her chicks, and I sing again.

They say things that hurt me like sharp swords, and their words wound me like arrows.
They hide in places where no one can see them, and they shoot honest people. They are not afraid.

God in Zion, we praise you and give you what we promised.
Anyone can come to you, God, and God will listen to their prayers.
Our sins are too many for us to carry, but You forgive our sins and take them away.

God, By awesome and wondrous things You answer us and do what is right. You do amazing things to save us. People all over the world know they can trust in you, even those who live across the sea.
You are stronger than anything and anyone. You are the One who put the mountains in their place.
When You speak, the sea and the waves become still, and all the nations become quiet.

Be happy as you serve the LORD! Come before him with singing!
Know that the Lord is God, The Lord has made us and not ourselves. We are his people. We are the sheep that belong to Him.

Come through the gates to his Temple giving thanks to him. Enter his courtyards with songs of praise Honor him and bless his name. Psalms 105:5 The Lord is good! There is no end to his mercy. The Lord faithful to all people, and to all generations.
I will sing of mercy and judgment: unto thee, O Lord, will I sing.
I will be careful to live a pure life.When will You come to me? I will live in my house with complete honesty.
I will not look at anything wicked. I hate all wrongdoing. I want no part of it!
I don’t want to be false or dishonest, I will have nothing to do with evil.
I will stop anyone who secretly says bad things about a neighbor. I will not allow people to be proud and think they are better than others.
I will look throughout the land for those who can be trusted. Only such people can live with me. Only those who walks blamelessly is the one who will minister to and serve me.
In my house there may be no one who deceives other people. No one who tells lies may work for Me.
My goal each morning will be to put an end to the wicked living in our land. I will force all who do evil to leave the city of the Lord.
Lord, hear my prayer. Listen to my cry for help.
Don’t turn away from me when I have troubles. Listen to me and answer me quickly when I cry for help.
My life is passing away like smoke. and the bones in my body burn like fire.
My heart is sad, I am like dry, dying grass. I even forget to eat.
Because of my sadness, I am losing so much weight that my skin hangs from my bones.
I am like a desert pelican, like an owl living among old, ruined buildings.
I cannot sleep. I am like a lonely sparrow on the roof.
My enemies mock me all day. and they use my name for a curse word.
I eat ashes for my food, and when I drink something, it is full of my tears.
This is because You are angry with me. You picked me up and threw me away.
My life is like shadows that disappear in the afternoon, I am like dry and dying grass.
But, You, O Lord, will be King forever, and every generation knows your name.
You will rise up and comfort Zion. The time has come for you to be kind to Zion.
Jerusalem is just a heap of stones, but we, your servants, love every stone of the city, and we feel sorry for her dust.
The nations will worship the Lord’s name. All the kings on earth will honor you.
Yes, the Lord will build Zion again, and people will see his glory in the city.
The lord listens to the prayers of people who have nothing. Their prayers are important to Him.
Write these things for future generations, so that they will praise the Lord.
The Lord will look down from his Holy Place above. The Lord will look down at the earth from heaven.
The Lord will hear the prisoner’s prayers. The Lord will free those who were condemned to die.
The people in Zion will tell about the Lord. They will praise his name in Jerusalem
when the people of all the nations come together and people from other lands come to serve the Lord.
The Lord made me weak. The made my life short.
I said: My God, please don’t take away my life now. I am only in the middle of it. But You, O God, live forever, and your life goes on and on.
Long ago You made the earth, and your hands made the heaven.
The earth and sky will end, but you will live forever! The earth and sky will wear out like clothes, and like clothes, you will change them.
But You will always be the same. You live forever
We are your servants today. 0ur children will live here, and their descendants will come here to worship you.
My soul, praise the Lord! Every part of me, praise his holy name!
My soul, praise the Lord and never forget how kind he is!
The Lord forgives all our sins and heals all our sicknesses.
The Lord saves us from the grave, and he gives us love and compassion.
The Lord is the One who is very good to me, and who makes me strong and young again, like an eagle.
The Lord does what is right and fair to people who have been hurt by others.
The Lord taught his laws to Moses. The Lord let Israel see the powerful things he can do.
The Lord is full of mercy and grace. The Lord is patient and does not become angry quickly. His love for us will never change.
The Lord will not always strive with us, Nor will He keep His anger forever.
We sinned against The Lord, but The Lord didn’t give us the punishment we deserved.
The Lords love for his followers is as high above us as heaven is above the earth.
As far as the east is from the west, that is how far the Lord takes away our sins from us.
As a father love his children so the Lord love his followers
The Lord knows all about us. The knows we are made from dust.
A person does not live for a long time, and his life is like grass. It grows like a wild flower,
When the hot wind blows the wild flowers, it dies. Soon, you cannot even see where the flower was.
But the mercy of the Lord never stops. It continues forever for the people who worship and serve Him, and He is good to their children and their grandchildren.
To those who are faithful to his agreement and who remember to obey his commands.
The throne of the Lord is in heaven. The Lord is King of everything and everyone.
Angels of the Lord, praise the Lord! You angels are the powerful soldiers who obey his commands. You listen to him and obey his commands.
All the armies in heaven, you are The Lord servants who do what the Lord wants, and you must praise Him.
Everything the Lord has made should praise The Lord throughout the world that the Lord rules! My soul, praise the Lord!
Praise the LORD, my soul! O LORD, my God, how great you are! You are clothed with majesty and glory;
Lord, You wear light like a robe. Lord, You spread out the skies like a curtain
Lord, you built your home on the waters above. Lord, You use the clouds as your chariot and ride on the wings of the wind.
Lord, you make the winds your messengers, flames of fire your servants.
Lord, You built the earth on its foundations, so it can never be moved.
Lord, You placed the ocean over it like clothes, and the water covered the mountains.
When you rebuked the waters, they fled; the water rushed away when they heard your shout of command.
The waters flowed over the mountains and into the valleys, to the place you had made for them.
Lord, You set a boundary they can never pass, to keep the waters from covering the earth again.
Lord, You make springs flow in the valleys, and rivers run between the hills.
The springs provide water for the wild animals; here the wild donkeys quench their thirst.
Wild birds come to live by the pools; the birds sing in the branches of nearby trees.
From the sky Lord, you send rain on the hills, and The earth gets everything it needs from what you have made.
Lord, You make grass grow for the cattle and Lord, You provide plants for the crops we grow. The plants that give us food from the earth.
Lord, you give us the wine that makes us happy, the oil that makes our faces to shine, and the food that makes us strong.
The cedars of Lebanon which the LORD planted are full of sap
There the birds build their nests; the stork’s home is in the fir trees.
The high mountains are a home for wild goats. The large rocks are hiding places for rock badgers.
Lord, You created the moon to mark the seasons: the sun knows the time to go down
Lord, You made the night, and in the darkness all the wild animals come out.
The young lions roar while they hunt, looking for the food, that God provides.
When the sun rises, they go back and lie down in their dens.
Then people go out to do their work and keep working until evening
LORD, you have made so many things! How wisely you made them all! The earth is filled with your creatures.
There is the ocean, large and wide where countless creatures live, large and small alike.
Ships sail over the ocean, and playing there is Leviathan, the great sea creature you made.
They all wait for You To give them their food in its appointed season.
You give it to them, they gather it up; You open Your hand, they are filled and satisfied with good things.
When you turn away, they are afraid; when you take away your breath, they die and go back to the dust from which they came.
But when you give them breath, they are created; you give new life to the earth.
The glory of the LORD will last forever! The LORD will be glad about what he has made!
The Lord looks at the earth, and the earth trembles; The Lord touches the mountains, and smoke rises from them.
I will sing to the Lord for the rest of my life. I will sing praises to my God as long as I live.
May my meditation be pleasing to the: Lord; I will rejoice in the Lord.
May sinners be destroyed from the earth; may the wicked be no more. Praise the LORD, my soul! Praise the LORD!
By the rivers of Babylon, there we sat down and cried when we remembered Zion.

We hung our harps nearby, there on the willow trees. Psalms 137: There in Babylon, those who captured us told us to sing. Our enemies told us to entertain them. They said, Sing us one of your songs about Zion.
But we could not sing the song of the Lord in a strange land.
Jerusalem, if I forget you, then my right hand must forget how to play the harp.
If I don’t think about Jerusalen, then my tongue must stick to the top of my mouth. Jerusalem must always be the city that I love the most.
Lord, remember what the Edomite people did when they destroyed Jerusalem. The Edomite people said: Break down the walls. Destroy them down to the foundations.
Babylon, you will be destroyed! Bless the one who pays you back for what you did to us.
Your enemies will kill your babies against the rocks.
Theophilus, in my first book I wrote about all the things that Jesus did and what He told people to do while He was here on earth,
Before Jesus went to heaven. Jesus chose 12 men to be his apostles. Jesus and the Holy Spirit told them what to do when He had gone away.

After Jesus had suffered and died, He started to live again, and He came to the apostles and showed them that He was alive. He appeared to them a lot of times in 40 days, and He told them about God who is King over everyone
And when they came together, Jesus gave them this order: Do not leave Jerusalem, but wait for the gift I told you about, the gift my Father promised.
John baptized with water, but in a few days, you will be baptized with the Holy Spirit.
The apostles were all together. They asked Jesus, Lord, is this the time for you to give the people of Israel their kingdom again?
Jesus said to them, The Father is the only one who has the authority to decide dates and times. They are not for you to know.
But when the Holy Spirit comes upon you, you will be filled with power, and you will be witnesses for me in Jerusalem, in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.
After Jesus said this, he was lifted up into the sky. While they were watching, he went into a cloud, and they could not see him anymore
They were staring into the sky where he had gone. Suddenly two men wearing white clothes were standing beside them.
They said, Men from Galilee, why are you standing here looking into the sky? You saw Jesus carried away from you into heaven. He will come back in the same way you saw him go.
Then the apostles went back to Jerusalem from the Mount of Olives. This mountain is 1000 steps from Jerusalem.

They entered the city and went up to the room where they were staying: Peter, John, James and Andrew, Philip and Thomas, Bartholomew and Matthew, James the son of Alphaeus, Simon the Patriot, and Judas son of James.
The apostles always prayed together with a few women and with Mary, the mother of Jesus, and his brothers were also with them.
One day there were about 120 believers together and Peter got up and said to them:

‘Friends, long ago the Holy Spirit told King David to write about Judas in the Old Testament and that is what has happened now. Judas went to the Jewish leaders, and he told them how they could catch Jesus. Acts 1: 17 Judas was one of us and he worked with us,
but then he took the money that the Jewish leaders gave him, and he bought a field. A few days later he fell on his face and his stomach burst open and his insides came out.
When they told the people of Jerusalem what had happened, they said the name of that field in their own language would be Akeldama. It means Field of blood.
We read these words in the book of Psalms: His house must be empty, and no one must live there. And: Someone else must do his work.
Paul also went back to the towns of Derbe and Lystra. In Lystra there was a believer whose name was Timothy. His mother was a Jew and a believer and his father was a Greek.
The believers in Lystra and Iconium said good things about Timothy.
Paul wanted Timothy to go with him, so Paul circumcised Timothy because of the Jews that lived in those Romans towns. All the Jews knew that Timothy’s father was a Greek.

Then Paul and those with him traveled through other cities. Paul and those with him gave the believers the rules and decisions from the apostles and elders in Jerusalem. They told them to obey these rules.
Paul and those traveling with him encouraged the churches to become stronger in their faith, and the number of believers grew every day.
Paul and those traveling with him went to the country of Phrygia and Galatia and they preached there. They wanted to go to the country of Asia, but the Holy Spirit did not allow it.
When Paul and those traveling with him came into the country of Mysia, Paul and those traveling with him tried to go to the country of Bithynia, but the Holy Spirit stopped them again.
Then Paul and those traveling with him went through Mysia and came to the town of Troas.

That night Paul saw a vision. A man from Macedonia came to Paul. The man stood there and begged Paul, Come across to Macedonia and help us. Acts 10:10 After Paul had seen this vision, Paul and those traveling with him immediately got ready to sail to Macedonia because we believed that God had called us to preach the Good News to those people.
Paul and those traveling with him sailed from the town of Troas straight to the Island of Samothrace. The next day Paul and those traveling with him were in the town of Neapolis.

From there Paul and those traveling with him went to the town of Philippi, in the first part of the country of Macedonia, where the Romans ruled, and Paul and those traveling with him stayed in Philippi for a few days.

On the Sabbath day Paul and those traveling with him went outside the town to a place by the river. Paul and those traveling with him looked for a place where the Jews came together to pray and sat down and Paul and those traveling with him started to talk to the women who came there.

One of the women who listened to Paul and those traveling with him was Lydia, she sold expensive purple material. Lydia was from the city of Thyatira. Lydia was there to worshipped the God. As Paul and those with him preached Lydia listen and The Lord opened her heart and she accepted the message that was preached.

Lydia and all the people in her family were baptised. Then Lydia told Paul and those traveling with him and said: If you think I really believe in the Lord, then please come to my house and stay there. She asked Paul and those traveling with him again and again until we said yes and we went to her home.

One day when Paul and those traveling with him were going to pray, and a slave girl met us. She had an evil spirit in her that gave her the power to say what would happen in the future. People paid to listen to her and she got a lot of money for her owners.
This slave girl followed Paul and those traveling with him and she continued to shout and say: These men are servants of the Most High God. They will tell you how God can save you.

The slave girl followed Paul and those traveling with him and she shouted this for many days and then Paul became angry. He turned to the girl and said to the evil spirit: By the power of Jesus Christ, I command you to come out of her! Immediately, the spirit came out.

The owners of the slave girl saw that they would no longer get money from this girl. The owners were angry and they grabbed Paul and Silas and they pulled them into the court, where the leaders of the town were.
The owners told the judge and the leaders: These men are Jews and they are making problems in our town.
Paul and Silas are telling the people to do things that are not right for us as Romans to do.
The whole crowd turned against Paul and Silas. The officials tore the clothes off both men and ordered that they should be beaten.
After the soldiers had beaten Paul and Silas with many stripes, they put them in jail and they told guard to make sure they didn’t escape.
Then the guard put Paul and Silas in a room deep inside the jail. He put Paul and Silas feet in blocks of wood.
About midnight Paul and Silas were praying and singing songs to praise God. The other prisoners were listening to them.
Suddenly there was a strong earthquake. The ground and the jail trembled. Immediately all the doors of the jail opened and all the chains of the prisoners fell off.
When the guard woke up and saw that the doors of the jail were open, he took out his sword. He wanted to kill himself because he thought that all the prisoners had run away.
But Paul shouted very loudly and said to him: Do not kill yourself. All of us are here.
The guard asked someone to bring a light and he ran into the room where Paul and Silas were. He trembled and knelt down on the ground in front of Paul and Silas’ feet.
The guard took Paul and Silas outside and he asked them: Sirs, what must I do to get saved?
They Paul and Silas said to the guard, Believe in the Lord Jesus and you will be saved— you and your family.
Paul and Silas told the guard and all the people in his family about the message of the Lord.
The guard took them the same hour and he washed their wounds and sores. Then Paul baptised the guard and all the people in his family.
The guard took them to his house and he gave them food to eat. He and his family were very happy because he now believed in God.
The next morning the Roman leaders sent soldiers to the guard and told him to let Paul and Silas out of jail.
The guard said to Paul: The Roman leaders said that I must let you go free. Come out and go in peace.

But Paul said to the guard: We are Roman citizens and the Roman leaders told the soldiers to beat us where everyone could see it and they put us in jail. But they did not first take us to a court to judge us. Now they want us to go out of jail and they want no one to know about it. No, they can not do that. Those Roman leaders must come here to this jail themselves and they must let us out.
When the soldiers told the Roman leaders that Paul and Silas were Roman citizens, they became afraid.
The Roman leaders came to the jail and told Paul and Silas that they were very sorry for what they had done. Then they walked with them and asked them to go out of their town.
Paul and Silas walked away from the jail and they went to Lydia’s house. There they saw the believers and they encouraged them. Then Paul and Silas went away from the town of Philippi.
Later, Paul left Athens and went to the city of Corinth

There he met a Jewish man named Aquila, who was born in the country of Pontus. But he and his wife, Priscilla, had recently moved to Corinth from Italy. They left Italy because Claudius had given an order for all Jews to leave Rome. Paul went to Aquila and Priscilla
They were tentmakers, the same as Paul, so he stayed with them and worked with them.
Every Sabbath day Paul went to the synagogue and talked with both Jews and Greeks, because he wanted them all to believe in Jesus.

When they disagreed with what they were teaching and said that they were cursing God. Paul then shook the dust from his clothes. He said to them, if you are not saved, it will be your own fault! I have done all I can do. After this I will go only to the non-Jewish people.
Then Paul went, and he stayed in the house of a man named Titius Justus, who also worshipped the true God. His house was next to the synagogue.
Crispus was the leader of that synagogue. He and all the people living in his house believed in the Jesus. Many other people in Corinth listen to Paul. They, too, believed and were baptized.
That night, Paul had a vision. The Lord said to him, be not afraid. You must keep on telling the people about Me. You must not stop.
I am with you, and no one will be able to hurt you. Many of my people are in this city.”
Paul stayed there for a year and a half teaching God’s message to the people.
During the time that Gallio was the governor of Achaia, they grabbed Paul and took him to court.
They said to Gallio, this man Paul, is teaching people to worship God in a way that is against our law!
Paul was ready to say something, but Gallio spoke to the Jews. He said, I would listen to you if your complaint was about a crime or other wrong.
But you argue with each other about words and names and about your own laws. You must decide what is right and what is wrong. I will not judge things like this.
So Gallio made them leave the court.
Then they all grabbed Sosthenes, the leader of the synagogue. They beat him before the court. But Gallio did not worry about that.
Paul stayed with the believers for long time. Then he left and sailed for Syria. Priscilla and Aquila were also with him. In Cenchrea Paul cut off his hair, because he had made a promise to God.
Then they went to the city of Ephesus, where Paul left Priscilla and Aquila. While Paul was in Ephesus, he went into the synagogue and talked with the Jews.
They asked him to stay longer, but he refused.
He left them and said, I must go to Jerusalem for the feast. I will come back to you again if God wants me to. And he sailed away from Ephesus.
When Paul arrived at Caesarea, he went to Jerusalem and visited the church there. After that he went to Antioch.

Paul stayed in Antioch for a while. Then he left there and went through the countries of Galatia and Phrygia. He traveled from town to town in these countries, helping the followers of Jesus grow stronger in their faith.
There was a Jew who came to the city of Ephesus. His name was Apollos, born in the city of Alexandria, he was an educated man who knew the Scriptures well.
He had been taught about the Lord and was always excited to talk to people about Jesus. But he only knew about the baptism that John taught.
Apollos started to preach in the church. He was not afraid of people church. When Priscilla and Aquila heard him speak, they took him to their home and helped him understand the way of God.

Apollos was sent to go to Achaia. So, the believers in Ephesus wrote a letter to the believers in Achaia and asked them to accept Apollos. When he arrived there, he was a great help to those who had believed in Jesus because of God’s grace.
He argued very strongly against the Jews before all the people. He clearly proved that the Jews were wrong. He used the Scriptures and showed that Jesus is the Messiah.
In the beginning God made the heaven and the earth
The earth was formless and empty, and darkness covered the deep waters. And the Spirit of God was hovering over the surface of the waters.
Then God said, let there be light, and there was light.
God looked at the light and He saw that the light was good. God took the dark away from the light.
God named the light day, and he named the darkness night. There was evening, and then there was morning. This was the first day.
God said: There must be sky high up above the earth. There must be water above and under the sky.
God made the sky and then there was water above and under the sky.
God named that sky Heaven. There was evening, and then there was morning. This was the second day.
Then God said, “Let the water under the sky be gathered together so that the dry land will appear. And it happened.
God said that the name for the dry land was Earth. He said that the name for the water was sea. God saw that it was good.

Then God said, Let the earth grow grass, plants that make grain, and fruit trees. The fruit trees will make fruit with seeds in it. And each plant will make its own kind of seed. Let these plants grow on the earth. And it happened Genesis 1: 12 The earth grew grass and plants that made grain. And it grew trees that made fruit with seeds in it. Every plant made its own kind of seeds. And God saw that this was good.
It became night and then it was morning. That was the 3rd day.

God said: There must be lights in the sky. They will show when it is day and when it is night. They will be used for signs to show when special meetings begin. They will also show when it is summer, winter, spring or autumn. The lights will also show when the days and years start and when they end.
Let the lights in the sky shine and give light to the earth. Then it happened.
God made the sun, the moon and the stars. The biggest light, the sun, to shine in the day and the smaller light, the moon, had to shine at night.
God put them in the sky to give light on the earth.
He put them in the sky to rule over the day and over the night. They separated the light from the darkness. And God saw that this was good.
There was evening, and then there was morning. This was the fourth day.
Then God said, Let the water be filled with many living things, and let there be birds to fly in the sky of heaven.

God created the whales, and he also created all the living things in the sea. God created every kind of bird that flies in the air. God looked at how He had made the fish and the birds, and He saw that it was good.
God blessed them, and He said: There must be more and more fish and water animals. The water must be full of fish. And there must be lots of birds on earth.
There was evening, and then there was morning. This was the fifth day.

God said: Let the land make all kinds of animals, each having babies of the same animals. The cows, the creatures that move along the ground, and the wild animals, each according to its kind.” And it was so.
God made the different wild animals, the cows and creeping animals. Each was able to make babies after itself. God looked at how He had made the animals and He saw that it was good.

Then God said, Now let Us make people who will be like Us. They will rule over all the fish in the sea and the birds in the air. They will rule over all the large animals and all the little things that crawl on the earth.
So God created humans in his own image. He created them to be like himself. He created them male and female.

God blessed them, and He said: You must have many children and fill the earth with people. You must rule over the fish in the sea and over the birds in the sky. You must rule over all the animals on the earth.
God said, I am giving you all the grain bearing plants and all the fruit trees. These trees make fruit with seeds in it. This grain and fruit will be your food.

Every animal on earth, every bird in the air, and all the little things that crawl on the earth. I am giving all the green plants to the animals. These green plants will be their food. And all these things happened.
God looked at everything that He had made, and He saw that it was very good. It became night and then it was morning. That was the 6th day.
This is how God made heaven and earth and everything in it.
On the seventh day God finished the work he was doing, And God rested from his work.
God blessed the seventh day and made it a holy day. He made it special because on that day he rested from all the work he did while creating the world.
This is the story about the creation of the Heavens and the earth. This is what happened when the Lord God made the earth and the heavens.
There were no plants or trees in the fields on earth, because the Lord God had not given rain on earth and there were no people to care for the plants.
So water came up from the earth and spread over the ground.
Then the Lord God took dust from the ground and made a man. God breathed the breath of life into the man’s nose, and the man became a living soul, and he started to live.
Then the Lord God planted a garden in the East, the name of the place was called Eden. He put the man that he made in that garden.

The Lord God planted many kinds of trees in the ground. The trees were beautiful to look at and their fruit was good to eat. In the middle of the garden. He planted the tree of understanding what is good and what is bad.
A river flowed from Eden and watered the garden. The river then separated and became four smaller rivers.
The name of the first river was Pison. This river flowed around the entire country of Havilah, where there was gold.
12There was good gold in that land, and balm and onyx stones.
The name of the second river was Gihon. This river flowed around the whole land of Ethiopia.
The name of the third river was Hiddekel . This river flowed east of Assyria. The fourth river was the Euphrates.
The Lord God put the man in the Garden of Eden to work the soil and take care of the garden.
The Lord God said to the man: You may eat fruit from any tree in the garden.
But you must not eat from the tree that gives knowledge about good and evil. If you eat fruit from that tree, on that day you will certainly die!
Then the Lord God said, I see that it is not good for the man to be alone. I will make the helper just for him.

Then the Lord God he Lord God used dust from the ground, and made all the animals and the birds. Then He said that they must come to Adam, and he must give a name to each animal. That is how the animals got their names
The man gave names to all the cows, to all the birds in the air, and to all the wild animals. But Adam did not find someone who could help him and live with him.
So the Lord God caused Adam to sleep very deeply. While he was asleep, God took one of the ribs from Adam’s body Then he closed the Adam’s skin where the rib had been.
The Lord God used the rib from the man to make a woman. Then he brought the woman to the man.
And the Adam said, here is a person like me. She was made with bones from my bones and a body from my body. She was taken out of a man, so I will call her woman.
That is why a man leaves his father and mother and is joined to his wife. In this way two people become one.
The man and his wife were naked, but they were not ashamed.
The snake was the smartest of all the wild animals that the Lord God had made. The snake said to the woman and said, Did God really tell you that you must not eat from any tree in the garden?
The woman told the snake, no, we can eat the fruit from the trees in the garden.
But God said that we may not eat the fruit from the tree that is in the middle of the garden. God said we may not eat fruit from that tree, and we may not touch it or we will die.
But the snake said to the woman, you will not die.
God knows that when you eat the fruit from that tree you will learn about good and evil, and then you will be like God!

Then the woman could see that the tree was beautiful, and the fruit looked so good to eat. She also liked the idea that it would make her wise. She then took some of the fruit from the tree and ate it. Her husband was with her, so she gave him some of the fruit, and he ate it.
Then they started to understand everything, and they saw that they were naked. They took leaves from fig trees and sewed the leaves together to make clothes.
During the cool part of the day, the Lord God was walking in the garden. The man and the woman heard him, and they hid themselves among the trees in the garden.
The Lord God called Adam and said, where are you?
The man said, I heard you walking in the garden, and I was afraid. I was naked, so I hid.
God said to the man, who told you that you were naked? Did you eat fruit from that tree that I told you not to eat from!
Adam said: Lord, it was because of the woman You gave me. She gave me some of the fruit and I ate it.
Then the Lord God said to the woman, what have you done? She said, the snake tricked me, so I ate the fruit.

So the Lord God said to the snake, because You have done this bad thing. I make bad happen to you. It will be worse for you than for any other animal. You must crawl on your belly and eat dust all the days of your life.
I will make you and the woman enemies to each other. Your children and her children will be enemies. You will bite her child’s foot, but her child foot will crush your head.

Then God said to the woman, I will cause you to have much trouble when you are pregnant. And when you give birth to children, you will have much pain. You will want your husband very much, but he will rule over you.

Then God said Adam, I told you not to eat the fruit from the tree that will make you understand what is good and what is bad. But you did not listen to Me. You listened to your wife, and you ate the fruit. Because you did not listen to Me, I will curse the ground because of you. You will have to work hard all your life for the food the ground produces.
The ground will grow thorns and weeds for you. And you will have to eat the plants that grow wild in the fields.
You will work very long and hard to get food, and your face will be wet and full of sweat. You will work hard until the day you die, and when you die, you will become dust again.
Adam named his wife Eve. He gave her this name because Eve would be the mother of everyone who ever lived.
The Lord God used animal skins and made some clothes for Adam and his wife.
The Lord God said, look, the man has become like us to know good and evil. And now the man might take the fruit from the tree of life. If the man eats that fruit, he will live forever.
So the Lord God forced the man out of the Garden of Eden to work the ground he was made from.

God forced the man to leave the garden. Then he put Cherub angels and a sword of fire at the entrance to the garden to protect it. The sword flashed around and around, guarding the way to the tree of life.
Adam made love to his wife Eve, and she became pregnant. She gave birth to her son and named him Cain. She said, With the help of the LORD I have given birth to a male child.
Later Eve gave birth again to Cain’s brother, Abel. Abel became a shepherd, and Cain became a farmer.
After some more time, Cain brought some of food that he grew from the ground and gave it as an offering to the LORD.
Then Abel brought the first lamb born to one of his sheep, killed it, and gave the best parts of it as an offering. The LORD was pleased with Abel and his offering,
But God rejected Cain offering. Cain became very angry, and Cain became very sad.
Then the LORD said to Cain, why are you angry? Why are you very sad?
If you had done the right thing, I will accept you. But if you don’t, sin is ready to attack you. Sin desires to have you, but you must rule over it.

Cain talked with Abel his brother about what God had said. And when they were alone in the field, Cain attacked Abel his brother and killed him. Then Cain said to his brother Abel, Let’s go out in the fields.” When they were out in the fields, Cain turned on his brother and killed him.
The LORD asked Cain, where is your brother Abel? He answered, I don’t know. Am I supposed to take care of my brother?
