Soon the world will not see me anymore, but you will see me. Because I live, you will live too.
On that day you will know that I am in my Father, and you are in me, and I am in you.
Whoever has my commands and obeys them is the one who loves me. My Father will love the one who loves me, and I will love him and show myself to him.
Then Judas (not Judas Iscariot) said: Lord, why will you show yourself to us, and not to the world?
Jesus answered: If anyone loves me, he will obey my word. My Father will love him, and we will come to him and make our home with him.
Anyone who does not love me does not obey my words. And these words you hear are not my own; they come from the Father who sent me.
I have told you all this while I am still with you.
But the Helper, the Holy Spirit, whom the Father will send in my name, will teach you all things. He will remind you of everything I have told you.
I leave you peace. My own peace I give to you. I do not give to you the way the world gives. So don't let your hearts be worried, and don't be afraid.
You heard me say: I am going away, and I will come back to you. If you loved me, you would be glad that I am going to the Father, because the Father is greater than I am.
I have told you now, before it happens, so that when it does happen, you will believe.
I will not talk with you much longer, because the ruler of this world is coming. He has no power over me.
But the world must know that I love the Father and that I do exactly what the Father has commanded me. Come now, let us go.

15.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
He cuts off every branch in me that does not give fruit. And he trims every branch that does give fruit, so that it will give even more.
You are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you.
Stay joined to me, and I will stay joined to you. A branch cannot give fruit by itself; it must stay joined to the vine. In the same way, you cannot give fruit unless you stay joined to me.
I am the vine; you are the branches. Whoever stays joined to me, and I to him, will give much fruit. Without me you can do nothing.
Whoever does not stay joined to me is thrown away like a branch and dries up. Such branches are gathered, thrown into the fire, and burned.
If you stay joined to me, and my words stay in you, you may ask for anything you want, and it will be done for you.
This is how my Father is honored: that you give much fruit and show that you are my disciples.
I have loved you just as the Father has loved me. Now stay in my love.
If you obey my commands, you will stay in my love, just as I have obeyed my Father's commands and stay in his love.
I have told you this so that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be full.
This is my command: love one another, just as I have loved you.
No one has greater love than this: to give up his life for his friends.
You are my friends if you do what I command you.
I no longer call you servants, because a servant does not know what his master is doing. Instead, I have called you friends, because I have told you everything I heard from my Father.
You did not choose me; I chose you. And I gave you this work: to go and give fruit that will last. Then the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
This is my command to you: love one another.
If the world hates you, remember that it hated me first.
If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. But you do not belong to the world. I have chosen you out of the world, so the world hates you.
Remember what I told you: a servant is not greater than his master. If people treated me badly, they will treat you badly too. If they obeyed my teaching, they will obey yours too.
They will do all this to you because of my name, because they do not know the One who sent me.
If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
Whoever hates me also hates my Father.
If I had not done among them the works that no one else has done, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have seen these works, and still they have hated both me and my Father.
But this happened so that the word written in their law would come true: They hated me for no reason.
When the Helper comes, I will send him to you from the Father. He is the Spirit of truth, who comes from the Father, and he will tell people about me.
And you also must tell people about me, because you have been with me from the beginning.

16.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I have told you all this so that you will not give up your faith.
They will put you out of the synagogues. In fact, the time is coming when anyone who kills you will think he is serving God.
They will do this because they have not known the Father or me.
But I have told you, so that when the time comes, you will remember I warned you. I did not tell you this at the beginning, because I was with you.
Now I am going to the One who sent me, and none of you asks me: Where are you going?
Instead, your hearts are full of sadness because I have told you these things.
But I tell you the truth: it is better for you that I go away. If I do not go, the Helper will not come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you.
When he comes, he will show the world the truth about sin, about being right with God, and about judgment.
He will show them about sin, because they do not believe in me.
He will show them about being right with God, because I am going to the Father, where you will not see me anymore.
And he will show them about judgment, because the ruler of this world has already been judged.
I still have much more to tell you, but it is too much for you now.
But when the Spirit of truth comes, he will lead you into all truth. He will not speak on his own; he will speak only what he hears, and he will tell you what is coming.
He will honor me, because he will take what is mine and make it known to you.
Everything the Father has is mine. That is why I said the Spirit will take what is mine and make it known to you.
In a little while you will not see me. Then a little while later you will see me.

Some of his disciples said to one another: What does he mean? He says, In a little while you will not see me, and then a little while later you will see me. And he says, Because I am going to the Father.
They kept asking: What does he mean by, A little while? We don't understand what he is saying.

Jesus knew they wanted to ask him about it, so he said to them: Are you asking each other what I meant when I said, In a little while you will not see me, and then a little while later you will see me?
I tell you the truth: you will cry and be sad, but the world will be glad. You will grieve, but your sadness will turn into joy.
When a woman is giving birth, she has pain because her time has come. But when her baby is born, she forgets the pain, because she is so happy that a child has come into the world.
It is the same with you. Now you are sad, but I will see you again. Then your hearts will be glad, and no one can take away your joy.
On that day you will not need to ask me anything. I tell you the truth: my Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
Until now you have not asked for anything in my name. Ask, and you will receive, so that your joy will be full.
I have been speaking to you in word-pictures. But the time is coming when I will not speak that way anymore. Instead, I will tell you plainly about the Father.
On that day you will ask in my name. I am not saying that I will ask the Father for you.
The Father himself loves you, because you have loved me and have believed that I came from God.
I came from the Father into the world. Now I am leaving the world and going back to the Father.
Then his disciples said: Now you are speaking plainly, not in word-pictures.
Now we can see that you know everything, and that you do not even need anyone to ask you questions. This makes us believe that you came from God.
Jesus answered: Do you believe now?
Listen, the time is coming, and is already here, when you will all be scattered to your own homes. You will leave me alone. Yet I am not alone, because the Father is with me.
I have told you these things so that you can have peace in me. In this world you will have trouble. But be brave! I have defeated the world.

17.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: After Jesus said this, he looked up to heaven and prayed: Father, the time has come. Honor your Son, so that your Son may honor you.
You gave him power over all people, so that he could give eternal life to all those you have given him.
And this is eternal life: to know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, the one you sent.
I have honored you here on earth. I have finished the work you gave me to do.
And now, Father, honor me in your presence with the glory I had with you before the world began.
I have made your name known to the people you gave me out of the world. They were yours; you gave them to me, and they have obeyed your word.
Now they know that everything you gave me comes from you.
For I gave them the words you gave me, and they accepted them. They know for sure that I came from you, and they believe that you sent me.
I am praying for them. I am not praying for the world, but for those you have given me, because they are yours.
All that is mine is yours, and all that is yours is mine. And I am honored through them.

I will not be in the world much longer, but they are still in the world, and I am coming to you. Holy Father, keep them safe by the power of your name, the name you gave me, so that they may be one, just as we are one.

While I was with them, I kept them safe by the power of your name, the name you gave me. I watched over them, and not one of them was lost, except the one who was bound to be lost, so that the Scripture would come true.
Now I am coming to you. I say these things while I am still in the world, so that they may have my full joy in them.
I have given them your word, and the world has hated them, because they do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
I am not asking you to take them out of the world, but to keep them safe from the evil one.
They do not belong to the world, just as I do not belong to the world.
Make them holy by your truth; your word is truth.
Just as you sent me into the world, I have sent them into the world.
For their sake I make myself holy, so that they too may be made holy by the truth.
I am not praying only for them. I am also praying for all who will believe in me through their message,
so that they may all be one. Father, just as you are in me and I am in you, may they also be one in us, so that the world may believe that you sent me.
I have given them the glory you gave me, so that they may be one, just as we are one:
I in them and you in me. May they be brought to complete unity, so that the world may know that you sent me and have loved them, just as you have loved me.
Father, I want those you have given me to be with me where I am. I want them to see my glory, the glory you gave me because you loved me before the world began.
Righteous Father, the world does not know you, but I know you, and these followers know that you sent me.
I have made your name known to them, and I will keep making it known, so that the love you have for me may be in them, and that I myself may be in them.

18.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: When Jesus had finished praying, he went out with his disciples across the Kidron Valley. There was a garden there, and he and his disciples went into it.
Judas, who was going to betray him, knew the place, because Jesus had often met there with his disciples.
So Judas came to the garden, leading a group of soldiers and some officers from the chief priests and the Pharisees. They were carrying lanterns, torches, and weapons.
Jesus knew everything that was going to happen to him. So he stepped forward and asked them: Who are you looking for?
They answered: Jesus of Nazareth. Jesus said: I am he. And Judas, who betrayed him, was standing there with them.
When Jesus said, I am he, they drew back and fell to the ground.
So he asked them again: Who are you looking for? And they said: Jesus of Nazareth.
Jesus answered: I told you that I am he. So if you are looking for me, let these others go.
This happened so that the words he had spoken would come true: I have not lost one of those you gave me.
Then Simon Peter, who had a sword, drew it and struck the high priest's servant, cutting off his right ear. The servant's name was Malchus.
But Jesus said to Peter: Put your sword away! Should I not drink the cup the Father has given me?
Then the soldiers, their captain, and the Jewish officers arrested Jesus. They tied him up
and led him first to Annas, who was the father-in-law of Caiaphas, the high priest that year.
Caiaphas was the one who had told the Jewish leaders that it would be better for one man to die for the people.
Simon Peter and another disciple were following Jesus. That other disciple was known to the high priest, so he went with Jesus into the high priest's courtyard.
But Peter waited outside at the door. Then the other disciple, who was known to the high priest, came back, spoke to the girl at the door, and brought Peter in.
The girl at the door said to Peter: Aren't you also one of this man's disciples? He said: I am not.
It was cold, so the servants and officers had made a charcoal fire. They were standing around it, warming themselves. And Peter stood there with them, warming himself too.
Meanwhile the high priest questioned Jesus about his disciples and his teaching.
Jesus answered: I have spoken openly to the world. I always taught in the synagogues and at the temple, where all the Jewish people come together. I said nothing in secret.
Why are you asking me? Ask the people who heard me. They know what I said.
When Jesus said this, one of the officers standing nearby slapped him in the face and said: Is that how you answer the high priest?
Jesus answered: If I said something wrong, then say what was wrong. But if I spoke the truth, why did you hit me?
Then Annas sent him, still tied up, to Caiaphas the high priest.
As Simon Peter stood warming himself, they asked him: Aren't you also one of his disciples? He denied it and said: I am not.
One of the high priest's servants, a relative of the man whose ear Peter had cut off, said: Didn't I see you with him in the garden?
Again Peter denied it, and at that moment a rooster crowed.

Then they led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Roman governor's palace. It was early morning. The Jewish leaders did not go inside, because they did not want to make themselves unclean; they wanted to be able to eat the Passover.
So Pilate came outside to them and asked: What charge are you bringing against this man?
They answered: If he were not a criminal, we would not have handed him over to you.
Pilate said to them: Take him yourselves and judge him by your own law. But the Jewish leaders said: We are not allowed to put anyone to death.
This happened so that the words of Jesus would come true, about the kind of death he was going to die.
Then Pilate went back inside the palace, called Jesus, and asked him: Are you the King of the Jews?
Jesus answered: Is that your own question, or did others tell you about me?
Pilate answered: Am I a Jew? It was your own people and the chief priests who handed you over to me. What have you done?
Jesus said: My kingdom does not belong to this world. If it did, my servants would fight to keep me from being handed over to the Jewish leaders. But my kingdom is not from here.

So Pilate said to him: Then you are a king? Jesus answered: You are right to say that I am a king. I was born for this, and I came into the world for this: to tell people the truth. Everyone who belongs to the truth listens to my voice.
Pilate said to him: What is truth? After he said this, he went out again to the Jewish leaders and told them: I find nothing wrong with this man.
But it is your custom for me to set one prisoner free for you at the Passover. So do you want me to set free the King of the Jews?
They shouted back: No, not this man! Give us Barabbas! Now Barabbas was a robber.
Pilate told the soldiers to beat Jesus with a whip.
The soldiers made a crown of thorn and they put it on Jesus’ head. Then they put a purple robe on Him.
The soldiers said, Hail, King of the Jews (which means Good health! Peace! Long life to you, King of the Jews]! And they slapped Him in the face.
Pilate went outside again. He said to the Jews I am bringing Jesus out to you. I want you to know that I find nothing I can charge him with.
Then Jesus came outside. The crown of thorns was still on his head and Jesus had on the purple robe. Pilate said to the Jews: Look at this Man now.

When the chief priests saw Jesus, they shouted to Pilate: Kill him on a cross! Kill him on a cross!”. Pilate told them: No, You take him and nail him to a cross yourselves. I find nothing I can charge him with
The Jews said to Pilate: We have a law that says he must die, because he said he is the Son of God.
When Pilate heard what the Jews said, he became even more afraid.
Pilate went into the judgement hall again and he said to Jesus: ‘You must tell me where You come from. But Jesus did not answer Pilate.
Then Pilate said to Jesus: Why don’t You talk to me? I have the power to make you free or to kill you on a cross.
Jesus said to Pilate: The only power you have over me is the power given to you by God. That is why the people who brought Me to you have done a bigger sin than you have done.

When Pilate heard what Jesus said, he tried to let Jesus go free, but the Jews shouted: Anyone who makes himself a king is against Caesar. So if you let Jesus go free, that means you are not Caesar’s friend.

When Pilate heard what the Jews said, he took Jesus outside. Then Pilate went to the chair where he always judged, and he sat on it. The name of the place was the Pavement. (The Hebrew name of that place was Gabbata).
It was the day before Passover. On that day the Jews always prepared everything for Passover. It was about noon when Pilate said to the Jews: Look, here is your King.

They shouted loudly Take him away! Take him away! Kill him on a cross! Pilate asked them: Must I crucify Jesus, your King?’ The chief priests said to Pilate: Jesus is not our King. We have only one king and that is Caesar.
So Pilate handed Jesus to the chief priest to be killed on a cross.
Jesus carried his cross and they went out of the city to a place with the name of The Skull. (The Hebrew name of that place is Golgotha).
There the soldiers nailed Jesus to the cross. They also nailed 2 other men to crosses with Jesus, one on his left hand side and one on his right hand side.
Pilate told them to write a sign and put it on the cross. The sign said, Jesus of Nazareth, the king of the jews.
The sign was written in Aramaic, in Latin, and in Greek. Many of the Jews read this sign, because the place where Jesus was nailed to the cross was near the city.
The Jewish chief priests went to Pilate and said to him: You must not write: Jesus, King of the Jews. You must write: Jesus said He is the King of the Jews.
Pilate told them: I wrote it and I will not change it.

After the soldiers nailed Jesus to the cross, they took his clothes. They divided his clothes into 4 parts, one part for each soldier. Jesus had a garment that was all one piece of cloth woven from top to bottom. It did not have a seam.

The soldiers said to each other: We must not tear this garment into smaller pieces. We must draw lots to see who will get it. This happened because it is written in the Old Testament: They divided my clothes, and each one took some of it. They drew lots to get my garment. This is what the soldiers did.
Jesus’ mother was standing near his cross. Her sister was also there and Mary, the wife of Cleophas, and Mary Magdalene.
Jesus saw his mother standing there, and the disciple whom He loved very much, John. Jesus said to his mother: This disciple is now your son.
And Jesus said to the disciple John: This woman is now your mother. From that day John took Jesus’ mother to live in his home.
Jesus knew that He had done all the work on earth that God had sent Him to do. Jesus said: I am thirsty. He said this because these words are written in the Old Testament.
There was a jar full of vinegar wine. The soldiers put a sponge into the vinegar wine and they put it on a hyssop-branch and held it out in front of Jesus mouth.
When Jesus tasted the vinegar wine, He said: Now I have finished my work that I had to do. Jesus bowed his head forward and He died.

This day was Preparation day. The next day was a special Sabbath day. The Jewish leaders did not want the bodies to stay on the cross on the Sabbath day. So they asked Pilate to order that the legs of the men be broken. And they asked that the bodies be taken down from the crosses
So the soldiers came and broke the legs of the two men on the crosses beside Jesus.
When the soldiers came to Jesus, they saw that He was dead, so they did not break Jesus bones.
One of the soldiers took a spear and he pierced the side of Jesus’ body with it. Immediately blood and water came out of his body.
The man who saw this happen told this to the other people and every word that he said is the truth. He knows that he speaks the truth so that you may believe.
All this happened because the Old Testament said it would happen. It says: The people will not break Jesus bones.
At another place in the Old Testament it is written: They will see him. People will look at the one they stabbed.

Joseph, from the town of Arimathea, was a disciple of Jesus but he did not tell anyone because he was afraid of the Jewish leaders. After Jesus had died, Joseph went to Pilate and asked him if he could take the body of Jesus. Pilate said he could take it. Then Joseph took Jesus’ body from the cross.
Nicodemus went with Joseph. He was the man who had come to Jesus before and talked to him at night. He brought about 100 pounds of spices—a mixture of myrrh and aloes.
Joseph of Arimathea and Nicodemus covered Jesus’ body with the myrrh and aloes and they put pieces of cloth around his body. (The Jews always did this before they buried someone).
In the place where Jesus was killed on the cross, there was a garden. In the garden there was a new tomb. No one had ever been buried there before.

Joseph of Arimathea and Nicodemus took Jesus’ body and they buried it in that tomb because it was near them and it was the day before the special Sabbath day when the Jews prepared everything for that day.
Early that Sunday morning Mary Magdalene went to the tomb where Jesus was buried. It was still dark but she saw that someone had rolled away the stone that had been in front of the tomb.

Mary Magdalene ran to Simon Peter and to another disciple, John, that Jesus loved very much. Mary told Peter and John: Someone has taken away the Lord’s body from the tomb and we do not know where they have put Him.
Peter and John went to the tomb.
Peter and John ran to the tomb but John ran faster than Peter and John got there first.
John bowed over and looked into the tomb. He saw the pieces of cloth that were lying there but he did not go into the tomb.
Then Simon Peter also came there. Peter went into the tomb and he saw the long pieces of cloth.
Peter also saw the cloth that had been around Jesus’ head. The cloth was folded and it was not in the same place as the other cloths.

Then John who came to the tomb first went into the tomb. John saw that Jesus’ body was not there and John believed. John 20: 9 At that time the disciples did not understand the Old Testament where it says that Jesus will live again.
Peter and John went back home.
Mary stood outside the tomb. crying. While she was crying, she bent down and looked inside the tomb.
Mary saw 2 angels. They had white clothes. They were sitting where Jesus’ body had been before. One angel sat where Jesus’ head had been and the other angel sat where his feet had been.
The angels asked Mary: Why are you crying? Mary said: They have taken away my Lord’s body and I do not know where they have put Jesus.
After Mary had said this, Mary looked back and she saw Jesus. Jesus was standing there, but she did not know that it was Jesus.

Jesus said to her: Why are you crying? Who are you looking for? Mary thought that it was the man who worked in the garden and Mary asked Him: Sir, have you taken away my Lord’s body? If you took Him, please tell me where you put my Lord so I can go and get Him. John 20: 16 Jesus said to her: Mary. Then Mary turned to Jesus and said: Rabboni. (Rabboni is a Hebrew word that means: My Lord).

Jesus said to her: Do not hold Me. I have to go to my Father in heaven. You must go back to my brothers, the disciples. Go and tell them that I am going to my Father and my God. He is also your Father and your God now.
Mary Magdalene told the disciples: I have seen the Lord. Mary told them what Jesus had said to her.

That Sunday evening, 1st day of the week, the disciples were together. All the doors were closed and locked because they were afraid of the Jewish leaders. Suddenly, Jesus was standing there among them. He said: Peace be with you.

After Jesus had said this, Jesus showed the disciples his hands and the side of his body where the soldier had pierced Him with his spear. The disciples were very happy when they saw the Lord. John 21 Again Jesus said to them: I give you my peace. The Father has sent Me. Now I send you.
After Jesus had said this, Jesus breathed on them and said: I give you the Holy Spirit.
If you forgive the sins of anyone, their sins are forgiven. If there is anyone whose sins you don’t forgive, their sins are not forgiven.
Thomas, one of the 12 disciples, also had another name. His other name was Didymus. He was not with the other disciples when Jesus appeared to them.

Later the disciples told Thomas: We have seen the Lord. Thomas said to them: No, I do not believe it. I will believe only when I see the wounds in Jesus hands where the nails were and when I can put my finger into those wounds. I will believe only when I can put my hand into the wound on the side of Jesus’ body.
8 days later the disciples were together again and Thomas was with them. The doors were locked, but Jesus came and He stood in front of them. He said to them: I give my peace to you.
Then Jesus said to Thomas: Come, put your finger into my wounds and look at my hands. Come and put your hand into the wound on the side of my body. Do not doubt. You must believe.
Thomas said to Jesus: You are my jLord and my God.
Jesus said to Thomas: Thomas, you believe because you see me. Great blessings belong to the people who believe without seeing me
Jesus did a lot of other miracles that the disciples saw, but those miracles are not written in this book.
The miracles that you read about in this book are written so that you can believe in Jesus hrist, the Son of God. If you believe in Him, you will have eternal life because you belong to Him.
Later, Jesus appeared again to his disciples at the Lake of Tiberias. This is how it happened:
There were some of the disciples together. Simon Peter, Thomas (called Didymus), Nathanael from Cana in Galilee, the two sons of Zebedee, and two other followers.

Simon Peter said to the Disciples: I am going fish. Then the disciples with him said to him: We will go with you. Right then they got into the boat and they went fishing. They fished all night and caught no fish.
The next morning, very early, Jesus was standing on the beach but the disciples did not know that it was Jesus.
Jesus said to them: Children, did you catch any fish? They said to Him: No

Jesus said to them: You must throw your fishing net out on the right-hand side of the boat, then you will catch fish. They threw the fishing net into the water on the right-hand side and there were so many fish in the net that they could not pull the net into the boat.

The disciple that Jesus loved very much said to Peter: That is the Lord. When Simon-Peter heard that it was the Lord, he wrapped his coat around himself (because he did not have on all his clothes). Peter jumped into the water and went to Jesus.
The other disciples came in the boat and they pulled the fishing net that was full of fish behind them. They were not far from the beach, about 200 cubits.
When the disciples in the boat came to the beach, they saw the burning coals of a fire and fish and bread on the coals.
Jesus said to them: Bring some of the fish that you have caught.
Simon-Peter climbed into the boat and he pulled the fishing net onto the beach. The net was full of big fish. There were 153 fish in the net, but the net did not tear.
Jesus said to them: Come and eat breakfast. The disciples did not ask g the fish.
This was the 3rd time that Jesus appeared to his disciples after he was raised from death.

When the disciples had finished eating breakfast, Jesus said to Simon-Peter: Simon, son of Jonas, do you love Me more than these others do (with total commitment and devotion?) Peter said: Yes, Lord, You know that I love You. [with a deep, personal affection, as for a close friend] Jesus said to Peter: feed the littlest of my people.

Jesus asked him the 2nd time: Simon, son of Jonas, do you love Me with total commitment and devotion? Peter said to Him: ‘Yes, Lord, You know that I love You with a deep, personal affection, as for a close friend. Jesus said to Peter: Then you must be a shepherd to my people.

Jesus asked Peter the 3rd time: Simon, son of Jonas, do you love Me with a deep, personal affection for Me, as for a close friend? Peter was sad because Jesus had asked him 3 times if he loved Him. He said to Jesus: Lord, You know everything. You know that I love you with a deep, personal affection, as for a close friend. Jesus said to Peter: Then you must take care of my people.

Peter, I tell you this and it is sure: When you were young, you dressed yourself and walked where you wanted to go. But when you are old, you will hold out your hands and someone else will dress you. Then they will take you where you do not want to go.
When Jesus said this, He told Peter how he, Peter, would die and how he would honour God. Then Jesus said to Peter: Come with Me.

Peter looked back and he saw that the disciple that Jesus loved very much was following them. It was the same disciple who had sat next to Jesus when they were eating and who had asked Jesus: Lord, who is the one who will betray You?
When Peter saw the disciple that Jesus loved very much, he said to Jesus: Lord, what will happen to him?
Jesus said to Peter: If I want him to live until I come, you don’t have to worry about that. I told you to follow Me.

The disciples thought that this disciple that Jesus loved very much would never die. But they were wrong. Jesus did not say that this disciple would not die. Jesus said to Peter: If I want him to live until I come, you don’t have to worry about that.
The disciple that Jesus loved very much was the disciple who told all of this and who wrote it all in this book. We know that he told the truth.

Jesus did a lot of other things, but you do not read about them all in this book. If people wanted to write all of those things, then I don’t think there would be enough place in the world for all those books.
The Lord talked to Jonah the second time said:
You must go to the big city of Nineveh. You must tell those people what I said to you.
Jonah did what the Lord said, and he went to Nineveh. Nineveh was a very big city. If a person walked from one side of the city to the other side, he walked for 3 days.
Jonah walked into the city for one day, and he told the people: After 40 days, Nineveh will be destroyed!

The people of Nineveh believed God. They decided to stop eating for a time to think about their sins. They put on special clothes to show they were sorry. All the people in the city did this, from the most important to the least important
The king of Nineveh heard what Jonah said. He got up from his throne, and he took off his beautiful royal clothes. He put on sackcloth, and he sat on the ash-heap.

The king wrote a special message and sent it throughout the city: A command from the king and his great rulers: For a short time, no person or animal should eat anything. Nothing living in Nineveh will eat or drink water
All the people must put on sackcloth. Their animals must also have on sackcloth. All the people must pray to God, and they must stop all the bad things and sins that they are doing.
Maybe God will change his plan and feel sorry for us. Maybe He will stop being angry with us and not punish us, so we will not die.
0 God saw what the people did. He saw that they stopped doing evil. So, God changed his mind and did not do what he planned. He did not punish the people.

Jerubbaal also known as Gideon, took all of his men and got up early the next morning and they put up their tents near the well of Harod. The camp of the Midianite soldiers was on the hill of Moreh in the valley north of Gideon’s camp.
The Lord said to Gideon: You have too many men with you. If I the LORD, help them defeat the Midianite men, then the Israelites will boast and say that they have saved themselves.
So make an announcement to your men. Tell them, Anyone who is afraid may leave Mount Gilead and go back home. At that time 22,000 men left Gideon and went back home, but 10,000 still remained.
Then the Lord said to Gideon: There are still too many men with you. Take them down to the water, and I will choose who must stay. I will tell you who can go with you and who can not go with you.

So Gideon led the men down to the water. There the Lord said to him, Separate the men like this: Those who drink the water by using their tongue to lap it up like a dog will be in one group. And those who bend down to drink will be in the other group.
There were 300 men who used their hands to bring water to their mouth and lapped it like a dog does. All the other people bent down and drank the water
The Lord said to Gideon, I will use the 300 men who lapped the water like a dog. I will use them to save you, and I will allow you to defeat the Midianites. Let the other men go home

So Gideon sent the other men of Israel home. He kept the 300 men with him. Those 300 men kept the supplies and the trumpets of the other men who went home. The camp of the Midianite soldiers was in the valley lower than the camp of Gideon.
That night the Lord said to Gideon: You must attack the camp of the Midianite soldiers. I will help you defeat them.
But if you are afraid to attack them, then take your servant, Phurah, with you
Listen to what the Midianite soldiers say, and then you will no longer be afraid to attack them. Gideon and Phurah went to the side of the camp.

The Midianites, the Amalekites, and all the other people from the east were camped in that valley. There were so many people that were like grasshoppers. It seemed like they had as many camels as there are grains of sand on the seashore

When Gideon got to the camp, a man was telling his friend what he had dreamed. He said: I dreamed I saw a round loaf of barley bread rolling into the Midianite camp. It rolled up to our tent and hit our tent so hard that it fell to the ground and the bread rolled over it.
The man’s friend said: This means that the Israelite Gideon, son of Joash, is coming to fight against us and God will help him win. He will defeat the Midianites and all the soldiers in this camp.
When Gideon heard what they said about the dream and what it meant, he bowed and praised the Lord. He went back to the camp of Israel and said: Come, the Lord will help you defeat the army of Midian.
Gideon divided his 300 men into 3 groups. He gave each man a trumpet and a jar with a torch in it.
Gideon told the men of Israel: Look at me, and when we come near the camp, you must do the same as I do.
When I and all the men with me blow the trumpet, you must also blow your trumpet around the Midianite camp, and you must shout: Make war for the Lord and for Gideon.
Gideon and the 100 men with him got to the Midianite camp in the middle of the night when a new group of guards took their places. Gideon and his men blew their trumpets, and they broke their jars

Then all three groups of Gideon’s men blew their trumpets and smashed their jars. The men held the torches in their left hands and the trumpets in their right hands. As they blew their trumpets, they shouted, A sword for the Lord and a sword for Gideon
Gideon’s men stood still around the camp of the Midianites but the soldiers in the camp were shouting and running away.

When Gideon’s 300 men blew their trumpets, the Lord made the Midianite soldiers kill one another. They fled to the town of BethShittah near Zererath and to the border of the land from the town AbelMeholah near Tabbath.
Then Gideon called more Israelite men from the Naphtali tribe, the Asher tribe and the Manasseh tribe. They came and they chased the Midianite soldiers:

Gideon also sent messengers to the people of the Ephraim tribe in the mountains. He told them to catch the Midianite people before they went over the Jordan River, as far as the town of Beth-Barah. These Israelite men came and they did what Gideon asked.

They caught the 2 generals of the Midianite soldiers. Their names were Oreb and Zeeb. They killed Oreb and named the place Oreb-Rock and they killed Zeeb and named that place Zeeb Wine-press. After they had chased the soldiers of Midian, they took the heads of Oreb and Zeeb and they gave them to Gideon on the other side of the Jordan River.
Jesus was filled with the Holy Spirit, Jesus returned from the Jordan River. And then the Spirit led him into the desert.
The devil tested Jesus for 40 days and tried to tempt Him to do wrong. Jesus had not had any food for 40 days and He was very hungry.
The devil said to Jesus: If You are the Son of God, say to these stones that they must change into bread.
But Jesus said to The Devil: In the Old Testament it is written: ‘It is not just bread that keeps people alive but by obeying every word of God.
Then the devil took Jesus to a high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world in a moment of time
The devil said to Jesus: I will make you king over all these places. You will have power over them, and you will get all the glory. It has all been given to me. I can give it to anyone I want
You must just worship me, then You will have it all.
But Jesus said to the devil: get behind me. In the Old Testament it is written: You must worship the Lord your God and serve only Him.
Then the devil took Jesus into Jerusalem and up to the top of the temple. The devil said to Jesus: If You are the Son of God, then jump down,
because it is written in the Old Testament: God will tell his angels to protect You.
They will hold You in their hands, so that You will not hurt your foot on a stone.
But Jesus said to the devil: It is also written in the Old Testament: You must not test the Lord your God.
After the devil had tempted Jesus to do wrong, he went away for a long time.
Jesus went back to Galilee with the power of the Spirit. Stories about him spread all over the area around Galilee.
Jesus started to teach them in the synagogues, and everyone said good things about Him.
Jesus came to Nazareth, the town where He had lived when He was a child. On the Sabbath day he went to the synagogue as he always did. He stood up to read.
They gave Jesus the book of the prophet Isaiah. Jesus opened the book and He read what Isaiah had written. Jesus read:

The Spirit of the Lord leads me. The Lord has anointed me. God said I must bring the good news to poor people. God has sent me to heal the brokenhearted, to tell the prisoners that they are free, and they can go. He sent me to heal the blind people so they can see again. I must free all the people who are suffering.
I must say: Now is the time to announce that the time has come for the Lord to show his kindness.
Then Jesus closed the Book, and He gave it back to the person who had to put it away. Jesus sat down and everyone in the synagogue looked at Him.
Then Jesus started to talk to the people, and He said: Today God has made these things, what I have read from the Bible, happen.
Everyone there said good things about Jesus. They were amazed to hear him speak such wonderful words. They said, “How is this possible? Isn’t he Joseph’s son?

But Jesus said to them: Later you will say the words of this proverb to Me: If You are a doctor, then you must heal Yourself. You will also say: They told us of all the miracles that You have done in Capernaum. You must do the same things here in this town.
But I tell you: People do not listen to a prophet in the town where his people live.
But in truth, I tell you, there were many widows in Israel in the days of Elijah, when the heavens were shut up 3 years and 6 months, and a great famine came over all the land,
but the Lord did not tell Elijah to go to one of them. He sent Elijah only to a widow in the town of Zarephath in the land of Sidon.
And in the time when the prophet Elisha lived, there were a lot of lepers in the land of Israel. But the Lord did not heal all of them. He healed only Naaman, from the land of Syria.
Everyone in the synagogue was angry when they heard what Jesus said.
They got up and forced Jesus to go out of town. Their town was built on a hill. They took Jesus to the edge of the hill to throw him off
But Jesus walked through the crowd of people, and He went away.
Jesus went to Capernaum, a town Galilee. Jesus went to the synagogue on the Sabbath day to teach the people.
They were amazed at his teaching because he spoke with authority.
In the synagogue there was a man who had an evil spirit in him. The spirit shouted loudly and said:
Jesus of Nazareth, what do You want to do with us Jesus of Nazareth? Have You come to kill us all? I know who You are. You are the holy One of God.

But Jesus warned the evil spirit to stop. He said, Be quiet! Come out of the man! The evil spirit threw the man down on the ground in front of everyone. Then the evil spirit left the man and did not hurt him.
All the people were surprised, and they said to each other: The words that Jesus says come from God. He tells the evil spirits to go out and they listen to Him and go out.
And so the news about Jesus spread to every place in the whole area.
Jesus went away from the synagogue, and He went to Simon’s house. Simon’s mother-in-law was very sick in bed. The people asked Jesus to help her.
e stood very close to her and ordered the sickness to go away. The sickness left her immediately, and she got up and began serving them.
When the sun went down, the people started to bring all the sick people to Jesus. Jesus put his hands on everyone, and He healed them.
He also chased demons out of many people and the demons shouted: You are the Son of God. The demons knew He was the Christ. That is why Jesus was angry with them and told them to keep quiet.
The next morning Jesus went away to desert place. Many people were looking for Him. When they saw Him, they did not want Him to go away from them.
But Jesus told them: I must also go to the people in other towns. I must tell them the Good News about God who is King. This is why God sent Me.
Then Jesus went to other towns in Galilee and He started to teach the people in the synagogues.
After this, the Lord chose 70 more disciples. He told them to go in pairs of 2 to all the towns and places where Jesus would go later.

Jesus told the 70 disciples There is such a big harvest of people to bring in. But there are only a few workers to help witness to them. Pray to the Lord of the harvest and ask Him to send more people to witness.
Go now. Listen! I am sending you, and you will be like sheep among wolves.
Don’t carry any money, a bag, or sandals. Do not let anyone delay you from doing your mission.
Before you go into a house, say, Peace be with this home.
If the people living there love peace, your blessing of peace will stay with them. But if not, your blessing of peace will come back to you.
Stay in that house of peace. Eat and drink what the people give you. For the worker is worthy of his wages. Don’t leave that house to stay in another house.
If you go into a town and the people welcome you, eat the food they give you.
Heal the sick people who live there, and tell them, God’s kingdom has come near to you.
But if you go into a town and the people do not welcome you, then go out into the streets of that town and say,
Even the dirt from your town that has sticks to our feet we wipe off against you. But remember that God’s kingdom is coming soon.
I tell you, on the judgment day it will be worse for the people of this town than for the people of Sodom.

It will be bad for you, Chorazin! It will also be bad for you, Bethsaida! I did many miracles in you. If I did the same miracles in Tyre and Sidon, then the people in those cities would have changed their lives and stopped sinning a long time ago. They would have repented; they would had worn sackcloth and sat in ashes to show that they were sorry for their sins.
But on the judgment day God will punish you more than He will punish the people of the towns of Tyre and Sidon.
And you people of Capernaum, you think you will be honored in heaven because of the many things Jesus did in your town? No, you will go down to hell.

Jesus told the 70 disciples, when anyone listens to you, they are really listening to me. But when anyone refuses to accept you, they are really refusing to accept me. And when anyone refuses to accept me, they are refusing to accept the one who sent me.
When the 70 disciples came back from their trip, they were very happy. They said, Lord, even the demons obeyed us when we used your name
Jesus said to them, I saw Satan falling like lightning from the sky.
Satan is the enemy, but I have given you more power than he has. I have given you power to crush his snakes and scorpions under your feet. Nothing will hurt you.
Yes, be happy, not because the spirits obey you, but because your names are written in heaven.

At that time Jesus feel very happy. Jesus said, I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth. You have hidden these things from those who are so wise and so smart. But you have shown them to people who are like little children. Yes, Father, you did this because it’s what you really wanted to do.

My Father has given me all things. No one knows who the Son is: only the Father knows. And only the Son knows who the Father is. The only people who will know about the Father are those the Son chooses to tell.
Then Jesus turned to his followers. They were there alone with him. Jesus said, it is a great blessing for you to see the things that you have seen.
I tell you, many prophets and kings wanted to see what you have seen, but they could not. They also wanted to hear what you have hear, but they could not.
Then a lawyer stood up to test Jesus. He said, Teacher, what must I do to get eternal life?
Jesus said to the lawyer, what is written in the law? What do you understand from it?
The lawyer answered, Love the Lord your God with all your heart, all your soul, all your strength, and all your mind. Also, love your neighbor the same as you love yourself.
Jesus said, you have answer is right. Do this and you will live.
The lawyer wanted to show that the way he was living was right. So, he said to Jesus, but who is my neighbor?
Jesus said, A man was going down the road from Jerusalem to Jericho. Some robbers surrounded him, tore off his clothes, and beat him. Then they left him lying there on the ground almost dead.
It happened that a Jewish priest was going down that road. When he saw the man, he did not stop to help him, but walked on the other side.
Next, a Levite came near. When he came to where the man was laying. He looked at the man that was hurt, but he just walked away on the other side.
Then a man from town of Samaria traveled down that road. He came to the place where the hurt man was lying. He saw the man and felt very sorry for him.

A man from town of Samaria went to him and poured oil and wine on his wounds. Then he covered the man’s wounds with cloth. A man from town of Samaria had a donkey. He put the hurt man on his donkey, and he took him to an inn. There he cared for him.

The next day, the man from town of Samaria took out two silver coins and gave them to the man who worked at the inn. The man from town of Samaria said, Take care of this hurt man. If you spend more money on him, I will pay it back to you when I come again.
Then Jesus said to the lawyer, which one of these three men do you think was really a neighbor to the man who was hurt by the robbers?
The lawyer answered, the one who helped him. Jesus said, then you go and do the same.
While Jesus and his followers were traveling, he went into a town, and a woman named Martha let him stay at her house.
She had a sister named Mary. Mary was sitting at Jesus’ feet and listening to him teach.
But her sister Martha was busy doing all the work that had to be done. Martha went in and said, Lord, don’t you care that my sister has left me to do all the work by myself? Tell her to help me!
Jesus answered her, Martha, Martha, you are getting worried and upset about too many things.
Only one thing is important. Mary has made the right choice, and it will never be taken away from her.
All the tax-collectors and sinners came to Jesus to listen to Him.
The Pharisees and teacher of the Laws began to complain, Look, this man welcomes sinners and even eats with them!
Then Jesus told them this parable. He said:
If one of you has 100 sheep and one sheep gets lost, what will he do? He will leave the other 99 sheep in the desert, and he will go and look for the lost sheep until he finds it.
And when the man finds the sheep that was lost, he will be very happy and he will carry the sheep on his shoulders.
When he comes home, he will call his friends and neighbors and say to them, Be happy with me because I found my lost sheep.

In the same way, I tell you, The angels in heaven are happy when one sinner turns to God and starts to do what God wants. They are more happy about one sinner that repents than about the 99 people who think they live in the right way and do not have to repent.

Jesus said: And if a woman has 10 silver coins and she loses one of the coins, what will she do? She will light a lamp and sweep and clean the floor and look in her house until she finds the coin that was lost.
When she finds the coin, she will call her friends and her neighbors and say to them: Be happy with me because I have found the coin that I lost!
In the same way, it’s a happy time for the angels of God when one sinner decides to repent
Jesus also said: A man had 2 sons.
The youngest son said to his father: Father, please give me my part of your money and the things that I will inherit when you die. So, the father gave his sons their part of everything he had.
few days later the younger son gathered up all that he had and left. He traveled far away to another country, and there he wasted his money living like a fool.
After he spent everything he had, there was a terrible famine throughout the country. He was hungry and needed money.
So he went and got a job with one of the people who lived there. The man sent him into the fields to feed pigs.
The son wanted to eat the food that the pigs ate because he was hungry, but no one gave him any food.
The son realized that he had been very foolish. He thought, all my father’s hired workers have plenty of food. But here I am, almost dead because I have nothing to eat.
I will get up and go back to my father and I will say to him: Father, I have sinned against God, and I have done wrong to you.
I am no longer worthy to be called your son. But let me be like one of your hired workers.
The son got up and he went back to his father. When he was still far away, his father saw him, and his father was moved with love. His father ran to his son and hugged and kissed him.
Then the son said to his father: Father, I have sinned against God, and I have sinned against you. I am no longer worthy to be called your son.
But his father told his servant: Bring the best clothes and put them on him. Put a ring on his finger and put sandals on his feet.
And bring our best calf and kill it so that we can celebrate with plenty to eat.
My son was dead, but now he is alive again! He was lost, but now he is found! So, they began to have a party.
The oldest son was in the field. When he came near the house, he heard people making music and dancing.
He called one of the servants and asked him what was happening at home.
The servant said: Your brother has come back, and your father killed the best calf to eat. He is happy because he has his son back safe and sound.
The oldest son was very angry, and he did not want to go into the house. His father came out and he begged his oldest son to come inside.

But the oldest son said to his father: Father, I have worked for you for many years, and I have always listened to you and obeyed you, but you never gave me a little goat so that I could eat it with my friends.
But then this son of yours comes home after wasting your money on prostitutes, and you kill the best calf for him!
The father said to the oldest son: My son, you are always with me and everything that I have is yours.
But this was a day to be happy and celebrate. Your brother was dead, but now he is alive. He was lost, but now he is found.
Jesus said to his disciples: There was a rich man who hired a manager to take care of his business. Later, he learned that his manager was wasted the rich man’s money.
Then the rich man called his manager and said to him. I have heard bad things about you. Give me a report of what you have done with my money. You can not be my manager anymore.
The manager then said to himself: What will I do? My master is taking my job away from me. I am not strong enough to dig ditches. I am too proud to beg.
But now I know what I will do, I will do something to make friends, so that when I lose my job, they will welcome me into their homes.
Then the manager called all the people who owed the master. He asked the first man: How much oil do you owe my master?
The man answered him: I owe him 100 jars of oil. The manager said to him, here is your bill. Hurry! Sit down and write it 50 jars of oil.

Then the manager asked another man: How much do you owe my master? The man answered and said, I owe him 100 measures of wheat. Then the manager said to him, here is your bill; write it for 80 measures of wheat.

The master told the dishonest manager that he had done a smart thing The people of this world look out for themselves better than the people who belong to the light. Luke 8:9 I say to you, make friends for yourselves by means of the wealth of the unrighteousness, so that when it is all gone, they will receive you into the eternal dwellings
He who is faithful in a very little is faithful also in much; and he who is dishonest in little is dishonest also in much.
If you have not been faithful in the wealth of the unrighteous, who will entrust to you the true riches?
If you have not been faithful in that which is another mans, who will give you that which is your own?

No servant can serve two masters; for either he will hate the one and love the other, or he will work hard to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve God and You can not think that money is as important as God.
When the Pharisees heard the things that Jesus said, they did not want to believe Him. They laughed at Him because they loved money.

Then Jesus said to the Pharisees: When people look at you, you want them to think that you are good people. But God knows what you are thinking. There are things that people think are very good and important, but God hate this thinking.

The law and the prophets were important until the time when John the Baptist came; But from the time of John the Baptist, I have started to bring you the Good News about God who is King. Everyone is trying to force their way into where God is King.
But it is easier for heaven and earth to pass away than for one stroke of a letter of the Law to fail.
Everyone who divorces his wife and marries another commits adultery, and he who marries a woman divorced from her husband commits adultery.
Jesus said: There was a rich man who wore expensive clothes of purple. Every day ate the best food.
At the rich man’s gate was a poor beggar who lay there. His name was Lazarus. He was full of sores.
Lazarus covet to eat the food that fell from the rich man’s table. Moreover, even the dogs came and licked his sores
The poor man, Lazarus, died and was carried by the angels to Abraham’s side in heaven. The rich man also died and was buried;
When the rich man opened his eyes in hell, he was suffering with a lot of pain. He saw Lazarus far away. Lazarus was with Abraham.
The rich man cried and said: Father Abraham, please have mercy for me and send Lazarus so that he can put his finger in water and touch my tongue, because I am suffering a lot with pain in this fire.
But Abraham said: Son, you must remember that in your life on earth you had all the good things and Lazarus had the bad things. Lazarus is now happy and comfortable, and you have a lot of pain.
There is a very deep hole between you and us. We can not go to you, and you can not come to us.
The rich man said: Please send Lazarus to my father’s house.
The rich man said I have 5 brothers and he must warn them so that they do not come to this place of pain.
Abraham said to the rich man: Your brothers have the books of Moses and the books of the prophets. Your brothers must read them and listen to them.
But the rich man answered and said: but if someone goes to them from the dead, they will repent.
But Abraham said: If they don’t want to do what the books of Moses and the prophets say, then they will never listen to anyone who has died and comes back to them.
One day Jesus was in the temple teaching people. Jesus told them the Good News. The chief priests and the teachers of the law, came to talk to Jesus
The chief priests and the teachers of the law asked Jesus: Tell us what authority you have to do these things. Who gave you this authority?
Jesus said to the chief priests and the teachers of the law: I will ask you one question.
John the Baptist came, and he baptized people. Who sent John to do it? Was it God who sent John, or did the people send John?

The chief priests and the teachers of the law talked to each other and said: If we say that John was sent by God, then Jesus will ask us: Why did you not believe what John said? Luke 20: 6 But if we say that John’s baptism was from someone else, the people will stone us to death. They all believe that John was a prophet.
The chief priests and the teachers of the law said to Jesus: We don’t know who sent John.
Then Jesus said to the chief priests and the teachers of the law: Then I will not tell you who told Me that I may do these things.
Jesus told the people this story. Jesus said: A man planted a vineyard. Then he rented out the vineyard to other farmers and went on a journey for a long time to another country.

When it was time to pick the grapes, the owner sent a servant to the farmers who rented his vineyard, and the owner asked the farmers to give the servant his share of the grapes. The farmers grabbed the servant and beat him and chased him away. The farmers gave the servant no grapes.
The owner of the vineyard sent another servant to the farmers. The farmers took the 2nd servant and beat him also. The farmers dishonored him, and they gave him no grapes.
The owner of the vineyard then sent a 3rd servant to the farmers, but the farmers beat him, and they threw the servant out of the vineyard.
Then the owner of the vineyard said: What can I do? I will send my son that I love very much. I think they will respect my son. Luke:
When the farmers saw it was the owner’s son, they said to each other: This son will inherit all the owner’s money and things one day. Let us kill him, then we will get everything.
The farmers threw the owner’s son out of the vineyard, and they killed him. Jesus ask the farmers, What. do you think the owner of the vineyard will do to the farmers do?
Jesus said. The owner will come and kill all those bad farmers and he will give the vineyard to other farmers. When the people heard this, they said: this will never happen.

But Jesus looked into their eyes and said: If you say that this must never happen, what do these words in the book of Psalms mean? The stone that the builders did not want to use is now the most important stone in the building.
Everyone who falls over this stone will die and break his legs. And if this stone falls on anyone, will scatter him like dust.

The chief priests and the teachers of the law then tried to arrest Jesus immediately. The chief priests and the teachers of the law knew that Jesus told this parable to show that they were the same as the bad farmers. But the chief priests and the teachers of the law were afraid of the people, and they did not arrest Jesus.

The chief priests and the teachers of the law waited for the right time to get Jesus. They sent people who were spies to Jesus. These spies told Jesus that they wanted to learn more from Him, but the leaders wanted Jesus to say something wrong against the Roman ruler so that they could punish Him.
The spies then asked Jesus: Teacher, we know that what you say and teach is true. It doesn’t matter who is listening, you teach the same to all people. You always teach the truth about God’s way.
Now tell us: Is it right to pay taxes to Caesar? Must we pay taxes to him or not?’
But Jesus knew that these men were trying to trick him. Jesus said to them, why you trying test me?
Jesus said show me a penny. Jesus asked the spies: Whose head and whose name is on this money? They answered Him: It is Caesar’s head and his name.
Jesus said to the spies: That is right. Then you must give to Caesar what belongs to him, but you must also give to God what belongs to Him.
The men were amazed at his wise answer. They could say nothing. They were not able to trick Jesus there in front of the people. He said nothing they could use against him.
A few Sadducees (Sadducees believe that people will not rise from death.) came to Jesus. They said to Jesus:
Teacher, Moses wrote that if a married man dies and had no children, his brother must marry his widow. Then they will have children for the dead brother
Now, there were 7 brothers. The oldest brother married a woman, but he died, and they did not have any children.
The 2nd brother then married the widow of the oldest brother, but he also died, and they did not have any children.

The same happened with the 3rd brother. He also married her, and he died. It happened to all 7 brothers. All of them married this woman but they did not have any children. Luke 20;32 Later, the woman also died.
Now we ask You, Jesus: Whose wife will this woman be when all the dead people rise and live again? Because all of these 7 brothers were married to her.
Jesus said the Sadducees: In this life people get married.
But when God is King, He will give life to all the people who have believed in Jesus and have died. Then people will not be married and they will not get married.
They will be like the angels in heaven who do not die and who live with God in heaven. They will be children of God because God gave them life and He made them live again.

Moses also said that people who have died will rise and live again. He wrote about it when he spoke about the burning bush. There God said to Moses: I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac and the God of Jacob.
God is not the God of dead people. He is the God of people who live.
Some of the teachers of the Laws said to Jesus: Teacher, that is right. You have given a very good answer.
From that day on the people were afraid to ask Jesus any more questions.
Jesus asked teachers of the Laws: Why do people say the Christ is the Son of David?
David says in the book of Psalms: The Lord God said to my Lord: Sit by me at my right side,
I will make your enemies bow before You. You will put your feet on them.
David said the Christ is his Lord. Now, how can the Christ be the Son of David if He is the Lord?
Jesus said to his disciples and all the people who could hear Him:

You must beware of the teachers of the Laws. They walk where all the people can see them, they have long clothes, and they want everyone to greet them and to respect them. They want to sit where the most important people sit in the synagogues and at the feasts.
But these teachers of the Laws are people who cheat widows and take their houses. Then they try to make themselves look good by saying long prayers. God will punish them very much.
Very early Sunday morning, the women came to the tomb where Jesus’ body was laid. They brought the sweet-smelling spices they had prepared.
When the women came to the tomb, the women saw that someone had rolled away the stone that was in front of the tomb.
The women went into the tomb but they did not see the body of the Lord Jesus there.
The women did not understand what had happen. While they were thinking about this, two men in shining clothes stood beside them.
The women were afraid and they bowed down with their faces to the ground. The men said to them: Why do you look for the One who lives, here in a tomb for dead people?
Jesus is not here. God has raised Him up and made Him live again. Remember what Jesus told you when you were in Galilee.
Jesus said God would give Him to bad people and they would kill Him, the Son of man. They would nail Jesus to the cross but on the 3rd day Jesus would rise and live again.
The women then remembered what Jesus had said.
The women went back home and told everything to the 11 apostles and the other believers.
The women who saw what happened at the tomb were Mary Magdalene, Joanna, Mary the mother of James and some other women with them. When they told these things to the apostles,
The apostles did not believe them. They said it could not be true.

Then Peter got up and ran to the tomb. Peter bowed down and looked into the tomb and he saw only the cloth that they had put around the body of Jesus. Peter went back and was very surprised about what had happened.
That same Sunday 2 of the men who followed Jesus walked to the town of Emmaus. It is about seven miles from Jerusalem.
The 2 men talked to each other about everything that had happened to Jesus.
While the 2 men were talking and thinking about the events that had happen, Jesus came and walked with them.
But the 2 men did not know that it was Jesus. Jesus did not want them to know at that time who He was.
Jesus asked the 2 men: Why are you so sad what are you talking about as you walk down this road?
Cleopas, one of the men, asked Jesus: You must be the only person in Jerusalem who doesn’t know what has just happened there.

Jesus asked them: What things?’ They said: All the things that happened to Jesus, the Man from Nazareth. Jesus was a prophet who did wonderful miracles. Jesus was a Teacher from God. God was with Him and all the people listened to Jesus.
But the chief leading priests and our leaders handed him over to be judged and killed. They nailed him to a cross.
We were hoping that Jesus would be the one to free Israel. But then all this happened. And It has been three days since Jesus was killed.
Also Today some of our women told us an amazing thing. Early this morning they went to the tomb where the body of Jesus was laid.
But the women did not find Jesus body there. They came and told us they had seen some angels in a vision. The angels told them Jesus was alive!
So some of our group went to the tomb too. It was just as the women said. They saw the tomb, but they did not see Jesus.
Jesus said to the 2 men: You are foolish and slow to realize what is true. You should believe everything the prophets said.
The prophets said the Messiah must suffer these things before he begins his time of glory.
Then Jesus started to explain to the 2 men what the books of Moses and the prophets and the other books of the Old Testament said about Jesus.
When the 2 men got near the town of Emmaus, Jesus said He wanted to go to the next town.
But the 2 men wanted Jesus to stay. The 2 men begged Jesus, Stay with us. It’s almost night. There’s hardly any daylight left. So Jesus went in to stay with the 1 men.
When they started to eat, Jesus took the bread and thanked God for it. Jesus broke the bread and gave it to the 2 men.
Immediately God opened their eyes and the 2 men saw that it was Jesus. But then Jesus disappeared. The 2 men could not see Jesus anymore.
The 2 men said to each other: Now we know why we were so happy when Jesus talked to us on the road. When Jesus explained the Bible to us.
The 2 men immediately got up and went back to Jerusalem. The 2 men found the 11 apostles and other believers with them.
The 2 men said: The Lord really has risen from death! Jesus did appear to Simon.
Then the 2 men told the apostles and other people everything that had happened when they walked to Emmaus, and when they saw that it was Jesus when He broke the bread.
While the two men were saying these things to the other followers, Jesus stood there with them and Jesus said to them: Peace be with you.
The followers of Jesus were afraid. They thought they had seen a ghost.
But Jesus said to them: Why are you afraid? Why don’t you believe what you see?
Look at my hands and my feet. It’s really me. Touch me. You can see that I have a living body; ghost does not have a body with flesh and bones.
After Jesus said this, Jesus showed the his followers his hands and his feet.
Jesus followers were amazed and very, very happy to see that Jesus was alive. But the Jesus Followers still could not believe what they saw. Jesus said to his followers, Do you have any food here?
Jesus followers gave Jesus a piece of fish and honey.
Jesus took it and his followers watched Jesus eat it.

Then Jesus said to his followers: Do you remember what I said when I was with you before I died? I told you that everything that is written about Me in the books of Moses, and also the things that are written in the books of the prophets and the book of Psalms, must happen.
Then Jesus helped them to understand the Bible.
Jesus told his followers: The Old Testament says that the Christ must suffer and die, but that the Christ will rise and start to live again on the 3rd day.

It is written that the people who believe in God must go and tell everyone that God forgives the sins of everyone who believes in Christ and turns to God and starts living as He wants. You must start telling the people in Jerusalem.
Tell the people what you saw Me do and what I said.
And I will send upon you what my Father has promised. But you must wait in the city of Jerusalem, until the power from above comes down upon you.
Then Jesus told the apostles to go with Him. Jesus took the apostles to a place near the town of Bethany. Then Jesus held up his hands and blessed them.
Jesus blessed the apostles then he was separated from them and carried into heaven.
The apostles worshipped Jesus and then they went back to Jerusalem. They were very happy.
They went to the temple every day and they praised God.
After the Sabbath day, Mary Magdalene and Mary, the mother of James and Salome, bought some sweet-smelling spices to put on Jesus’ body.
Very early on that Sunday morning, the women were going to the tomb. It was very early after sunrise.
The women asked each other: Who will roll away the stone in front of the tomb?
The stone was very big, but when the women came there, they saw that someone had rolled the stone away.
The women went inside the tomb, and they saw a young man sitting on the right hand side. The young man had a long white robe. The women were afraid.

The young man said to the women; Do not be afraid. You are looking for Jesus of Nazareth who was killed on a cross. Jesus is risen and He lives again. Jesus is not here. Look, here is where Joseph of Arimathea and Nicodemus put his body.
Go and tell Jesus disciples and Peter: Jesus is going to Galilee. Jesus is going before you. You must also go. You will see Jesus there as He told you.
The women ran away from the tomb. The women were very afraid and amazed. They did not tell anyone about what happened, because they were afraid.

Jesus rose from the dead and started to live again early on Sunday morning. Jesus first appeared to Mary Magdalene. Mary Magdalene was the woman who had had 7 demons in her before Jesus cast out the demons.
Mary Magdalene went to the disciples who followed Jesus and Mary Magdalene told them that Jesus had appeared to her. They were crying and mourning.
When Mary told them that Jesus was alive and that she had seen Jesus, the disciples did not believe Mary.
Later, Jesus appeared to two followers while they were walking in the country. But Jesus did not look the same as before he was killed.
The 2 disciples went back to the city and they told the other disciples but the other disciples did not believe them.

Later Jesus appeared to the 11 disciples when they were eating. Jesus rebuked the disciples because they had so little faith. They were stubborn and refused to believe the people who said Jesus had risen from death.
Jesus said to them, “Go throughout the whole world and preach the gospel to all people
Whoever believes and is baptized will be saved. But those who do not believe will be judged guilty.
And the people who believe will be able to do these things as proof: They will use my name to force demons out of people. They will speak in languages they never learned.
They will pick up snakes with their hands, and if they drink something poisonous, they will not become sick or die. They will put their hands on sick people and the sick will be healed.
After the Lord Jesus had said these things to his disciples, Jesus was carried up into heaven. There, Jesus sat at the right side of God.
The disciples went and they preached everywhere and the Lord worked with them. Jesus did miracles and powerful things to show that their message was true.
This is the family history of Jesus Christ. He came from the family of David, who was from the family of Abraham.
Abraham was the father of Isaac, Isaac was the father of Jacob and Jacob was the father of Judas and his brothers.
Judas was the father of Perez and Zera and their mother was Tamar. Perez was the father of Esrom and Esrom was the father of Abam.
Aram was the father of Aminadab, Aminadab was the father of Naasson and Naasson was the father of Salmon.
Salmon was the father of Boaz and Boaz’s mother was Rahab. Boaz was the father of Obed and Obed’s mother was Ruth. Obed was the father of Jesse,
Jesse was the father of King David. David was the father of Solomon and Solomon’s mother was the wife of Uriah.
Solomon was the father of Reboam, Reboam was the father of Abia and Abia was the father of Asa.
Asa was the father of Jehoshaphat, Jehoshaphat was the father of Joram and Joram was the father of Uzziah.
Uzziah was the father of Joatham, Joatham was the father of Ahaz and Achaz was the father of Hezekiah.
Hezekiah was the father of Manasses, Manasses was the father of Amon and Amon was the father of Josiah.
Josiah was the father of Jechonias and his brothers. This was the time when the people of Judea were taken away to be slaves in the land of Babylon.
After the people of Judea were slaves in Babylon, Jechonias became the father of Salathiel. Salathiel was the father of Zerubbabel,
Zerubbabel was the father of Abiud, Abiud was the father of Eliakim and Eliakim was the father of Azor.
Azor was the father of Sadoc, Sadoc was the father of Achim and Achim was the father of Eliud.
Eliud was the father of Eleazar, Eleazar was the father of Matthan and Matthan was the father of Jacob.
Jacob was the father of Joseph and Joseph was Mary’s husband. Mary was the mother of Jesus who was named the Christ.

From Abraham to King David there were 14 generations. From King David to the time when the people of Judea became slaves in Babylon, there were 14 generations and from the time when they were slaves in Babylon to the time when Christ was born, there were 14 generations.

This is how the birth of Jesus Christ happened. His mother Mary was engaged to marry Joseph. But before they married, he learned that she was expecting a baby. (She was pregnant by the power of the Holy Spirit.)
Mary’s husband, Joseph, was a good man. He did not want to cause her public disgrace, so he planned to divorce her secretly.

But after Joseph thought about this, an angel from the Lord came to him in a dream. The angel said, Joseph, son of David, don’t be afraid to accept Mary to be your wife. The baby inside her is from the Holy Spirit.
She will have a Son and his name must be Jesus, because He will save his people from their sins.
All these things happened as the prophet had said:
The virgin will become pregnant, and she will have a son, and his name will be Emmanuel, Emmanuel means God is with us.
When Joseph woke up from his sleep, he did what the angel of the Lord had told him, and he married Mary.
He did not have sex with her. He waited until after the Child was born. Joseph named the Baby Jesus.

Jesus was born in the town of Bethlehem in the land of Judea. He was born at the time when Herod was king. After Jesus was born, there were some wise men who came to Jerusalem. They were from a land in the east.
The wise men was asking the people: Where is the Child who was born to be king of the Jews? We saw the star that shows he was born. We saw it rise in the sky in the east and have come to worship him
When they told King Herod about this, he and all the people in Jerusalem were upset and very worried.
King Herod called the chief priests and the teachers of the Laws. They had a meeting and King Herod asked them: Where will the Christ be born?
The chief priests and the teachers of the Laws said to Herod: The Christ will be born in the town of Bethlehem in the land of Judea. That is what the prophet wrote:
God said: Bethlehem, in the land of Judea, you a small place but a leader will come from you. He will be born in you and that ruler will lead Israel, my people.
Then King Herod called the wise privately. Herod asked the wise men when they had first seen the star.
Then King Herod said to the wise men: Go to Bethlehem and find the Child. And when you find Him, you must come back and tell me. I also want to go and worship Him.

After the king said this to the wise men, they went away. The star that they saw in the east went in front of them and they followed the star until it came to the place where the Child was and then the star stopped.
When the wise men saw the star there, they were very happy.

Then the wise men went into the house, and they saw the Child with his mother Mary. The wise bowed down and worshiped him. The wise men opened their bags, and they gave their gifts to the Child: They were gold, incense and myrrh.
God came to the wise men in a dream, and He warned them and said they must not go back to King Herod. That is why they went home on another road.
