After the wise men went away, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and he said: Get up, take the Child and his mother and flee to Egypt. You must stay there until I tell you to come back home, because King Herod wants to kill the Child.
That night Joseph went away to Egypt. He took the Child and his mother with him.
They stayed in Egypt until King Herod died. This happened as the prophet had written: The Lord said, I, God, have called my son out of Egypt.

King Herod found out that the wise men from the east had deceived him. King Herod was very angry. He sent soldiers to kill all the boys in the town of Bethlehem who were 2 years old and younger than 2 years. They also killed the boys who lived near Bethlehem. King Herod told the soldiers to kill all the boys that were 2 years old and younger, because that was the time that the wise men told King Herod that they had seen the star.
All of this happened as the prophet Jeremiah wrote:
A sound was heard in Rama, crying and great sadness. Rachel cries for her children, and she cannot be comforted, because her children are dead.
After King Herod died, the angel of the Lord came to Joseph. He appeared to him in a dream in the land of Egypt. The angel said to Joseph:
Get up, take the Child and his mother and go back to the land of Israel, because the people who wanted to kill the Child have died.
Joseph took the Child and his mother, and he went back to Israel.

But Joseph heard that Archelaus was now king in Judea. Archelaus became king when his father Herod died. So, Joseph was afraid to go to Bethlehem. Then, after being warned in a dream, he went away to the area of Galilee
Then Joseph went to stay in the town of Nazareth. This happened because the prophet wrote, the people will say Jesus is a man from Nazareth.
The Holy Spirit took Jesus into the desert so that the devil could test Him.
Jesus did not eat any food for 40 days and 40 nights. He was very hungry.
The devil came to Jesus and he tried to make Him do something wrong. The devil said to Jesus: If You are the Son of God, tell these stones to change into bread.
But Jesus answered and said, “It is written in the Old Testament, It is not just bread that keeps people alive. Their lives depend on what God says.’”
Then the devil took Jesus into Jerusalem, the Holy City, and put Jesus on a high place at the edge of the Temple area.

The devil said to Jesus: If You are the Son of God, then off, because it is written in the Old Testament: God will command his angels to help you, and their hands will catch you, so that you will not hit your foot on a rock.
Jesus said to the devil: It is also written in the Old Testament: You must not test the Lord your God.”
Then the devil took Jesus to a very high mountain. He showed Jesus all the lands of the world and all the wonderful things in them.
The devil said to Jesus: I will give all of this to You if You kneel before me and worship me.
Then Jesus said to the devil: Go away, Satan. In the Old Testament it is written: You must worship the Lord your God and serve only Him.
The devil went away from Jesus and angels came to help Jesus.
When they told Jesus that John the Baptist was in jail, Jesus went to Galilee.
Jesus did not stay in the town of Nazareth. He went to the town of Capernaum, near Lake Galilee. This town was next to Lake Galilee where the tribes of Zebulun and Naphtali had lived long before.
This happened because the prophet Isaiah had said:
Land of Zebulun and Naphtali, near the road to Lake Galilee, near the Jordan River, Galilee, land of people who are not Jews,
The people who live in spiritual darkness have seen a great light. The light has shined for those who live in the land that is as dark as a grave.
Then Jesus started to preach. Jesus said: You must turn to God and live as He wants, because God’s kingdom is now very near

Jesus walked on the beach of the Sea of Galilee. Jesus saw 2 brothers, Simon and Andrew. Simon also had another name. Simons other name was Peter. They were fishermen and they were busy putting their fishing nets into the lake.
Jesus said to Peter and Andrew, Come, follow me, and I will make you a different kind of fishermen. You will bring in people, not fish.
Simon and Andrew immediately stopped working with their fishing nets and they went with Jesus.

Jesus walked further and Jesus saw 2 other brothers, James and John. They were the sons of Zebedee. They were in the boat with their father and they were working with their fishing nets and mending them. Jesus called them to come with Him.
They immediately got out of their boat and went away from their father and went with Jesus.

Jesus went all over the land of Galilee, teaching people in the synagogues and preaching. Jesus told them the Good News that God in heaven rules over everyone. Jesus also shealed people who were sick.

The news about Jesus spread all over Syria, and people brought to him all those who were sick. They were suffering from different kinds of diseases and pain. Some had demons inside them, some suffered from seizures, and some who could not walk. Jesus healed them all.
There were many people who went with Jesus. They came from the Galilee, and from Decapolis, and from Jerusalem, and from Judaea, and from beyond Jordan.
If you judge others, you will be judged by God the same way you judge them. God will treat you the same way you treat others.
Why do you see the small piece of wood in your brother’s eye, but you do not see that there is a big piece of wood in your own eye?
You can not say to your brother that you want to take out the small piece of wood in his eye, when you have a big piece of wood in your own eye.
You are a hypocrite. You must first take out the big piece of wood from your own eye. Then you will be able to take out the small piece of wood from your brother’s eye.
You must not throw things that belong to God to the dogs. And you must not throw your pearls in front of the pigs. The pigs will trample the pearls with their feet and run after you.
Ask and keep on asking and it will be given to you; seek and keep on seeking and you will find; knock and keep on knocking and the door will be opened to you.
Yes, whoever continues to ask will receive. Whoever continues to look will find. And whoever continues to knock will have the door opened for them.
When a boy asks his father for bread, his father will never give his son a stone.
And when a boy asks his father for a fish, his father will never give his son a snake.
You people are so bad, but you still know how to give good things to your children. So surely your heavenly Father will give good things to those who ask him.
Do for others what you would want them to do for you. This is the meaning of the Law of Moses and the teaching of the prophets.
Jesus also said: You must go in through the small gate. The other gate, the gate to hell, is big and wide and it is easy to walk on that road. Many people go through that big gate.
But the gate to eternal life is small and it is not easy to walk on this road. There are only a few people who find this road and walk on it.’
Be careful of false prophets. They come to you and look gentle like sheep. But they are really dangerous like wolves.
You can identify them by the way they act. Can you pick grapes from thornbushes, or figs from thistles? Matthew 7: 17 A good tree always has good fruit and a bad tree always has bad fruit.
A good tree can not have bad fruit and a bad tree can not have good fruit.
Every tree that has bad fruit will be cut down and people will throw that tree into the fire.
You will know these false people by what they do.
Not everyone who calls me Lord will enter God’s kingdom. The only people who will enter are those who do what my Father in heaven wants.
On that last Day many will call me Lord. They will say, Lord, Lord, by the power of your name we spoke for God. And by your name we forced out demons and did many miracles.
Then I will tell those people clearly, Get away from me, you people who do wrong. I never knew you.
Jesus said: Whoever hears these teachings of mine and obeys them is like a wise man who built his house on rock.
The rain came down and the rivers were full of water. The wind blew hard and it started to shake the house. But it did not fall because it was built on rock.
Whoever hears these teachings of mine and does not obey them is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
The rain came down and the rivers became full of water. The wind blew hard and it started to shake the house and destroyed it. There was nothing left of it.
When Jesus finished talking to the people, all of them were amazed by what He taught them,
because they knew his words came from God. Jesus did not teach them like the teachers of the Laws did.
When Jesus walked away from the temple, his disciples came to Him and the disciples showed Jesus the buildings of the temple.
Jesus said to the disciples: Do you see these buildings? The buildings will be destroyed. Every stone will be thrown down to the ground. Not one stone will be left on another.

Later when Jesus sat on the Mount of Olives, Jesus disciples came to Him when they were alone with Him. The disciples asked Jesus: Tell us: When will these things happen that You have told us about? How will we know when it will happen? And what will happen to prepare us for your coming and the end of time
Jesus said to his disciples: You must be careful that no person tells you lies.
Many people will use my name and say that they are the Christ. They will deceive many people who will believe them.
Do not be afraid when you hear there is war near you or a war far from you. These things must happen, but that will not be the end of everything.

One nation will fight against another nation, and the people of one land will make war against the people of another land. There will be many diseases and earthquakes in many places and people will have no food to eat.
This will be only the beginning of the suffering.
People will arrest and punish you. They will beat you and they will kill some of you. People of all nations will hate you because you believe in Jesus.
During that time many believers will lose their faith. They will turn against each other and hate each other.
There will be many false prophets and they will teach things that are wrong to the people, and the false prophets will deceive the people.
There will be so much more evil in the world that the love of most will grow cold
But if someone continues to follow Jesus, then God will save the one who believes.
People who believe will bring this message to all the people on earth. All the nations will hear it and only then will it be the end of the world.
Jesus said: You will see a false god sacrifices in the temple. It will make God very angry. The prophet Daniel wrote about it. (You who read this should understand what it means.)
When this happens, the people who are in Judea must run and flee to the mountains.
If someone is on the roof of his house, he must run. He must not first go into the house to get something.
And if someone is in the field, he must not go home to get his clothes.
During that time it will be hard for women who are pregnant or have small babies!
Pray that it will not be winter or a Sabbath day when these things happen and you have to run away
it will be a time of great trouble. There will be more trouble than has ever happened since the beginning of the world. And nothing as bad as that will ever happen again.
And if those days of trouble had not been cut short, no human life would be saved; but for the sake of God’s chosen ones those days will be shortened.
When someone says in that time: Look, here is the Christ, or Look, there He is, you must not believe him.

Because there will be people who are false, and they will say they are the Christ. There will also be false prophets and they will make signs and do miracles. They will try to deceive the chosen people of God to follow them.
Now I have warned you about this before it happens.
When people say: Look, the Christ is in the desert, then you must not go there. And if they say: Look, He is here in a room, you must not believe them.
When I, the Son of man, come, it will be like the lightning in the sky that you can see from the east to the west.
It is like looking for a dead body: You will find it where the eagles are gathering above.
Right after the trouble of those days, this will happen: The sun will become dark, and the moon will not give light. The stars will fall from the sky, and everything in the sky will shake.

Then there will be a sign in the sky that will show that I, the Son of man, am coming. And the people of the earth will cry and mourn when they see the Son of man coming on the clouds in the sky. I will come with power and great glory
When you hear the big trumpet, I will send my angels to bring all the chosen people of God together. They will come from the east, the west, the north and the south, from all over the world.
Jesus said: You must learn this lesson from the fig tree: When the branches of a fig tree are getting soft and the leaves start to grow, then you know that summer is near.
So, when you see all these things happen, then you will know that the Lord is near, and He will be coming soon.
I tell you this and it is sure: Some people who are alive now will still be living when these things will happen.
Heaven and earth will disappear one day, but my words will not disappear.
Jesus said: No one knows on what day and what time of day I will come again. The angels in heaven do not know when it will be. Only the Father knows.

When I, the Son of man, come again, it will be like in the days of Noah. Matthew 24;38 In those days before the flood of water came, the people were eating and drinking and getting married until the day that Noah went into the ark.
They did not know what was happening, until the flood came and the water took everything away. It will be the same when I, the Son of man, come again.
Then 2 men will be working in field and God will take one person but not the other person.
And 2 women will be grinding wheat together and God will take one woman but not the other one.
You must be ready, because you do not know when the Lord will come.
But you must know this: If the owner of a house knows when a thief will come in the night, then he will stay awake and he will not let the thief break into his house and steal his things.
That is why you must always be ready, because you do not know when I, the Son of man, will come.
Who is the wise and trusted servant? The master trusts one servant to give the other servants their food at the right time. Who is the one the master trusts to do that work?
When the master comes and finds that servant doing the work he gave him, it will be a day of blessing for that servant
I tell you this: The master will make that servant the manager of all the things of the master.
But if the master chooses a bad servant to be the manager over the other servants, that servant will think his master will stay away for a long time, or that his master will not come back.
Then he will start to beat the other servants and he will eat and drink with bad people who are always drunk.
And one day when the master of the servants comes back, he will surprise that bad servant who did not think that the master would come back on that day and at that time.
Then the master will punish that servant. He will send him away to be with the hypocrites, where people will cry and grind their teeth with pain.
Jesus said: in the future when God in heaven rules over all, will be like ten virgins who took their lamps and went to meet the bridegroom before the wedding.
Five of the girls were foolish, and five were wise.
The 5 foolish girls took their lamps, but they did not take extra oil for the lamps
The 5 wise girls took their lamps and also jars of oil.
The bridegroom was late, and all the girls became sleepy and they started to sleep.
At midnight someone shouted, the bridegroom is coming. Come out and meet him.
Then all 10 girls woke up and made their lamps ready.
The foolish girls said to the wise girls: Give us some of your oil, because our lamps are going out.
The wise girls answered, No, The oil we have might not be enough for all of us. But go to those who sell oil and buy some for yourselves.
So the foolish girls went to buy oil. While they were gone, the bridegroom came. The girls who were ready went in with the bridegroom to the wedding feast. Then the door was closed.
When they came back, the door was closed and they shouted: Sir, sir, open the door for us.
But the bridegroom said: I am telling you this and it is true: I do not know you.
Jesus then said: You must be ready, because you do not know what day and what time of the day the Son of man will come.
Jesus said: in the future when God in heaven rules over all, it is like when a man went away from his home to another country. He called his servants and told them to look after his money.
He gave 5 talents of money to the first servant, 2 talents of money to the 2nd servant and one talent of money to the 3rd servant.
The servant who got 5 talents started to buy and sell many things and he got 5 more talents, so then he had 10 talents.
The servant with 2 talents did the same and he got 2 more talents, so then he had 4 talents.
But the servant who had one talent dug a hole in the ground and he hid his master’s money.
After a long time the master of the servants came back and asked them what they had done with his money.

The servant who got 5 talents brought the 10 talents and he said: Sir, you gave me 5 talents. Here are another 5 talents that I have worked for. Matthew 25: 21 His master said: Good Job. You are a good servant who can be trusted. You did well with that small amount of money. I will let you care for much greater things. Come and share my happiness with me.
The servant who got 2 talents came with the 4 talents and he said: Sir, you gave me 2 talents. Here are another 2 talents that I have worked for.

His master said: That is good. You are a good servant. You did what I wanted. You have worked well with the little money that I gave you. Now I will give you much to work with. Come in and be happy with me, your master.
The servant who got one talent came and said: Sir, I know you are a very hard-working man. You harvest wheat that you did not plant. You cut wheat where you did not sow any seeds.
I was afraid, so I buried the money and hid it under the ground. Here is the one talent that you gave me. It belongs to you.
His master said: You are a bad and lazy servant. You know I harvest wheat where I did not plant seeds and I cut wheat where I did not sow any seeds.
Why didn’t you put my money in the bank? Then when I came home, the bank would have given me more money.
He said to his other servants: Take the money away from him and give it to the servant who has 10 talents.
I will give more to everyone who has something, then he will have a lot more. But I will take away from anyone who does not have anything. I will take away what he has.
Then the master said, throw that useless servant outside into the darkness, where people will cry and grind their teeth with pain.
Jesus said: The Son of Man will come again with greatness, and all his angels will come with him. He will sit as king on his great and glorious throne.
Then all the nations will come together before Me, and I will separate all the people into 2 groups like a shepherd separates his sheep from the goats.
I will put the sheep on my right-hand side and the goats on my left-hand side.
Then the king will say to the godly people on his right, Come, my Father has great blessings for you. The kingdom he promised is now yours. It has been prepared for you since the world was made.
When I was hungry, you gave Me food to eat and when I was thirsty, you gave Me water to drink. When I was a stranger, you welcomed Me into your house.
I did not have any clothes and you gave Me clothes to wear. I was sick and you cared for Me. I was in jail, and you visited Me.
Then the godly people will answer, Lord, when did we see you hungry and give you food? When did we see you thirsty and give you something to drink?
When did we see that You were a stranger and when did we welcome You into our homes? When did we see that You had no clothes and when did we give You clothes to wear?
When did we see that You were sick or in jail and when did we visit You?
Then the king will answer, the truth is, anything you did for any of my people here, you also did for me.
Then the king will say to the evil people on his left, go away from Me. God has punished you. You will go into the fire that burns forever. God has made that fire for the devil and his angels.
I was hungry, you gave Me no food to eat and when I was thirsty, you gave Me no water to drink.
I was a stranger, and you did not welcome Me into your home and when I had no clothes, you did not give Me clothes to wear. I was sick and in jail and you did not come to visit Me.”

Then the people on his left-hand side will ask the King: Lord, when did we see that You were hungry or thirsty, or when were You a stranger and did not have clothes? When were You sick or in jail? When did we not help You?
The king will answer, the truth is, anything you refused to do for any of my people here, you refused to do for me.
Then these evil people will go away to be punished forever. But the godly people will go and enjoy eternal life.
The Sabbath day was over. It was Sunday morning just before the sun came up. Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to see the tomb.
An angel of the Lord came down from heaven and there was a big earthquake. The angel went to the tomb, and he rolled the stone away and sat on the stone.
The angel looked as bright as lightning and his clothes were as white as snow.
The soldiers who guarded the tomb were afraid. They trembled and became like dead men.
The angel said to the women: Do not be afraid. I know you are looking for Jesus who was crucified.
Angel said Jesus is not here. Jesus is alive again. Jesus said He would live again. Come and look. Here is the place where his body was lying.
Go quickly and tell Jesus disciples that Jesus lives again. Tell them Jesus is going to Galilee and they must also go there. They will see Jesus there. This is what I came to tell you.
Mary Magdalene and the other Mary left the tomb quickly. They were afraid, but they were also very happy. They ran away from the tomb to tell Jesus disciples.

Mary Magdalene and the other Mary were running when Jesus stood before them, and Jesus greeted them. Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went to Jesus. They knelt before Him and touched Jesus’ feet and worshipped Him.
Then Jesus said to Mary Magdalene and the other Mary: Do not be afraid. Go and tell my disciples to go to Galilee. They will see Me there.
Mary Magdalene and the other Mary went away from the tomb, and the soldiers who had guarded the tomb went to Jerusalem. The soldiers told the chief priests everything that had happened at the tomb.
Then the chief priests met with the older Jewish leaders came together and they made plans. They gave a lot of money to the soldiers.
The chief priests and the older Jewish leaders told the soldiers: You must tell the people that the disciples of Jesus came in the night to steal his body while you were sleeping.
If the governor hears about this, we will talk to the governor and keep you soldiers out of trouble.
The soldiers then took the money, and they did what the chief priest and Jewish leaders had told them to do. The Jews still believe that this is what happened.
The 11 disciples went to the land of Galilee. Jesus had told the disciples to go to a mountain.
When the disciples saw Jesus, they knelt before Jesus and worshipped Him. But some of the disciples doubted if it was Jesus.
Jesus came to the disciples and said to them: All authority in heaven and on earth is given to me.
Do and make followers of all people in the world. Baptize them in the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit
Teach them to obey everything I have commanded you. And I will be with you always, to the end of the world.

I had finished building the wall and had repaired every place where it was broken down, but we had not put in the doors of the gates. Some people told Sanballat, Tobiah and Geshem, the Arabian, and our other enemies this Nehemiah 6: 2 Then Sanballat and Geshem sent me a message and asked me to meet them in the village of the Ono Valley. I knew they wanted to hurt me.
I sent the messengers back to them and told them: I am doing an important work, and I cannot come. Why should I stop working? I cannot go away from here and come to you.
They sent me the same message 4 times and I gave them the same answer each time.
Sanballat then sent the same message for the 5th time, but this time the letter was open, and any person could have read it.

The letter said: There is a rumor going around. People are talking about it everywhere. And Geshem says it is true. People are saying that you and the Jews are planning to turn rebel against the king. This is why you are building the wall of Jerusalem. People are also saying that you will be the new king of the Jews.

And the rumor is that you have chosen prophets to announce this about you in Jerusalem: There is a king in Judah! Now I warn you, Nehemiah, King Artaxerxes will hear about this. So come, let’s meet and talk about this together.
I told him: What you are saying is not true. You have made up this story yourself
Yes, they wanted to make all of us afraid. They thought that we would be discouraged and would stop working. So, I prayed and asked God to make me strong.

One day I went to the house of a man named Shemaiah son of Delaiah. Delaiah was the son of Mehetabel. Shemaiah had to stay in his house. He said, Nehemiah, let’s meet in God’s Temple. Let’s go inside the Holy Place and lock the doors. Men are coming to kill you. Tonight, they are coming to kill you.
But I said to Shemaiah, should a man like me run away? You know that an ordinary man like me cannot go into the Holy Place without being put to death. I will not go
I knew that God had not sent Shemaiah. I knew that he had prophesied against me because Tobiah and Sanballat had paid him to do that
They hired Shemaiah to scare me and make me sin by going into that part of the Temple. They were planning those bad things against me so that they could shame me.
My God, please remember Tobiah and Sanballat and the bad things they have done. Also remember the woman prophet Noadiah and the other prophets who have been trying to scare me.
We finished building the wall on the 25th day of the month of Elul. We worked for 52 days.

Then all our enemies heard that we had completed the wall, and all the nations around us saw that it was finished. So they lost their courage, because they understood that this work had been done with the help of our God.
Also in those days after the wall had been completed, the rich people of Judah were sending many letters to Tobiah, and he was answering their letters.

They sent those letters because many people in Judah had promised to be loyal to him. The reason for this is that Tobiah was son-in-law to Shecaniah son of Arah. And Tobiah’s son Jehohanan had married the daughter of Meshullam. Meshullam is the son of Berekiah.

And in the past, those people had made a special promise to Tobiah. So they kept telling me how good Tobiah was. And they kept telling Tobiah what I was doing. Tobiah kept sending me letters to make me afraid.
And the Lord told Moses,
You must send people to go and look at the land of Canaan, the land that I gave to the Israelites. Send one person from each tribe, one of the leaders of the tribe.
Moses obeyed the command of the Lord and then sent people from the Paran Desert. They were all leaders of Israel
These are their names: from the tribe of Reuben, Shammua son of Zaccur;
From the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat son of Hori;
From the tribe of Judah, Caleb son of Jephunneh;
From the tribe of Issachar, Igal son of Joseph;
From the tribe of Ephraim, Oshea son of Nun;
From the tribe of Benjamin. Palti son of Raphu;
From the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel son of Sodi;
From the tribe of Manasseh (a tribe from Joseph), Gaddi son of Susi;
From the tribe of Dan, Ammiel son of Gemalli;
From the tribe of Asher, Sethur son of Michael;
From the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi son of Vophsi;
From the tribe of Gad, Geuel son of Maki.
These are the names of the men Moses sent to look at and study the land. (Moses called Hoshea son of Nun by another name. Moses called him Joshua.)
When Moses was sending them out to explore Canaan, he said, Go to the south into the hill country.
See what the land looks like. Learn about the people who live there. If they be strong or are they weak? Are they few or are they many?
Learn about the land that they live in. Is it good land or bad land? What kind of towns do they live in? Or do they live in camps. Do the towns have walls protecting them?

And learn other things about the land. Is the soil good for growing things, or is it poor soil? Are there trees on the land? Try to bring back some of the fruit from that land.” (This was during the time when the first grapes should be ripe.)
So they went to explore the country. They explored the area from the desert of Zin to Rehob and Lebo Hamath.

They entered the country through the south and went to Hebron. (The town of Hebron was built seven years before the town of Zoan in Egypt.) Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai lived there. These men were descendants of Anak.

Then the men went to Eshcol Valley. There they cut off a branch from a grapevine that had a bunch of grapes on it. They put that branch on a pole, and two men carried it between them. They also carried some pomegranates and figs
That place is called the Eshcol Valley, because there the men of Israel cut off the bunch of grapes
The men explored that country for 40 days, and then they went back to the camp

They came to Moses and Aaron and all the Israelites who came together at the town of Kadesh in the Paran Desert. The men told them what they had seen and showed them what the fruit of the land looked like.
They said to Moses: We went to the land that you sent us to. This is a land with a lot of food, milk, and honey. Here is some of the fruit of the land.
But there is a problem: The people who live in the land are strong. Their cities are very large and strongly defended. We even saw some Anakites there.
The Amalekites live in the south. The Hittites, Jebusites, and Amorites live in the hill country. The Canaanites live near the sea and by the Jordan River.

Caleb told the people near Moses to be quiet. Then Caleb said, we should go up and take that land for ourselves. We can easily take that land. Numbers 13: 31 But the men who had gone with him said, we cannot fight against these people! They are much stronger than we are.

Then the other men said bad things to the Israelites about the land that they had seen. They said: we went through the land to look at everything. All the people that we saw there are very big. They will kill anyone who tries to attack them.
We saw giants (The descendants of Anak.) We felt like little grasshoppers. Yes, we were like grasshoppers to them!
That night all the Israelites in the camp began shouting loudly and crying.
The Israelites complained against Moses and Aaron and they said to them: We wish we had died in Egypt or in the desert.
Why did the Lord bring us to this land to die here in war? The enemies will take our wives and children. It will be better for us if we go back to Egypt.
They said to each other: Let us choose a new leader and go back to Egypt.
Moses and Aaron fell down with their faces to the ground in front of all the Israelites.
Joshua, son of Nun, and Caleb, son of Jephunneh, were 2 of the men who had gone to see what the land was like. They tore their clothes
They said to the Israelites: We went through the land to see what the land was like, and we are telling you that this is a very good land.
If the Lord is pleased with us, He will bring us into this land and He will give it to us. This is land with a lot of food, milk and honey.

But you must not rebel against the LORD and you must not be afraid of the people in the land. We will easily destroy them. They have no protection, nothing to keep them safe. But we have the Lord with us, so don’t be afraid!
All of the Israelites began talking about killing Joshua and Caleb with stones. But then all of the Israelites saw the Glory of the Lord appeared over the meeting tent.
The Lord said to Moses: How long will these people continue to make me angry? They show that they don’t trust me or believe in my power, I have done many miracles for them.
I will send a disease and kill all of them. I will destroy them, and I will use you to make another nation. Your nation will be greater and stronger than these people.
Then Moses said to the Lord: If you do that, the Egyptians will hear about it. (They know that you used your great power to bring your people out of Egypt.)

The people of Egypt will talk to the people who live in this land. They will tell them that You, the Lord, were with your people and that You appeared to them. They will tell them that You led them with a cloud above them in the day and with fire at night.
If you kill your people now, then all the people will say:
The Lord was not able to bring them into the land he promised them. So, he killed them in the desert.
Lord, show your strength! Show it the way you said you would.

The Lord is slow to become angry. He is full of great love. He forgives those who are guilty and break the law. But he always punishes those who are guilty. He punishes them, and he also punishes their children, their grandchildren, and even their great-grandchildren.
Now, show your great love to these people. Forgive their sin. Forgive them the same way you have been forgiving them since the time they left Egypt until now.
The Lord said to Moses: I will forgive them because you have asked Me.
But I tell you the truth. As I live, the Glory of the Lord will fill the whole earth.
These people have saw my glory and the great signs that I did in Egypt and in the desert. But they disobeyed me and tested me ten times.
That is why none of them will see the land that I have promised their grandfathers. Not one of the people who have turned away from Me and did not want to trust Me will go into the land.
But my servant Caleb is different. He follows me completely. So, I will bring him into the land that he has already seen, and his people will get that land.
(The Amalekite people and the Canaanites live in the valley.) So tomorrow you must leave this place. Go back to the desert on the road to the Red Sea.
The Lord talked to Moses and Aaron and He said:
How long will these evil people continue to complain against me? I have heard their complaints.
So tell them, The Lord said that as HE surely lives that he will surely do all those things to you that you complained about. This is what will happen to you:
Your dead bodies will lie in the sand of this desert, all of you whose names are written in the book, all who are older than 20 years, all who have accused Me.
I promised that you would live in that land, but now you will not go there. Only Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun, will go into the land.
You were afraid and complained that your enemies in that new land would take your children away from you. I tell you; I will bring them into the land. They will enjoy what you refused to accept.
Your dead bodies will lie in this desert,
Your children will wander around like shepherds here in the desert for 40 years. They will suffer because you were not faithful to me. They must suffer until all of you lie dead in the desert.

For 40 years you will suffer for your sins. (That is one year for each of the 40 days that the men explored the land.) You will suffer for 40 years for your sin, and you will know that I am angry with you and break my promise.
I, the Lord, have spoken. I will do this to these sinful people who have rebelled against Me. In this desert. You will all die.
The men Moses sent to explore the new land were the ones who came back and made all the Israelites complaining against Moses by bringing out a bad report about the land.
The men who had told those bad things to the other people all died of the disease that the LORD sent.
Joshua, son of Nun, and Caleb, son of Jephunneh, had also gone with the men to look at the land, but they did not die. They kept on living.
When Moses told the Israelites everything that the Lord had said, the people were very sad and cried.
They got up early the next morning and went to the top of the mountain. They said: We have sinned. We will go to the place that the Lord promised us.
Moses said to them: Why are you not obeying the Lord’s command? You will not be successful!
Don’t go, because the Lord is not with you. If you go into the land, the enemies will defeat you.
The Amalekite people and the Canaanites were there before you. You will die in war because you have turned away from the Lord. He will not be with you, and He will not help you.
But the people did not believe Moses. They went toward the high hill country. But Moses and covenant box of the Lord did not go with the people.
The Amalekite people and the Canaanites who lived in those mountains came down and defeated the Israelites. They chased the Israelites who ran away and fled to as far as the town of Hormah.
Now Korah, the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, and Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab, and On, the son of Peleth, sons of Reuben, took men:
And they rose up against Moses. With them were 250 Israelite men, well-known community leaders, famous in the church and some mighty men.

They came together to rebel against Moses and Aaron and said to them: ‘You want to do everything yourselves. To make sure everyone in the nation is holy and the Lord is with them. Why do you think that you are better then the people of Lord?
When Moses heard this, he bowed down and lay with his face to the ground.

Then Moses said to Korah and all his followers, “Tomorrow morning the Lord will show who belongs to him. He will show who is holy, and he will bring that man near to him. He will choose him and bring that man near to him.
Korah, you and all your followers must prepare your firepan for offering.
Tomorrow put fire and incense in some pans. Then bring those pans before the Lord. The man whom the LORD chooses shall be the one who is holy. You have gone far enough, you sons of Levi.
Moses also said to Korah, “You Levites, listen to me.

The God of Israel has chosen you from all the Israelites to be his special people, to serve Him in the tent of the Lord for the people of God. This is a very important work; don’t you think so? Numbers 16: 10 He brought you Levites near to him to help the priests, but now you are trying to become priests also. Numbers 16: 11 You and your followers have joined together and turned against the Lord! Did Aaron do anything wrong? No, so why are you complaining against Aaron?”
Then Moses called Korah, Dathan and Abiram, sons of Eliab, to come to him, but they said: ‘We will not come.
Is it not enough that you brought us out of Egypt, a land flowing with milk and honey, you brought us to the desert to kill us. And now you want to show that you have even more power over us.

Why should we follow you? You did not bring us into the new land filled with many good things. You did not give us the land God promised. You did not give us the fields or the vineyards. Are you trying to fool these men? No! We will not come.
Moses became very angry. He said to the Lord: ‘Please do not accept the offerings that they want to give You. I have taken nothing from them, and I have not done anything wrong to them.’
Then Moses said to Korah: ‘Tomorrow you and Aaron and all the people with you will stand before the Lord.
Each of you must have a pan with incense in it to give to the Lord. Each of you must have your own pan. There will be 250 pans and you and Aaron must each have a pan.’
The next morning everyone took his pan with coals and put incense on it. They went to the entrance of the tent of the Lord, and they stood there, with Moses and Aaron.
Korah also gathered all the people together at the entrance of the tabernacle. Then the Glory of the Lord appeared to everyone there.20Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
Then the Lord said to Moses and Aaron:
Go away from these people. I want to kill them all immediately.
But Moses and Aaron bowed down and fell with their faces to the ground and said: ‘O God, you give life to everyone. It is only a few who have sinned. Why are You angry with all your people?’
Then the Lord said to Moses:
Tell the people to go away from Korah, Dathan and Abiram’s tents.
Moses went to Dathan and Abiram and the leaders of Israel went with him.

Moses warned the people and said: Please, go away from the tents of these bad people, and do not touch anything that belongs to them, so that you will not be destroyed because of the sin they have done.
The people went away from the tents of Korah, Dathan and Abiram. Dathan and Abiram came and stood at the doors of their tents with their wives and children and their other people.
Then Moses said: ‘Now you will know that the Lord has sent me to do all these things, and you will know that it was not I who decided them.
These men will die, but if they die in a normal way the way people always die then that will show that the Lord did not really send me.

But if the Lord makes something happen that is different, if the earth opens its mouth and swallows them with everything they have, if they go down alive into the underworld, then you will know that these people have sinned and turned away from God.’
When Moses finished saying these things, the ground under the men opened.
The earth opened its mouth and swallowed Korah and all the people with him and their families and everything that belonged to them.
They went down into their grave alive. Everything they owned went with them. Then the earth closed over them. They disappeared from the people of God.
The Israelites heard the cries of the men being destroyed. So, they all ran in different directions and said, the earth will swallow us too!
Then a fire came from the Lord and destroyed the 250 men who were offering the incense.
After so many Israelites had died, the Lord talked to Moses and Eleazar, the priest, son of Aaron, and He said:
You must count all the Israelites, everyone who is 20 years old or older, and anyone who can go to war. You must also write down from what families they come.
Then Moses and Eleazar, the priest, talked to the Israelites in the Moab Flatlands next to the Jordan River near the city of Jericho. They told them
List all the men of Israel 20 years old and older, just as the LORD commanded Moses. This is the record of all the families of Israel who came out of Egypt.
Reuben was the oldest son of Israel. The families in the Reuben tribe were Hanok, Pallu,
6 Hezron from the Hezronite family group; Carmi from the Carmite family.
There were 43,730 people in the families of the Reuben tribe.
The son of Pallu was Eliab,

The sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan and Abiram. The people had chosen Dathan and Abiram to be leaders. They were the people who rebelled against Moses and Aaron with the group of Korah. They were also rebellious against the Lord.
Then the earth opened and swallowed them with Korah. The Korah group died when 250 of their men were burnt. They became a sign to the other people,
But the children of Korah did not die.
The families in the Simeon tribe were: Nemuel, Jamin, Jakin,
Zerah from the Zerahite family; Shaul from the Shaulite family.
There were 22,200 people in the families of the Simeon tribe.
The families in the Gad tribe were: Zephon, Haggi, Shuni,
Ozni from the Oznite family; Eri from the Erite family;
Arodi from the Arodite family; Areli from the Arelite family.
There were 40,500 people in the families of the Gad tribe.
Er and Onan were sons of Judah, but they died in Canaan.
The families of the Judah tribe were: Shelah, Perez and Zerah.
The families of the descendants of Perez were Hezron and Hamul.
There were 76,500 people in the families of the Judah tribe.
The families of the Issachar tribe were Tola, Puah,
Jashub from the Jashubite family; Shimron from the Shimronite family.
There were 64,300 people in the families of the Issachar tribe.
The families of the Zebulun tribe were: Sered, Elon and Jahleel.
There were 60,500 people in the families of the Zebulun tribe.
Joseph was the forefather of the families of Manasseh and Ephraim.
The first family in the Manasseh tribe was Makir and the people of Makir were the forefathers of the Gilead families.
The Gilead families were: Gilead, Iezer, Helek,
Asriel from the Asrielite family; Shechem from the Shechemite family;
Shemida from the Shemidaite family; Hepher from the Hepherite family.
Zelophehad, son of Hepher, had no sons. He had only daughters. The names of the daughters were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milkah and Tirzah.
There were 52,700 people in the families of Manasseh. 35The families of Ephraim were Shuthelah, Beker and Tahan.
Shuthelah was also the forefather of the Eran family.
There were 32,500 people in the families of the Ephraim tribe. These were all the families who were descendants of Joseph.
The families of the Benjamin tribe were: Bela, Ashbel, Ahiram,
Shupham from the Shuphamite family; Hupham from the Huphamite family.
Bela was the forefather of the Ard and Naaman families.
There were 45,600 people in the families of the Benjamin tribe.
The Shuham family was the only family of the Dan tribe.
There were 64,400 people in the family of Shuham.
The families of the Asher tribe were Imnah, Ishvi and Beriah.
Beriah was the forefather of the families of Heber and Malkiel.
Asher also had a daughter whose name was Serah.
There were 53,400 people in the families of the Asher tribe.
The families of the Naphtali tribe were Jahzeel, Guni,
Jezer from the Jezerite family; Shillem from the Shillemite family.
There were 45,400 people in the families of the Naphtali tribe.
All of the Israelites together were 601,730.
The Lord said to Moses:
You must divide the land among these families. Each family must get as much land as the number of people in the family.
You must give a big family a bigger part of the land and a small family a smaller part of the land. Each family must get as much land as the number of people in the family.
But you must draw lots when you divide the land.
The lots will show what part of land each family must get, what land the big families must get and what land the small families must get.
The families in the Levi tribe were Gershon, Kohath and Merari.
There were also other Levi families. They were Libni, Hebron, Mahli, Mushi and Korah. Kohath was the father of Amram,
Amram married Jochebed. She was born in the land of Egypt, and she was a descendant of Levi. She and Amram had 3 children. They were Aaron, Moses and their sister Miriam.
Aaron was the father of Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar and Ithamar.
But Nadab and Abihu died when they sacrificed offerings to the Lord with fire that the Lord did not want.
There were 23,000 men in the Levi families who were one month old or older. Moses and Eleazar did not count them with the other Israelites because they did not get land with the other Israelites.
Moses and Eleazar the priest counted all these people. They counted the Israelites while they were in the Jordan Valley in Moab. This was near the Jordan River across from Jericho.
Many years before, in the desert of Sinai, Moses and Aaron the priest counted the Israelites. But all these people were dead. Not one of them was still alive,

The Lord had told those Israelites that they would all die in the desert, that was sure. Not one of those Israelites stayed alive. Only Caleb, son of Jephunneh, and Joshua, son of Nun, were still alive.
During the third year of King Jehoiakim’s reign in Judah, King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon came to Jerusalem and surrounded it.

The Lord allowed Nebuchadnezzar to defeat Jehoiakim king of Judah. Nebuchadnezzar took some valuable objects from the house of God and carried them to the land of Shinar. He put those things in the temple of his god.

Aspenas was the manager of the people who worked in King Nebuchadnezzar’s house. The king told Aspenas to bring a few of the young Israelite men to work for him, young men from the family of the king of Israel, and sons of important people.

King Nebuchadnezzar wanted only healthy handsome young boys who did not have any bruises, scars, or anything wrong with their bodies. He wanted them to be wise and knowledgeable in science. Also, he wanted them to be able to learn things quickly and easily to serve in his palace. He told Ashpenaz to teach these young men the language and writings of the Chaldeans.

King Nebuchadnezzar gave the young men a certain amount of food and wine every day. This was the same kind of food that he ate. He wanted them to be trained for three years. After that they would become servants of the king of Babylon.
Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah were 4 of these young men who came from the land of Judah.
Ashpenaz gave them Babylonian names. Daniel’s new name was Belteshazzar, Hananiah’s was Shadrach, Mishael’s was Meshach, and Azariah’s was Abednego.

Daniel made up his mind to eat and drink only what God had approved for his people to eat. And he asked the king’s chief official for permission not to eat the food and wine served in the royal palace.
God caused Ashpenaz, the man in charge of the officials, to be kind and loyal to Daniel.

But Ashpenaz told Daniel, I am afraid of my master, the king. He ordered me to give you this food and drink. If you don’t eat this food, you will begin to look weak and sick. You will look worse than other young men your age. The king will see this, and he will become angry with me. He might cut off my head.
Then Daniel talked to the guard who had been put in charge of Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah.
He said, please give us this test for ten days: Don’t give us anything but vegetables to eat and water to drink.
Then after ten days, compare us with the other young men who eat the king’s food. See for yourself who looks healthier, and then decide how you want to treat us, your servants.
He agreed to let them try it for ten days.
After ten days, Daniel and his friends looked healthier than all the young men who ate the king’s food.
So, the guard continued to take away the king’s special food and wine and to give only vegetables to Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah.
God gave these four young men the wisdom and ability to learn many different kinds of writing and science. Daniel could also understand all kinds of visions and dreams.
Now at the end of the three years, Ashpenaz brought them in before Nebuchadnezzar.
The king talked with them all, and Daniel, Hananiah, Mishael, and Azariah impressed him more than any of the others. So, they became members of the king’s court.
In the 2nd year after Nebuchadnezzar had become king, he had dreams. He woke up and became worried and he could not sleep again.
So the king called the magicians, future tells, the enchanters, and the Chaldeans to come to him so that they could tell him what he had dreamed. They all came, and they stood in front of the king.
The king said to them: ‘I have dreamed, and I am worried about what I have dreamed. I want to know what this dream means.
Then the Chaldeans talked in the Syriack language and said to the king: We hope the king will live forever. Tell us what you have dreamed, and we will tell you what it means.

But the king said to the Chaldeans: No, I have decided that you must tell me what I have dreamed and what it means. If you cannot do it, then my men will kill you and cut you into pieces and break down your homes.
Again they said: ‘Your Majesty, you must first tell us what you have dreamed, then we will say what it means.’
Then the king told them: ‘I know what you want to do. You want to have some more time to think about what you must do. You know that I have already decided.

Do you know that you will be punished if you don’t tell me about my dream. So, you have all agreed to lie to me. You are hoping for more time so that I will forget what I want you to do. Now tell me the dream. If you can tell me the dream, I will know that you can tell me what it really means.

The Chaldeans said to the king: ‘There is no one in the world that can do what Your Majesty wants. There is no other king who has asked something like this of any magician or fortune-teller or Chaldeans.
Your Majesty, you want us to do something that we cannot do. No one else can do it. Only the gods can do it and they do not live here with people on earth.’
The king then became very angry, and he told his soldiers to kill all the wise men in Babylon.

When the king told the soldiers to kill all the wise men, they looked for Daniel and his friends to kill them. Daniel 2: 14 Arioch was the leader of the soldiers who guarded the king, and the king told him to kill all the wise men of Babylon. Arioch went to do it but then Daniel talked to him very wisely.
Daniel asked Arioch: Why did the king become angry and decide to kill wise men? Arioch told Daniel what had happened. Arioch told Daniel what had happened.
Then Daniel went to the king and asked the king to give him some time. Daniel said he would tell the king what his dream meant.
Daniel went home, and he told his friends Hananiah, Mishael and Azariah what had happened.

He they prayed to the God who rules from heaven and asked Him to be good to them and to tell him what the king had dreamed. Then the king’s men would not kill Daniel and his friends with the other wise men of Babylon.
That night the God of heaven showed Daniel what the king had dreamed, and Daniel praised God.
Daniel said: ‘Praise the name of God always because He knows everything, and He is very strong. Danie
Daniel 2;29
Then King Nebuchadnezzar bowed low to the ground in front of Daniel and showed respect to him. The king told his people to bring a grain-offering and an incense-offering to Daniel.
The king said to Daniel: Surely your God is stronger than all the other gods and all the kings. He told you things that no one else knows. That is why you could tell me what I had dreamed.
The king then made Daniel a very important man and he gave Daniel a lot of gifts. He told Daniel to rule over the land of Babylon and to be the leader of all the wise men in Babylon.
Daniel asked the king to choose Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego to help him rule over Babylon and the king did that. From that day Daniel stayed with the king in his palace.
King Nebuchadnezzar made a big statue. It was made of gold. It was 60 cubits high and 6 cubits wide. He put the statue upright in the Dura Valley in the land of Babylon
Then King Nebuchadnezzar called all the important officials in the land to be there when they dedicated the statue that King Nebuchadnezzar had made and put upright.
All the important officials in the land came to be there when they dedicated the statue. They all stood in front of the statue that the king had made.
Then one of the king’s officials talked very loudly and said: ‘All of you who come from other lands and speak different languages, you must listen to what the king says.
When they start to play music, you must bow down to the golden statue that Nebuchadnezzar has made, and you must worship the statue.
My men will take each person who does not bow down and immediately throw him into a big fire.

All the people who came from other lands and who talked different languages did what the king had said. Every time they started to play music, the people bowed down and worshipped the golden statue that Nebuchadnezzar had made.
But one day some of the Chaldreans came to the king and complained about the Jews
The wise men told King Nebuchadnezzar: O king, we hope you will live forever.
You said that everyone must bow down and worship the golden statue when the music plays,
And you also said that whoever does not bow down and worship the gold statue will be thrown into a very hot furnace.

There are some Jews who you made important officials in the province of Babylon that ignored your order, King. Their names are Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego. They don’t worship your gods, and they didn’t bow down to worship the gold statue you set up.
Then he said to them: ‘Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego, is it true that you do not want to serve my god? Is it true that you do not want to bow down and worship the statue that I have made?

Now, if the music plays and you bow down and worship the golden statue that I have made, it will be good, and there will be no problem. But if you don’t bow down, my men will take you immediately and throw you in the fire. I will do it to you. There is no God who can stop me. Now, if the music plays and you bow down and worship the golden statue that I have made, it will be good, and there will be no problem. But if you don’t bow down, my men will take you immediately and throw you in the fire. I will do it to you. There is no God who can stop me.
Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego said to King Nebuchadnezzar: we will not try to “talk our way out of” this situation
Our God can save us. We serve Him and He will keep us from burning in the fire.
Your Majesty, you must know that even if our God does not save us, we will not serve your god and we will not worship the statue that you have made.
Nebuchadnezzar became very angry with Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego. He told his men to make the fire 7 times hotter than it always was.
Then he told his mighty soldiers to tie Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego and to throw them into the fire.
Then the soldiers tied them up and threw them into the fire. Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego were still wearing all their clothes.
Then the king told them to make the fire hotter, and it was very hot. When the soldiers threw the 3 men into the fire, the flames burnt the soldiers and they died.
The 3 men, Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego, fell into the furnace. Their hands and feet were tied.
Then King Nebuchadnezzar jumped to his feet. He was very surprised, and he asked his advisors, we tied only three men, and we threw only three men into the fire. Is that right?
He answered and said, Lo, I see four men loose, walking in the midst of the fire, and they are not hurt; and the form of the fourth is like the Son of God.
Nebuchadnezzar went to the door of the big furnace where the fire was. He said: Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego, servants of the Highest God, come out. And they came out.

The important officials all came to look at them and they saw that the fire had not burnt the 3 men. It had not even burnt the hair on their heads. Their clothes were still the same and they did not smell like fire.

Then Nebuchadnezzar said: I praise the God of Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego. He sent his angel, and He saved his servants. They believed that He would help them, and they did not do what the king said. They thought it was better to die than to disobey their own God. They did not want to serve any other God.

That is why I say: All people in all the lands must honor the God of Shadrach, Meshach and Abednego. If someone says anything bad against their God, my soldiers will kill him and cut him into pieces and destroy his house. There is no other god who can save like this God.
King Nebuchadnezzar sent this letter to the many nations and language groups that lived around the world. He wrote: ‘I hope you are well
I am very happy to tell you about the miracles and wonderful things that the Highest God did for me.
His signs and miracles are great and wonderful. He will always be King and rule forever.
I, Nebuchadnezzar, was living in my palace safely and everything was going well.
But then I dreamed, and I saw something that made me become afraid.
So I gave an order that all the wise men of Babylon be brought to me to tell me what my dream meant.
When the men of magic and the Chaldeans came, I told them about the dream, but they would not tell me what it meant.
Finally, Daniel came to me. (I gave Daniel the name Belteshazzar to honor my god. The spirit of the holy gods is in him.) I told him about my dream.

I said: Belteshazzar, you are the leader of the wise men. I know that the holy gods are helping you and you know things that other people do not know. Tell me what my dream means. Tell me the meaning of the thing that I saw when I was dreaming.
These are the visions I saw while I was lying in my bed: I looked, and there in front of me was a tree standing in the middle of the earth. The tree was very tall.
The tree grew large and strong. The top of the tree touched the sky. It could be seen from anywhere on earth.

The leaves of the tree were beautiful. It had much good fruit on it. And on the tree was plenty of food for everyone. The wild animals found shelter under the tree, and the birds lived in its branches. Every animal ate from the tree.
I was looking at those things in the vision while lying on my bed. And then I saw a watcher, a holy angel coming down from heaven.

He spoke very loud, and said, ‘Cut down the tree, and cut off its branches. Strip off its leaves. Scatter its fruit around. The animals that are under the tree will run away. The birds that are in its branches will fly away.

But let the stump and roots stay in the ground. Put a band of iron and bronze around it. The stump and roots will stay in the field with the grass all around it. It will live among the wild animals and plants in the fields. It will become wet with dew.
He will not think like a man any longer. He will have the mind of an animal. Seven seasons will pass while he is like this.

That is what the holy angels said, and it will happen. Then everyone who lives will know that it is God, the Highest God, who decides who will be king. God can choose any person and make him king, even the most unimportant one.

Then Daniel (also called Belteshazzar) became very quiet for one hour. What he was thinking bothered him. So, the king said, “Belteshazzar, don’t let the dream or its meaning make you afraid.” Then Belteshazzar answered the king, My lord, I wish the dream were about your enemies, and I wish the meaning of the dream were about those against you
King Belshazzar gave a big party for 1000 of his officials. The king was drinking wine with them

Later King Belshazzar became drunk, and he told his servants to go and get the gold and silver cups that Nebuchadnezzar had taken from the temple of God in Jerusalem. The king wanted to drink wine from the cups with his wives, his concubines and his officials
The servants went to get the gold cups that King Nebuchadnezzar had taken from the temple of God in Jerusalem. King Belshazzar and his officials, his wives and his concubines drank from the cups.
They drank wine, and they praised their gods, the gods made of gold, silver, bronze, iron, wood and stone.
Suddenly, the fingers of a hand started to write on the wall of the king’s palace. The hand was near the lamp stand and the king saw the handwriting
The king became afraid. His face became pale. He could not stand up because his legs were too weak, and his body started to tremble.

He shouted and told them to bring the men of magic and the Chaldeans and the wise men. When the wise men came, he said: ‘If anyone can read what the hand has written on the wall and can tell what it means, I will give him clothes like a king’s clothes, purple clothes and a gold chain around his neck. He will be the 3rd most important man in the land.
The wise men all came into the room where the king was, but they could not read what was written on the wall and they could not say what it meant.
King Belshazzar became even more afraid, and his face became more pale. His officials did not know what to do.
When the queen heard what the king and his officials were talking about, she came into the room and said: ‘Your Majesty, I hope you will live forever. Do not be afraid or look so pale.

There is a man in your land who can help because the holy gods help him. King Nebuchadnezzar, who ruled before you, saw that this man was very wise and that he had wisdom like the gods. Then Nebuchadnezzar made him leader of all the wise men.

The man I am talking about is named Daniel. The king gave him the name Belteshazzar. He is very smart, and he knows many things. He could interpret dreams, explain secrets, and find the answer to very hard problems. Call for Daniel, he will tell you what the writing on the wall means.”

They called Daniel to come to the king and the king asked Daniel: ‘Are you Daniel, one of the people that Nebuchadnezzar took away from the land of Judah and brought here? Daniel 5: 14 They have told me that the gods help you and that you are very wise. Daniel 5: 15 I have called the wise men and clever people to come to read what is written on the wall and to tell me what it means, but they cannot tell me.

Then someone told me that you can tell what things mean and that you can help in any problem. If you can read what is written on the wall and tell me what it means, I will give you clothes like those of a king, purple clothes and a gold chain around your neck. You will be the 3rd most important man in the land.
Then Daniel said to the king: Your Majesty, you can keep your gifts, or you can give them to someone else. But I will read what is written on the wall and I will tell you what it means.
Nebuchadnezzar was king before you and you know that the Highest God made him a great and important king.

That is why the people of all nations were afraid of Nebuchadnezzar because he could kill anyone whom he wanted to kill. He could also let anyone live if he wanted that person to live. He could make people important, or he could humiliate them.
But Nebuchadnezzar became proud, and God decided that he would no longer be king to sit on the king’s throne. And the people did not respect him any longer.

They chased Nebuchadnezzar away. He became like an animal, and he went to live among the wild donkeys. He had to eat grass like an ox and the dew and rain fell on him. Nebuchadnezzar stayed there until he knew that it is God who decides who will be king.
But Belshazzar, you already knew this. But still you have not made yourself humble

You thought that you were more important than the Lord of heaven. You took the cups that Nebuchadnezzar had taken away from the Lord’s temple and you drank wine out of those cups with your officials, your wives and your concubines. And then you praised your gods that were made of silver, gold, bronze, iron, wood and stone. These are gods who cannot see or hear. They know nothing. But you did not want to honor God, the God who makes you breathe and who decides what will happen to you.
It is this God who sent the hand to write on the wall.
These are the words that are written on the wall: MENE, MENE, TEKEL, PARSIN.
MENE means: God has looked at you to see if you are a good king and He has decided that you will no longer be king.
TEKEL means: God has tested you and He has seen that you are not good enough to be king.
PERES means: God will give your land to other nations, to the people of Media and Persia.

Belshazzar then told his people to give Daniel clothes like those of a king, purple clothes and a gold chain around his neck. He told everyone that Daniel had become the 3rd most important man in the land.
That very same night, Belshazzar, king of the Babylonians, was killed.
A man named Darius the Mede became the new king. Darius was about 62 years old.
It pleased Darius to appoint 120 governors of the kingdom throughout the land
King Darius chose three men to rule over the 120 governors. Daniel was one of the three overseers that the king put these men in this position so that the king might not suffer loss.
Then Daniel excelled over all of them, because character was different than the others, and the King considered to set him up over all his kingdom.

The other 2 overseers and the governors tried to find grounds for charges against Daniel in his conduct of government affairs, but they were unable to do so. They could find no corruption in him, because he was faithful, always responsible, and completely trustworthy.
Finally, they said, we will never find any reason to accuse Daniel of doing something wrong. So, we must find something to complain about that is connected to the law of his God.
The 2 other overseers and the governors quickly went to the king and said: Your Majesty, we hope you will live forever.

The overseers, the governors, advisors, and the captains have all agreed on something. We think that the king should make this law and that everyone must obey it: For the next 30 days, whoever prays to any god or man except you, King, will be thrown into the lions’ den.
Now, King, make the law and sign the paper so that it cannot be changed, because the laws of the Medes and Persians cannot be canceled or changed.”
So King Darius made the law and signed it.

They told Daniel about this new law, but he went into his house, and he knelt down and prayed 3 times every day, and he praised God as he always did. He prayed in front of the open windows of his room on the roof. The windows were open towards Jerusalem.
The other officials came together, and they saw Daniel praying to his God. asking God for help

They went to the king and said to him: ‘Your Majesty, you have made a law that says: “All people may pray only to you for 30 days, and if anyone prays to another man or god during these 30 days, then you will throw him to the lions. The king said: ‘Yes, it is a law of the Medes and Persians. No one can change that law.

Then they said to the king, that man Daniel is not paying any attention to you. He is one of the captives from Judah, and he is not paying attention to the law you signed. Daniel still prays to his God three times every day.
The king became very sad and upset when he heard this. He decided to save Daniel. He worked until sunset trying to think of a way to save him.
But those officials went to the king and said to him: King, you know that according to the law of the Medes and the Persians, no law that the king signs can be changed.
So King Darius gave the order. They brought Daniel and threw him into the lions’ den. The king said to Daniel, “May the God you serve continually, he will save you!

A big rock was brought and put over the opening of the lions’ den. Then the king used his ring and put his seal on the rock. He also used the rings of his officials and put their seals on the rock. This showed that no one could move that rock and bring Daniel out of the lion’s den. Daniel 6: 18 Then King Darius went back to his house. He did not eat that night. He did not want anyone to come and entertain him. He could not sleep all night.
The next morning, King Darius got up just as it was getting light and ran to the lions’ den
When he got to the lions’ den, he called to Daniel. He said, “Daniel, servant of the living God, has your God who you always serve been able to save you from the lions?
Daniel answered, King, live forever.
My God sent his angel to save me. The angel closed the lions’ mouths. The lions have not hurt me because my God knows I am innocent. I never did anything wrong to you, King.

King Darius was very happy. He told his servants to lift Daniel out of the lions’ den. And when Daniel was lifted out of the den, they did not find any injury on his body. The lions did not hurt Daniel because he trusted in his God.

Then the king gave a command to bring the men who had accused Daniel to the lions’ den. The men and their wives and children were thrown into the lions’ den. The lions grabbed them before they hit the floor. The lions ate their bodies and then chewed on their bones.
Then King Darius wrote this letter to all the people from other nations and language groups all around the world: He wrote, I hope you all are very well.
I now make a new law that everyone in my kingdom must honor and fear the God of Daniel because He is the living God, and He will live forever. He will always be King and rule forever.
He saves people and makes them free. He gives signs and does miracles in heaven and on earth. He saved Daniel from the lions so that they could not kill him.
All went well with Daniel while Darius was king and when Cyrus became king. Cyrus was the man from the land of Persia.
These are the proverbs of Solomon, son of David, King of Israel.
Proverbs will help you learn to be wise, to accept correction, and to understand wise sayings.
Proverbs will teach you to develop your mind in the right way. You will learn to do what is right and to be honest and fair
Proverbs teach people who know very little to be wise. They will teach young people what they need to know and how to use what they have learned
Even the wise can become wiser by listening to these proverbs. They will gain understanding from others to learn how to solve difficult problems.
Better to be poor and honest than to be a liar and a fool.
I is not good for a person to be without knowledge. If you hurry and do something too quickly, you will make mistakes.
People destroy their lives when they do foolish things, then they get angry and blame the Lord and say it is his fault.
When someone is rich, they have a lot of friends, but if he becomes poor, he has no friends.
A false witness will be punished, and someone who tells lies will not escape.
Everyone wants to be friends with an important person. They also want to be friends with someone who gives away gifts. Provers
A poor man’s brothers do not like him, and his friends will avoid him even more. When he really needs them, they stay away from him. there are no one there to help him.
To obtain wisdom is to love yourself; those who do what they have learn shall do good
A false witness will be punished, and someone who tells lies will not escape.
It is not normal for a fool to be rich or for a slave to rule over princes!
A wise man does not become angry quickly. He always wants to forgive the mistakes of other people.
The shouts of an angry king are like a roaring lion, but his kind words are like the dew on the grass.
A foolish child brings a lot of trouble to his father and a complaining wife is like the constant dripping of water.
14 People receive houses and money from their parents, but a good wise wife is a gift from the LORD.
A lazy people wants to sleep all day, and someone who will not work will not have any food to eat.
People who obey the commandments of the LORD will live. But he that ignores God’s ways shall die.
Whoever is kind to the poor lends to the LORD, and he will reward them for what they have done.
Discipline your children while there is still hope. Avoiding it because of their crying will end badly for them.
A person who gets angry quickly will always have trouble. If you help him, you will have to help him again and again.
If you listen to what people teach you and you learn from them, you will have wisdom in the end.
People make a lot of plans about what they want to do, but the advice of the LORD will endure.
The most important thing that you want from other people is for them to kind. It is better to be poor than to be a liar.
The fear the LORD and you will have a good life, one that is satisfying and protected during trouble.
A lazy man puts his hand in the bowl to take food, but he is too lazy to bring back his hand to his mouth.
If you punish a mocker, the simpleminded will learn a lesson; if you correct the wise, they will be all the wiser.
Children who would steal from their father and chase away their mother are a child that causes shame and bring disappointment.
My son, if you stop learning, you will turn your back on knowledge.
Using a criminal as a witness makes a joke of justice. Sinful people love to do things that are wrong.
Judgments are prepared for mockers, and stripes for the back of fools.”
Wine is deceiving, and alochal causes fighting; everyone led astray by them lacks wisdom.
People should be afraid of an angry king, like they are of a lion that roars. If you make the king angry, you can lose your life.
It is a honor for people to stop from strife: Any fool can start an argument.
Because of the cold a lazy person will not prepare their fields: then in the summer they shall beg for food, and no one shall given to them.
Getting information from someone can be like getting water from a deep well. If you are wise, you can draw it out.
There are many people who say: ‘You can trust me.’ But there are only a few of them that do what they promise.
Good people live in the right way and are honest. God blesses their children who live like they do.
When a king sits on his throne to judge people, he separates out all evil with his eyes.
No one can say: Who can say, “I have made my heart clean, have never done anything sinful.’
The LORD hates people that use the wrong weights and measures to cheat others.
Even children show what they are like by the things they do. You can see if their actions are pure and right.
The Lord has given some people ears to hear and some people eyes to see.
Do not love excessive sleep, or you will become poor; Open your eyes so that you can do your work and you will be satisfied with bread.
When a person buying something, they always say, “It’s no good. It costs too much.” Then they go away and tell others what a good deal they got.
There is gold, and an abundance of jewels; But the words of someone who is wise are even more precious.
Take the garment of anyone who puts up collateral for a stranger; hold it in pledge if he does it for an unfamiliar woman.
If you have cheated someone to get food, it will taste good in the beginning but in the end, The guilt and consequence will be bitter.
Get good advice before you make your plans. Before you start a war, ask for the advice of wise advisors.
A gossip goes around telling secrets. Don’t be friends with someone who does not care what he says.
Those who would curse their father or mother are like a lamp that goes out in the darkest night.
If someone gets money that he inherited too quickly, when he is too young, It will not be a blessing in the end.
Don’t say, “I will get even for this wrong.” Wait for the LORD to handle the matter.
The Lord hates people who cheat when they weigh things and who are dishonest
No person knows what will happen to him. It is the Lord who decides what will happen to us.
Think carefully before you promise to give something to God. Later, you might wish you had not made that promise.
A king who is wise can find out who is guilty and punish those who have done wrong.
The LORD gave us our mind and conscience; we cannot hide our thoughts and doings from Him.
If a king does what he has promised and he is faithful to his people, but they must be merciful for their kingdoms to last.
We admire a young man for his strength, but we respect an old man for his gray hair.
Sometimes it takes a painful experience to make us change our ways.
These are the wise sayings that King Lemuel’s mother taught him:
My child, I am your mother. I prayed to God and He gave you to me.
Don’t waste your strength or your life on women. Not give your efforts on things that destroy kings.
Lemuel, kings should not drink wine, Or for rulers to desire strong drink.
If they drink too much, they will not remember the laws and will not be able to judge the people in trouble rightly.
