Alcohol is for the dying, and wine for those of bitter of heart
Let them drink and forget their troubles. Let them forget about all their suffering.
Speak up for people who cannot speak for themselves. Help people who are in trouble.
Speak for them and be fair when you judge, and protect the people who can not help themselves and who have nothing.
A wife of honest character is hard to find? She is worth far more than rubies.
Her husband trust her. He will never have to steal because he is poor
She does good for her husband all her life, and she never brings him trouble.
She gets wool and flax and enjoys making things with her hands.
She is like the merchants’ ships; she bringeth her food from afar.
She gets up while it is dark and she prepares food for her family and for the servants who work for her.

She looks carefully at a field and buys it and she plants the vineyard Proverbs 31: 17 Her arms are strong, and she keeps on working. Proverbs 31: 18 When she sees people wanting to buy the things that she makes, she works all night.
She makes thread with her hands, and she weaves at the loom and makes clothes.
She loves giving to poor people and those who can not protect themselves and who have nothing.
She does not worry about her family when it snows because they all have warm clothes.
She makes blankets and her clothes are made of linen and purple wool.
Everyone knows her husband. He sits with the leaders of the land at the gate.
She makes clothes and sells them, and she gives the merchants sashes to sell.
She is a strong and a neat woman, and people honour her. She is not worried about what will happen.
She always speaks words of wisdom, and she teaches her people to be friendly.
She oversees the care of her house. She is never lazy. Proverbs 31: 28Her children respect and praise her, and her husband says she is a good wife. He says:
‘There are a lot of good women, but you are better than all of them.’
A woman does not always stay young and beautiful, but you should always praise a woman who serves and fears the Lord.
She must get the reward for her hard work, and everyone in the city must praise her.
My dear brothers and sisters, I love you and want to see you. You bring me joy and make me proud of you. Continue to be faithful to the Lord.
I plead with Euodia and I plead with Syntyche to agree in the Lord.

For this I make a special request to my friend who has served with me so faithfully: Help these women. They worked hard with me in telling people the gospel, together with Clement and others who worked with me. Their names are written in the book of life.
Always be filled with joy in the Lord. I will say it again. Be filled with joy.
Let your gentleness be known to all men, because the Lord is coming again soon.
Don’t worry about anything, but in everything by prayer and pleading with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God.
Then you will experience God’s peace, which exceeds anything we can understand. His peace will guard your hearts and minds as you live in Christ Jesus.

Brothers and sisters, think about the things that are true and honest, things that are right and pure, and things that are beautiful and good report. Yes, you must think of things that are good and beautiful. If there be any honor, if there be any praise think on these things.
The things you have learned and received from me. The things you have heard and seen me do. You must do the same, then God who gives peace will be with you.
I am very happy and thankful that you have shown again that you think of me. I know that you were thinking of me before, but you could not show it.
I am telling you this, but not because I need anything. I have learned to be happy with what I have.

I know how to live when I am poor and when I have plenty. I have learned the secret of how to live through any kind of situation—when I have enough to eat or when I am hungry, when I have everything I need or when I have nothing.
I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me
But you did the right thing when you helped me when I had trouble and needed help.

You in the congregation of Philippi remember when I first told the Good News there. When I left Macedonia, you were the only church that talked about giving me the help I need. Philippians 416 You also sent me things that I needed more than once when I was in Thessalonica.
Really, it is not that I want to get gifts from you. But I want you to have the benefit that comes from giving.

I have everything I need. I have even more than I need. I have all I need because Epaphroditus brought your gift to me. Your gift is like a sweet-smelling sacrifice offered to God. God accepts that sacrifice and it pleases him.
My God will use his riches to give you everything you need. He will do this through Christ Jesus.
Glory to our God and Father forever and ever. Amen.
Greetings to all the believers in the town of Philippi who belong to Christ Jesus. The friends who are here with me send their greetings.
All the believers here send greetings to you. The people who work for Caesar send special greetings.
I pray that the Lord Jesus Christ will be gracious to you all.
I, John, saw an angel coming down out of heaven. The angel had the key to the bottomless pit. The angel also held a large chain in his hand.
The angel grabbed the dragon. The dragon is the old snake, the devil, Satan. The angel tied the dragon with the chain for 1000 years.

The angel threw the dragon into the bottomless pit and closed it and locked it. The angel did this so that the dragon could not deceive the people of the nations for 1000 years. But after 1000 years, the dragon must become free and come out for a short time.

Then I, John, saw thrones and people sitting on the thrones. God said those people could judge. The people sitting on the thrones were the believers who had died. People had cut off their heads because they told other people about Jesus and the Word of God. They did not worship the beast and his idol and they did not have the mark of the beast on their foreheads or on their hands. God made them live and they ruled with Christ for 1000 years.
This is the first raising of dead people. God did not make the other dead people live again. He will make them live again after 1 000 years.
Great blessings belong to those who share in this first resurrection. The second death has no power over them. They will be priests for God and for Christ. They will rule with him for 1000 years.
When the 1 000 years are over, God will let Satan out of the jail where he was kept.

He will go out to trick the nations in all the earth, the nations known as Gog and Magog. Satan will gather the people for battle. There will be more people than anyone can count, like sand on the seashore.
I, John, saw Satan’s army march across the earth and gather around the camp of God’s people and the city that God loves. But fire came down from heaven and destroyed Satan’s army.
And the Devil, the one who tricked these people, was thrown into the lake of burning sulfur with the beast and the false prophet. There they would be tortured day and night forever and ever.
Then I saw a big white throne and I saw God sitting on the throne. Earth and sky ran away from him and disappeared

And I saw those who had died, All of them, old people and children, there were standing before God. The books in heaven were opened. And another book was opened, the book of life. God read the things that each person had done. Then God judged them.
The people who died in the sea and the people who died and went to hell stood before God. God then looked in the book and read what they had done, and God judged them.
And Death and Hell were thrown into the lake of fire. This lake of fire is the second death.
If any person’s name was not written in the book of life, then God threw him into the sea of fire.
Brothers and sisters, I know you all understand the Law of Moses. So surely you know that the law rules over people only while they are alive.
The laws say that a woman who is married is her husband’s wife for as long as he is alive. But if her husband dies, then she is free. That what the laws say.

The laws say she commits adultery if she marries another man while her husband lives. But if her husband dies, she is free from the laws. If she marries another man then, she does not commit adultery.

In the same way, my brothers and sisters, your old selves died and you became free from the law through the body of Christ. Now you belong to someone else. You belong to the one who was raised from death. We belong to Christ so that we can be used in service to God
In the past we were ruled by our sinful selves. The law made us want to do sinful things. And those sinful desires controlled our bodies, so that what we did only brought us death.
In the past the law held us as prisoners, but our old selves died, and we were made free from the law. So now we serve God in a new way, not in the old way, with the written rules.

Some people say: Is God’s laws are sin? No, it is wrong to say that. I know God’s laws and that is why I know what sin is. For example, God’s laws say: You must not want something that belongs to someone else.
But sin uses these laws and makes me feel like I want to have it. If there were no laws, then sin could not make me want to have those things that are wrong.
I was once alive without knowledge of the Law; but when I understood the meaning of the commandment, sin became alive and I died since the Law sentenced me to death
God gave the laws to make me alive, but what happened was that those laws brought me death.
Sin found a way to fool me by using the command to make me die.
Now the law is holy, and the command is holy and right and good.

Does this mean that something that is good brought death to me? No, it was sin that used the good command to bring me death. This shows how terrible sin really is. It can use a good command to produce a result that shows sin at its very worst.
We know that the law is spiritual, but I am not. I am so human. Sin rules me as if I were its slave.
I do not understand what I am doing. I don’t do the good I want to do, and I do the evil I hate.
And if I don’t want to do what I do, that means I agree that the law is good.
But I am not really the one doing the evil. It is sin living in me that does it.
I know that there is nothing in me that is good. I am sinful. I want to do good things, but I don’t do them.
I don’t do the good that I want to do. I do the evil that I don’t want to do.
So if I do what I don’t want to do, then I am not really the one doing it. It is the sin living in me that does it.
So I have learned this rule: When I want to do good, evil is there with me
In my mind I am happy with God’s law
But I see another law working in my body. That law makes war against the law that my mind accepts. That other law working in my body is the law of sin, and that law makes me its prisoner.
What a miserable person I am! Who will save me from this body that brings me death?
I thank God for his salvation through Jesus Christ our Lord! So, in my mind I servant to God’s law, but in my sinful self I am a slave to the law of sin.
So now anyone who is in Christ Jesus is not judged guilty. Who not follow our sinful ways but follow the ways of the spirit.
It is because in Christ Jesus the law of the Spirit brings life and has made you free. The Spirit made you free from the law that brings sin and death

God did what his laws could not do. He saved us. The law was without power because it was made weak by our sinful flesh. But God sent his Son, and He became a Man, a human like the people who sin, and God punished Him for their sin. That is how God punished the sin which ruled over people.
God did this so that we could be right just as the law said we must be. Now we do not follow our sinful selves. We follow the Spirit.
People who follow their sinful selves think only about what they want. But those who follow the Spirit are thinking about what the Spirit wants them to do.
If you’re thinking is controlled by your sinful self, there is death. But if you’re thinking is controlled by the Spirit, there is life and peace.
Anyone whose thinking is controlled by their sinful self is against God. They refuse to obey God’s law. They are not able to obey it.
Those who are ruled by their sinful selves cannot please God.
But you are not ruled by your sinful selves. You are ruled by the Spirit, that Spirit of God really lives in you. But whoever does not have the Spirit of Christ does not belong to Christ.
Your bodies will die because sin. But if Christ lives in you, then the Spirit gives you life, because Christ made you right with God.

Jesus died, but the Spirit of God made Him live again. That same Spirit lives in your hearts and that is why He will also make you live again, you who have bodies that will die. The Spirit of God who lives in your heart will raise you as He raised Jesus.
So, my brothers and sisters, we have a responsibility not to be ruled by our sinful selves. We must not live the way our sinful selves want.
If you use live to do what your sinful selves want, you will die. But if you use the Spirit’s help to stop doing the wrong things you do with your body, you will have true life.
The true children of God are those who let God’s Spirit lead them.
The Spirit that we received is not a spirit that makes us slaves again to fear. The Spirit that we have makes us adopted children of God. And with that Spirit we cry out, Abba, Father.
And the Spirit himself speaks to our spirits and makes us sure that we are God’s children.

If we are God’s children, we will get the blessings God has for his people. He will give us all that he has given Christ. But we must suffer like Christ suffered. Then we will also be glorified with him.
We have to suffer now, But God will give us something much better, something wonderful, and we will see it.
Everything that God has made is waiting with excitement for the time when he will show the world who his children are.
Everything God made was allowed to become like something that cannot fulfill its purpose. That was not its choice, but God made it happen with this hope in view:
That the creation would be made free from ruin. That everything God made would have the same freedom and glory that belong to God’s children.
We know that everything God made has been waiting until now in pain like a woman ready to give birth to a child.

Not only the world, but we also have been waiting with pain inside us. We have the Spirit as the first part of God’s promise. So, we are waiting for God to finish making us his own children. I mean we are waiting for our bodies to be made free.
God has saved us, and He gave us this hope, to wait for something wonderful. If we can see what we are waiting for, that is not really hope. People don’t hope for something they already have.
But we are hoping for something we don’t have yet, and we are waiting for it patiently.

Also, the Spirit helps us. We are weak, but the Spirit helps us with our weakness. We don’t know how to pray as we should, but the Spirit himself speaks to God for us. He begs God for us, speaking to him with feelings too deep for words.
God already knows our deepest thoughts. And he understands what the Spirit is saying, because the Spirit speaks for his people in the way that agrees with what God wants.
The people who love God should know that everything that happens to them is for good. God makes it happen that way because that was his plan.
God knew them before he made the world. And he decided that they would be like his Son. Then Jesus would be the firstborn of many brothers and sisters.
God planned for them to be like his Son. God calls them to himself, and God made them right with him. And after he made them right, he gave them his glory.
So what should we say about this? If God is for us, no one can stand against us.
He even let his own Son suffer for us. God gave his Son for all of us. So now with Jesus, God will freely give us all things.
Who can accuse the people God? No one! God is the one who makes them right.
Who can say that God’s people are guilty? No one! Christ Jesus died for us, but that is not all. Jesus was also raised from death. And now he is at God’s right side, speaking to God for us.
Can anything separate us from Christ’s love? Can trouble or problems or persecution. Can no food or no clothes or face danger or even death?
As the scripture says, For your sake we are in danger of death at all times; we are treated like sheep that are going to be slaughtered.

No, in all these things we have complete victory through Jesus who loved us. Romans 8 38 For I am certain that nothing can separate us from his love: neither death nor life, neither angels nor other heavenly rulers or powers, neither the present nor the future,
Neither the world above nor the world below there is nothing in all creation that will ever be able to separate us from the love of God which is ours through Christ Jesus our Lord.

Brothers and sisters in Christ, I beg you, because of the great mercy God has shown us, offer your lives as a living sacrifice to God, an offering that is only for God and pleasing to him. Considering what he has done, it is only right that you should worship him in this way.
Do not be like the people of this world, but let God change you with a new way of thinking. Then you will know what the good, pleasing, and perfect will of God is.

God was very good to me and that is why I am saying to each of you: Do not think you are more important than you really are. You must be wise when you think of yourselves and remember it is God who tells each believer how he must serve Him.
All of us together are like a human body. A man has one body but his body has many parts and each part is doing something else.
It is the same with Us. There are many believers, but we all belong to Christ and that is why we are like a body. We all belong to each other.

God gave each one of us gifts, but God did not give the same gifts to each one of us. If God gave some believers the gift of preaching the messages of God, then they must tell people the right message that we believe.
If God gave some other believers the gift of serving, then they must help and serve other people. If God gave some believers the gift of teaching other people, then they must teach them about God.

If God gave some believers the gift of comforting other people, Whoever has the gift of comforting others should do that. Whoever has the gift of giving to help others should give generously. Whoever has the gift of leading should work hard at it. Whoever has the gift of showing kindness to others should do it gladly.
Your love must be real. You must hate evil and hold tight to good things.
Love each other in a way that makes you feel close like brothers and sisters. And give each other more honor than you give yourself.
As you serve the Lord, work hard and don’t be lazy. Be excited about serving him!
Be happy because of the hope you have. Be patient when you have troubles. Pray all the time.
Some of the believers are poor. Help them and take care of them. You must be friendly to strangers.
When people make you suffer and make trouble for you, ask the Lord to bless them. Don’t ask Him to punish them.
When other people are happy, be happy with them and rejoice. When they are sad, be sad and cry with them.

Live together in peace with one another and work together. Don’t be proud but be willing to be friends with people who are not important to others. Don’t think of yourself as smarter than everyone else.
When people do bad things to you, do not try to do bad things to them. You must always do what is right. Try to do what everyone thinks is right.
Try very hard to live in peace with other people.

Dear friends, when people make you suffer and do bad things to you, do not try to punish them yourselves. Wait for God to punish them, because it is written in the Old Testament: The Lord said: I am the One who will punish people for what they have done wrong.
But you should do this: When your enemies are hungry, give them food to eat, and when they are thirsty, give them something to drink. If you do that. In doing this you will make them feel ashamed.
Don’t let evil defeat you, but defeat evil by doing good.

Long ago when the land of Israel did not have a king. There were leaders who ruled the land called judges. There was no food for the people in Judah because there was a famine. Elimelek was a man who stayed in the town of Bethlehem in Judah. He, his wife, and his 2 sons moved to the country of Moab.

Elimelek wife was named Naomi, and Elimelek two sons were named Mahlon and Chilion. They were from the Ephrathah family of Bethlehem in Judah. The family traveled to the country of Moab and stayed there.
Later, Naomi’s husband, Elimelech, died, so only Naomi and her 2 sons, Mahlon and Chilion, were left.
Her sons, Mahlon and Chilion, married women from the country of Moab. One wife’s name was Orpah, and the other wife’s name was Ruth. They lived in Moab about 10 years.
Then Mahlon and Kilion also died. So, Naomi was left alone without her husband, Elimelek, or her 2 sons, Mahlon and Chilion.

While Naomi was in the country of Moab, she heard that the Lord had helped his people. Naomi heard how God had given food to his people in Judah. So, Naomi decided to leave the country of Moab and go back home. Her daughters in law, Orpah and Ruth, also decided to go with her.
Naomi and her daughter in law, Orpah and Ruth, left the place where they had been living and started traveling back to the land of Judah.

Then Naomi stopped, and she said to Ruth and Orpah: You must go back to your mothers and to their homes and you must stay there. You have been good to my 2 sons and to me. I pray that God will also be good to you.
Naomi said I also pray that God will give each of you another husband and a new home. Naomi kissed them and Ruth and Orpah started to cry.
Orpah and Ruth said, we want to come with you and go to your family.
But Naomi said, No daughters, go back to your own homes. Why should you go with me? I can’t have any more sons to be your husbands.

Go back home. I am too old to have a new husband. What do you think? If I marry another husband now and I have 2 baby boys, then they must grow up and become men before you can marry them. How long will that take before you can marry them? It will take too long.
Do you want to wait for so many years? I am sure that you can not wait that long, my daughters. This is very sad for you and it is also very sad for me. God has let bad things happen to me.
Ruth and Orpah cried again. Orpah kissed Naomi and she said goodbye, but Ruth did not want to go away from Naomi, and she held on to her.
Naomi said to Ruth: Orpah has gone back to her family. She has gone back to her people and her gods in the land of Moab. You must go back with her.
But Ruth said to Naomi: Do not tell me to leave you. I want to go with you. I want to stay with you. Your people will be my people and your God will be my God.

Ruth said I want to die in the place where you die and then they must dig a grave and bury me there. I will not leave you as long as I live. I make this promise to you. I want God to listen to what I say. He will be my Witness that I will do what I say.
Naomi saw that Ruth did not want to go home and she did not ask Ruth to go back again.
Naomi and Ruth went together to the town of Bethlehem. The people of Bethlehem were stirred when they saw them. The women from the town asked: Is this really Naomi?
Naomi said to them: My name has changed. My name is not Naomi anymore. My name is now Mara, because God Almighty has made my life very sad and bitter.

I went away from here with everything, my husband and my 2 sons, but God now brings me back with nothing. They have died. Why should you call me Naomi , which means Happy? God All-Powerful has given much trouble to me.
So Naomi and her daughter in law Ruth, the Moabite, came back from the country of Moab. These two women came to Bethlehem at the beginning of the barley harvest.
Now Naomi had a relative of her husband, a man of great wealth and influence, from the family of Elimelech, whose name was Boaz.

One day Ruth, the Moabite, said to Naomi, May I go to the fields. Maybe I can find someone who will be kind to me and let me gather the grain they leave in their field. Naomi said, Yes, my daughter, you may go.

So Ruth went to the fields. She followed the workers who were cutting the grain and Ruth gathered the grain that was left. It happened that part of the field belonged to Boaz, the man from Elimelech’s family.
Boaz came from the town of Bethlehem, and he said to his workers: I pray that God will bless you. And the worker said to Boaz: We pray that God will bless you also.
Boaz looked at Ruth and asked the leader of the people who were cutting the barley: Who does that young woman belong to ?
The leader said: Ruth is the young woman from the land of Moab who came back with Naomi.

Ruth asked us if she could walk behind the people who cut the barley to pick up the barley that falls onto the ground. She has worked on the field from this morning until now. Ruth has not gone home to rest.
Then Boaz said to Ruth: Listen to me my daughter. Do not go and pick up barley on the field of another man. Do not go away from here. Stay here with the women who work for me.

Look where the workers cut the barley and pick up the barley that falls onto the ground. Stay with the women who work for me. I have warned the young men not to bother you, and to be good to you. When you want to drink water, go and drink from the water jars that my workers filled with water.
Ruth bowed down in front of Boaz and Ruth asked him Boaz. Why are you so good to me? I am not from your people. I am a stranger from another land.

Boaz said: The people have told me all about the good things that you have done. When your husband died, you helped Naomi, and you did a lot of things for her. You went away from your father and your mother and your land. You came to stay with people that you did not know.

Boaz said, The Lord will reward you for all the good things you have done. The Lord, the God of Israel, will pay you in full. You have come to him for safety, and he will protect you, as a mother chicken protects her babies under her wings.

Ruth said: Sir, May I continue to find goodness from you, you have comforted me by speaking so kindly to me. I am not one of the women who work for you, but you help me, and you are good and friendly to me.

Boaz said to Ruth, when it is time to eat: Come here. Take some of the bread and dip it in our vinegar. Ruth sat down with the workers and Boaz gave her some roasted grain. Ruth ate until she was full and had some food leftover.
Then Ruth went back to work to pick up barley. Boaz told his servants; Let Ruth gather even around the piles of grain. Don’t stop Ruth or insult her.
Boaz also said that the servants was to made Ruth work easier by dropping some full heads of barley for her. Let Ruth gather that also. Don’t tell her to stop or insult her.
Ruth worked in the fields until evening. Then she separated the grain from the chaff. There was about 6 gallons of barley .

Ruth carried the barley into town to show her mother in law what she had gathered. She also gave her the food that was left from lunch. Ruth 1:19 Naomi asked Ruth: Where did you gather all this grain? Where did you work? Bless the man who noticed you. Then Ruth told her who she had worked with. Ruth said, the man I worked with today is a man named Boaz.

Naomi said to Ruth: The Lord bless him! He has continued showing his kindness to the living as well as the dead.” Then Naomi told her daughter in law, Boaz is one of our relatives. He is one of our protectors.
Then Ruth, the woman from Moab, said to Naomi: Boaz also said to me: You must stay with my workers until they have finished cutting all the barley.
Naomi said to Ruth: Yes, my daughter in law, it will be good for you to go with the women who work for Boaz. The workers on another field could give you problems and tell you to go away.
So Ruth worked near the women who worked for Boaz until they finished cutting all the barley and she stayed with Naomi in her house.
A few days later Naomi said to Ruth: My daughter, I must find a husband and a home so that you will be provided for.
Naomi said to Ruth, you have been working with the women who work for Boaz and he is in our family. Tonight, when it is dark, he will separate the barley tonight at the threshing floor.

You must wash yourself and anoint, put olive oil, on yourself. Put on your clothes and go to the threshing-floor but stay out of the man’s sight. Wait until Boaz has finished eating his food and drinking his wine.

After Boaz eats, he will lie down to rest. Watch where Boaz go so that you will know where he lies down. Go there and lift the cover off his feet. Then lie down there at Boaz feet. When Boaz wakes up, he will tell you what you must do.
Ruth said to Naomi: I will do everything you say to do.
Then Ruth went to the threshing-floor, and she did what Naomi, her mother-in-law, told her to do.
When Boaz finished his food and drink, he was happy, he lay down next to the heap of grain. Then Ruth went to him was very quietly and lifted the cover from his feet and lay down by his feet.
About midnight, Boaz rolled over in his sleep and woke up. He was very afraid because there was a woman lying near his feet
Boaz said, Who are you? Ruth said, I am Ruth, your servant girl. Spread your cover over me. for you are a close relative.

Boaz said: May you be blessed by the LORD, my daughter. because you have been very kind to me. Your kindness to me is greater now than at the beginning. for you have not gone after young men, whether poor or rich.
And now, young woman, you must not be afraid. I will do what you ask me. All the men in this town know that you are a good woman.
And it is true that I, Boaz, am a close relative. But there is a man who is a closer relative to you than I am.

Boaz told Ruth: Stay here tonight. In the morning we will see if he will help you. If he decides to help you, that is fine. If he refuses to help, I, Boaz, promise, as surely as the Lord lives, I will redeem you myself! Now lie down here until morning.
So Ruth lay near Boaz’s feet until morning. She got up before anyone could recognize one another; Boaz said to Ruth, No one must know that a woman was here at the threshing floor.

Then Boaz said, Bring me your cloak that you are wearing. And hold it open. So Ruth held her cloak open, and Boaz measured out 2 and half bushels of barley and put it on Ruth’s back. Then she went to the city.
When Ruth came home to her mother-in-law, Naomi asked Ruth: is that you, my daughter? Ruth told Naomi what Boaz had done for her.
And Ruth said: Boaz also gave me all this barley and he said: When you go back to Naomi, you must give her this barley. Your hands must not be empty.
Naomi said: Ruth, be patient until we hear what happens. Boaz will not rest until he has finished doing what he should do. We will know what will happen before the day is ended.

Boaz went to the place where people gather near the city gates. He sat there until the close relative he had told Ruth about passed by. Boaz called to him, Come and sit here, and the close relative came and sat with Boaz.
Boaz called also gathered ten of the elders of the city and Boaz said to them: Come and sit here with us. Then the 10 elders came and sat down with them.
Then Boaz said to Naomi’s close relative: Naomi has come back from the land of Moab. She decided to sell the field of our brother, Elimelek, who has died.

I thought that you must know about this. Now I ask you, in front of these 10 elders and before these people: If you want to buy it, then you can buy it. But if you do not want to buy the field, then you must tell me, and I will buy it. Naomi’s closest relative said, I will buy the land from her.

Then Boaz said: You must remember that if you buy the field from Naomi, you also acquire the dead man’s wife, Ruth, the Moabite woman. the widow of the deceased. She will have a child who will carry on her husband’s name and keep the land in the family.
When Boaz said this, The close relative answered: No, then I can not buy the field, If I do, I might destroy my own inheritance. So I cannot do it. You can buy the field yourself. I can not buy it.

During that time in Israel, it was the custom in Israel for anyone transferring a right of purchase one person took off a sandal and gave it to the other person. This was like signing an agreement, and the people were witness.
The close relative said to Boaz: You can buy the field and he took off his shoe and he gave it to Boaz.
Boaz said to all the elders and to the people: You can all see today that I will buy the field from Naomi. I will buy the field that belonged to Elimelek and to Kilion and Mahlon, his 2 sons.

I will also marry Ruth, the woman from the land of Moab. She was the wife of Mahlon and if she has a baby boy, he will inherit this field and the name of his family will not die, but it will go on. Today you are the witnesses.

All the elders and the people who were near the city gates said, We are witnesses to all of this. And may the Lord bless this woman who is coming into your home to be like Rachel and Leah. They are the ones who had many children to make the people of Israel strong. And may you become powerful in the tribe of Ephrathah and famous in Bethlehem!
May the Lord bless you with many children through Ruth. May your family become great like the family of Perez, the son Tamar bore for Judah.
Boaz married Ruth. The Lord allowed Ruth to become with child, and she had a son.
4 The women there said to Naomi, Praise the Lord who gave you this child. May he become famous in Israel.
We pray that he will make you happy and that he will help you and take care of you when you are old. Ruth is his mother and she loves you and she is better than 7 sons.
Naomi took the child in her bosom. She started to nurse him as his mom.

The women who lived there said: Naomi has a son. The women said his name would be Obed. He was the father of Jesse, and Jesse was the father of King David. Ruth 1:18 Here are the names of the family of Perez: Perez was the father of Hezron. Ruth 1:19 Hezron was the father of Ram, and Ram was the father of Amminadab. Ruth 1:20 Amminadab was the father of Nahshon, and Nahshon was the father of Salmon. Ruth 1:21 Salmon was the father of Boaz, and Boaz was the father of Obed. Ruth 1:22Obed was the father of Jesse, and Jesse was the father of David.

1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Jude, a servant of Jesus Christ and a brother of James. I am writing to you who are called by God. God the Father loves you, and Jesus Christ keeps you safe.
May God give you more and more mercy, peace, and love.

Dear friends, I really wanted to write to you about the salvation we all share. But I felt I must write instead to beg you to fight for the faith. God gave this faith once and for all to his holy people.

Some sinful people have quietly slipped in among you. Long ago it was written that they would be judged like this. They turn the grace of our God into an excuse for sin, and they say no to Jesus Christ, our only Master and Lord.
You already know all this, but I want to remind you. The Lord saved his people out of Egypt. But later he destroyed those who did not believe.
And remember the angels who did not stay where they belonged but left their proper home. God has kept them in darkness, in chains that last forever, until the day of judgment.
Remember also Sodom and Gomorrah and the towns around them. They were full of sexual sin and went after what was unnatural. They are an example to us, suffering the punishment of eternal fire.
In the same way, these dreamers do wrong with their bodies. They say no to God's power and say evil things about powerful angels.
Even the angel Michael did not do this. When he argued with the devil about the body of Moses, he did not dare to insult him. He only said: The Lord will correct you.
But these people say evil things about whatever they do not understand. And the things they do understand, like wild animals that act without thinking, are the very things that destroy them.
How terrible for them! They have followed the way of Cain. For money, they have rushed into the same mistake as Balaam. And like Korah, they have rebelled, and they will be destroyed.

These people are a danger at your shared meals. They eat with you without any shame, caring only for themselves. They are clouds with no rain, blown along by the wind. They are trees without fruit in the autumn, pulled up by the roots and twice dead.
They are wild waves of the sea, foaming up their own shame. They are wandering stars, and the deepest darkness has been kept for them forever.
Enoch, who lived seven generations after Adam, spoke about these people long ago. He said: Look, the Lord is coming with thousands and thousands of his holy ones.
He is coming to judge everyone. He will show all the sinful people the truth about the sinful things they have done, and about all the harsh words that sinful people have spoken against him.
These people are always complaining and blaming others. They follow their own evil desires. They brag about themselves, and they flatter others to get what they want.
But you, dear friends, remember what the apostles of our Lord Jesus Christ told you before.
They said to you: In the last days there will be people who laugh at the truth and follow their own sinful desires.
These are the people who cause division. They follow their natural desires and do not have the Spirit.
But you, dear friends, must keep making your holy faith strong, and pray in the Holy Spirit.
Keep yourselves in God's love as you wait for the mercy of our Lord Jesus Christ to bring you eternal life.
Be kind to those who have doubts.
Save others by pulling them out of the fire. Show mercy to others, but be careful; hate even the clothing that is stained by their sin.
God is able to keep you from falling. He can bring you into his glorious presence without fault and with great joy.
To the only God, our Saviour, be glory, greatness, power, and control through Jesus Christ our Lord, before all time, now, and forever. Amen.

1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, a prisoner because of Jesus Christ, and Timothy our brother. To Philemon, our dear friend and fellow worker,
and to our sister Apphia, and to Archippus, who fights for God alongside us, and to the church that meets in your house:
May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
I always thank my God when I remember you in my prayers.
I keep hearing about your love and your faith in the Lord Jesus and for all God's people.
I pray that, as you share your faith, you will understand every good thing we have in Christ.
Your love has given me great joy and comfort, my brother, because you have refreshed the hearts of God's people.
So, as a follower of Christ, I could be bold and simply order you to do what is right.
But because I love you, I would rather ask you. I, Paul, am an old man, and now also a prisoner because of Jesus Christ.
I am asking you for my son Onesimus. He became my son while I was in prison.
In the past he was not useful to you, but now he is useful to both you and me.
I am sending him back to you, and he is very dear to me.
I wanted to keep him here with me, so that he could help me in your place while I am in prison for the good news.
But I did not want to do anything without your permission. I want any good you do to be your own free choice, not something forced.
Maybe this is why he was away from you for a short time: so that you could have him back forever,
no longer as a servant, but better than a servant — as a dear brother. He is very dear to me, and even more to you, both as a person and in the Lord.
So if you think of me as your partner, welcome him as you would welcome me.
If he has done you any wrong, or owes you anything, charge it to me.
I, Paul, am writing this with my own hand: I will pay it back. And I will not even mention that you owe me your very life.
Yes, my brother, let me have some joy from you in the Lord. Refresh my heart in Christ.
I am writing to you because I am sure you will obey. I know you will do even more than I ask.
One more thing: please prepare a room for me, because I hope that, through your prayers, God will let me come to you.
Epaphras, who is in prison with me for Christ Jesus, sends you his greetings.
So do Mark, Aristarchus, Demas, and Luke, my fellow workers.
May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.

1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: From the elder. To my dear friend Gaius, whom I love in the truth.
Dear friend, I pray that you are doing well and that your body is as healthy as your soul.
It made me very glad when some believers came and told me how true you are, and how you keep living in the truth.
Nothing makes me happier than to hear that my children are living in the truth.
Dear friend, you are faithful in what you do for the believers, even for those who are strangers to you.
They told the church about your love. Please help them on their journey in a way that honors God.
They went out to serve the name of Christ, and they took no help from people who do not believe.
So we should welcome people like this, to work together for the truth.
I wrote to the church, but Diotrephes, who loves to be first, will not listen to us.

So when I come, I will bring up what he is doing: he tells lies about us with evil words. And he is not satisfied with that. He refuses to welcome the believers, and he stops those who want to, and he puts them out of the church.
Dear friend, do not copy what is evil, but copy what is good. Whoever does good belongs to God, but whoever does evil has not seen God.
Everyone speaks well of Demetrius, and so does the truth itself. We also speak well of him, and you know that what we say is true.
I have much to write to you, but I do not want to write it with pen and ink.
I hope to see you soon, and then we will talk face to face. Peace to you. The friends here send their greetings. Greet each friend there by name.

1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, a servant of God and an apostle of Jesus Christ. I serve to build up the faith of God's chosen people and to help them know the truth that leads to a godly life.
This faith and truth give us the hope of eternal life. God, who never lies, promised this life before time began.
And at the right time God made his message known through the preaching that God our Saviour trusted to me.
To Titus, my true son in the faith we share: May God the Father and Christ Jesus our Saviour give you grace and peace.
I left you in Crete so that you could put right the things that still needed work, and choose leaders for the church in every town, as I told you.
A church leader must have nothing against him. He must be faithful to his wife, and his children must be believers who are not wild or disobedient.
Since a leader is trusted with God's work, he must have nothing against him. He must not be proud, quick-tempered, a heavy drinker, violent, or greedy for money.
Instead, he must welcome strangers, love what is good, and be wise, fair, holy, and self-controlled.
He must hold firmly to the true message he was taught. Then he can encourage others with sound teaching and show those who argue against it where they are wrong.
For there are many people who will not obey. They talk nonsense and lead others away from the truth, especially those who say everyone must follow the Jewish law.
These people must be stopped. They are upsetting whole families by teaching wrong things, and they do it for dishonest money.
Even one of their own prophets said: The people of Crete are always liars, cruel animals, and lazy people who only want to eat.
This is true. So correct them strongly, so that their faith will be sound.
Tell them to stop paying attention to Jewish myths and to the rules of people who turn away from the truth.
To those who are pure, everything is pure. But to those who are sinful and do not believe, nothing is pure; even their minds and their hearts are unclean.
They say they know God, but their actions say no. They are disgusting and disobedient, and they are no good for doing anything right.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: But you, Titus, must teach what agrees with sound teaching.
Teach the older men to be serious, worthy of respect, and wise, and to be strong in faith, in love, and in patience.
In the same way, teach the older women to live in a holy way. They must not tell lies about others or drink too much wine. Instead, they must teach what is good.
Then they can train the younger women to love their husbands and their children,
to be wise and pure, to take care of their homes, to be kind, and to obey their husbands. Then no one can say evil things about the word of God.
In the same way, encourage the younger men to be wise and self-controlled.
In everything, you yourself must be an example of doing good. When you teach, be honest and serious.
Say what is true, so that no one can prove it wrong. Then anyone who is against us will be ashamed, because they have nothing bad to say about us.
Teach servants to obey their masters and to please them in everything. They must not talk back
or steal from them. Instead, they must show that they can be fully trusted. In this way they will make the teaching about God our Saviour beautiful in every way.
For the grace of God has come to save all people.
That grace teaches us to say no to sinful living and to the wrong desires of this world, and to live wisely, doing what is right and pleasing to God,
while we wait for the blessed hope: the glorious coming of our great God and Saviour, Jesus Christ.
Jesus gave himself for us, to set us free from all sin and to make us his own special people, eager to do good.
Teach these things. Encourage the people and correct them with full authority. Do not let anyone look down on you.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Remind the people to obey the rulers and authorities, to listen to them, and to be ready to do good.
Tell them not to say evil things about anyone, and not to argue or fight. Instead, they must be gentle and kind to everyone.

At one time we too were foolish and disobedient. We were fooled, and we were slaves to all kinds of wrong desires and pleasures. We were full of hate and jealousy. People hated us, and we hated each other.
But then the kindness and love of God our Saviour appeared.
God saved us — not because of the good things we had done, but because of his mercy. He saved us by washing us clean and giving us new life through the Holy Spirit.
God poured out the Holy Spirit on us freely through Jesus Christ our Saviour.
By his grace God made us right with himself, so that we could receive what he promised: the hope of eternal life.
This is a true saying. I want you to keep teaching these things, so that those who trust in God will be careful to do good. These things are good and helpful for everyone.
But stay away from foolish arguments, from family-tree lists, and from fights and quarrels about the law. They are useless and a waste of time.
If someone causes division, warn him once, and then a second time. After that, have nothing to do with him.
You know that such a person has turned away from the truth and is sinning. He has judged himself.
As soon as I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, do your best to come to me at Nicopolis, because I have decided to spend the winter there.
Do everything you can to help Zenas the lawyer and Apollos on their way, so that they have everything they need.
Our people must learn to spend their time doing good and meeting the real needs of others, so that their lives are not wasted.
Everyone here with me sends you greetings. Greet our friends in the faith. May grace be with you all. Amen.

1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, Silas, and Timothy. To the church in Thessalonica, who belong to God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ:
May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
Brothers and sisters, we must always thank God for you, and it is right to do so, because your faith is growing more and more, and your love for one another keeps getting stronger.
So we are proud of you among the other churches of God. We tell them how patient you are, and how strong your faith stays through all the trouble and suffering you go through.
All of this shows that God judges fairly, and that you will be counted worthy of God's kingdom, the kingdom you are suffering for.
God is fair: he will pay back trouble to those who trouble you,
and he will give rest to you who are troubled, and to us. This will happen when the Lord Jesus appears from heaven with his powerful angels.
Jesus will come in blazing fire to punish those who do not know God and who will not obey the good news about our Lord Jesus.
They will be punished with destruction that lasts forever. They will be shut out from the presence of the Lord and from his glorious power.
This will happen on the day when Jesus comes to be honored by his holy people and to be admired by everyone who believed — and that includes you, because you believed what we told you.

This is why we always pray for you. We ask our God to make you worthy of the life he called you to, and by his power to complete every good thing you want to do and every work that comes from your faith.
We pray this so that the name of our Lord Jesus will be honored through you, and you through him, because of the grace of our God and the Lord Jesus Christ.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Now, brothers and sisters, we want to talk about the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ and the time when we will be gathered to be with him.
Please do not let your minds be upset or worried by any message — whether by a spirit, a word, or a letter that claims to be from us — saying that the day of the Lord has already come.

Do not let anyone fool you in any way. That day will not come until two things happen first: many people will turn away from God, and the man of sin will appear — the one who is headed for destruction.
This man will fight against everything that people call God or worship, and he will lift himself above it all. He will even sit in God's temple and claim to be God.
Don't you remember? I told you about all this while I was still with you.
And now you know what is holding this man back, so that he will appear only at his proper time.
The secret power of sin is already at work. But the one who holds it back will keep holding it back, until he is taken out of the way.
Then the man of sin will appear. But the Lord Jesus will destroy him with the breath of his mouth, and will wipe him out by the brightness of his coming.
This man of sin will come by the power of Satan, with all kinds of false miracles, signs, and wonders,
and with every evil trick. These tricks will fool the people who are perishing, because they refused to love the truth that would have saved them.
For this reason God will let a powerful lie fool them, so that they believe what is false.
Then all who refused to believe the truth, and were happy to do evil, will be judged.
But we must always thank God for you, brothers and sisters whom the Lord loves. From the beginning God chose you to be saved, by the Holy Spirit making you holy and by your belief in the truth.
God called you to this through the good news we preached, so that you could share in the glory of our Lord Jesus Christ.
So then, brothers and sisters, stand firm. Hold on to the teachings we gave you, whether we spoke them or wrote them in a letter.
May our Lord Jesus Christ himself, and God our Father, who loved us and by his grace gave us comfort that lasts forever and a good hope,
comfort your hearts and make you strong in every good thing you do and say.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Finally, brothers and sisters, pray for us. Pray that the Lord's message will spread quickly and be honored, just as it was with you.
And pray that we will be kept safe from cruel and evil people, because not everyone has faith.
But the Lord is faithful. The Lord will make you strong and keep you safe from the evil one.
We are sure, because of the Lord, that you are doing what we told you, and that you will keep doing it.
May the Lord lead your hearts into God's love and into the patience that Christ gives.
Brothers and sisters, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ we command you: stay away from any believer who is lazy and does not live by the teaching we gave you.
You yourselves know that you should follow our example. We were not lazy when we were with you.
We did not eat anyone's food without paying for it. Instead, we worked hard, day and night, so that we would not be a burden to any of you.
We had the right to ask you for help, but we worked so that we could be an example for you to follow.
Even when we were with you, we gave you this rule: anyone who refuses to work should not eat.
We hear that some of you are lazy. You do no work at all; you only busy yourselves with other people's business.
We command and urge such people, by the Lord Jesus Christ, to settle down, do their work quietly, and earn their own food.
And you, brothers and sisters, never get tired of doing good.
If anyone will not obey what we say in this letter, take note of that person and do not spend time with him, so that he will feel ashamed.
But do not treat him as an enemy. Warn him as a brother.
May the Lord of peace himself give you peace at all times and in every way. The Lord be with you all.
I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand. This is the mark in every letter of mine; this is how I write.
May the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.

1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Simon Peter, a servant and an apostle of Jesus Christ. To those who have received a faith as precious as ours, through the goodness of our God and Saviour Jesus Christ:
May God give you more and more grace and peace, as you come to know God and Jesus our Lord.
God's own power has given us everything we need to live a life that pleases him. He did this by helping us know the One who called us by his own glory and goodness.
Through that glory and goodness, God gave us his great and precious promises. By these promises you can share in God's own nature and escape the ruin that evil desires bring into the world.
So do your best to add to your faith. To your faith, add goodness; to goodness, add knowledge;
to knowledge, add self-control; to self-control, add patience; to patience, add a godly life;
to a godly life, add kindness to your brothers and sisters; and to that kindness, add love.
If you have these things, and they keep growing, they will keep you from being useless. They will help you truly know our Lord Jesus Christ.
But anyone who does not have these things is blind. He cannot see clearly, and he has forgotten that his old sins were washed away.
So, brothers and sisters, work hard to make your calling and your place with God sure. If you do these things, you will never fall.
Then you will be given a rich welcome into the kingdom that lasts forever, the kingdom of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ.
So I will always remind you of these things, even though you already know them and stand firm in the truth you have.
I think it is right to keep reminding you as long as I live in this body.
I know that soon I must leave this body, as our Lord Jesus Christ has shown me.
So I will do my best to make sure that, after I am gone, you will always remember these things.
When we told you about the power of our Lord Jesus Christ and his coming, we were not following clever made-up stories. We saw his greatness with our own eyes.
God the Father gave Jesus honor and glory. A voice came to Jesus from the great glory of God, saying: This is my Son, whom I love. I am very pleased with him.
We ourselves heard that voice from heaven, because we were with Jesus on the holy mountain.

So we are even more sure of the message the prophets spoke. You will do well to pay attention to it. It is like a light shining in a dark place, until the day dawns and the morning star rises in your hearts.
Above all, you must understand that no prophecy of Scripture came from a prophet's own idea.
For prophecy never came simply because a person wanted it to. Instead, people spoke from God as the Holy Spirit led them.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: But there were also false prophets among the people, just as there will be false teachers among you. They will quietly bring in destructive lies. They will even say no to the Master who bought them, and they will bring quick ruin on themselves.
Many people will follow their shameful ways, and because of them, others will speak evil of the way of truth.
These teachers are greedy. They will use clever lies to make money from you. But their judgment has been ready for a long time, and their ruin is not asleep.
God did not spare the angels when they sinned. He threw them down to hell and kept them in chains of darkness, to wait for judgment.
And God did not spare the ancient world. But he saved Noah, who preached what is right, along with seven others, when he brought the flood on the world of sinful people.
God turned the cities of Sodom and Gomorrah into ashes. He condemned them, and made them an example of what will happen to sinful people.
But God rescued Lot, a good man who was troubled by the filthy lives of the evil people around him.
(Day after day, this good man lived among them and was deeply troubled in his soul by the evil things he saw and heard.)
So the Lord knows how to rescue godly people from their troubles, and how to hold sinful people for punishment on the day of judgment.
This is especially true for those who follow their dirty desires and hate authority. These teachers are bold and proud. They are not afraid to insult powerful spirits.
Yet even angels, who are far stronger and more powerful, do not bring insulting charges against those spirits before the Lord.
But these false teachers insult what they do not understand. They are like wild animals that act without thinking, born only to be caught and destroyed. And like animals, they too will be destroyed.
They will be paid back with harm for the harm they have done. They love to enjoy themselves in broad daylight. They are stains and spots among you, enjoying their lies while they eat with you.
Their eyes are full of adultery; they never stop sinning. They trap people who are weak. Their hearts are trained to be greedy. They are under God's curse.
They have left the right way and wandered off. They have followed the way of Balaam son of Beor, who loved to be paid for doing wrong.
But a donkey corrected him for his sin. The donkey, which cannot talk, spoke with a human voice and stopped the prophet's madness.
These false teachers are like wells with no water, like clouds blown away by a storm. The deepest darkness has been kept for them.
They brag with empty, proud words. By appealing to sinful desires, they trap people who are just escaping from those who live wrongly.
They promise these people freedom, while they themselves are slaves to what is rotten. For a person is a slave to whatever controls him.

These people had escaped the filth of the world by knowing our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. But if they get caught in it again and are beaten by it, they are worse off at the end than they were at the start.
It would have been better for them never to have known the right way, than to know it and then turn away from the holy command that was given to them.
What the true sayings say has happened to them: A dog returns to its own vomit, and: A washed pig goes back to rolling in the mud.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Dear friends, this is now my second letter to you. In both letters I have tried to wake up your honest minds by reminding you of things.
I want you to remember the words spoken long ago by the holy prophets, and the command of our Lord and Saviour that came through your apostles.
First of all, you must understand that in the last days people will come who make fun of the truth and follow their own evil desires.
They will say: Jesus promised to come back. So where is he? Ever since our ancestors died, everything has stayed the same, just as it has been since the world was made.
But they choose to forget that long ago God made the heavens by his word, and made the earth out of water and through water.
And by that same water, the world of that time was flooded and destroyed.
By that same word of God, the heavens and earth that exist now are being kept for fire. They are being kept for the day of judgment, when sinful people will be destroyed.
But do not forget this one thing, dear friends: with the Lord, one day is like a thousand years, and a thousand years are like one day.
The Lord is not slow to keep his promise, as some people think of slowness. Instead, he is patient with you. He does not want anyone to be lost, but wants everyone to turn away from sin.

But the day of the Lord will come like a thief. On that day the heavens will disappear with a roar; everything will be burned up and melt in the great heat, and the earth and all that is in it will be laid bare.
Since everything will be destroyed in this way, think what kind of people you should be. You should live holy and godly lives,
as you wait for the day of God and look forward to it. On that day the heavens will be set on fire and destroyed, and everything will melt in the heat.
But we are waiting for what God promised: a new heaven and a new earth, where what is right will live.
So then, dear friends, while you wait for this, do your best to be found by God at peace, without sin, and with nothing against you.
Remember that our Lord is patient so that people can be saved. Our dear brother Paul wrote to you about this too, with the wisdom God gave him.

He writes the same way in all his letters when he speaks about these things. Some of the things in his letters are hard to understand. People who do not know better, and who are not firm in faith, twist his words — and they twist the other Scriptures too — and so they destroy themselves.
So then, dear friends, since you already know this, be careful. Do not let the mistakes of evil people lead you away and cause you to fall from your firm faith.
Instead, keep growing in the grace and knowledge of our Lord and Saviour Jesus Christ. To him be glory both now and forever. Amen.

1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, an apostle of Jesus Christ because God wanted it, and Timothy our brother.
To God's holy people in Colosse, our faithful brothers and sisters in Christ: May God our Father give you grace and peace.
We always thank God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, when we pray for you.
We thank him because we have heard about your faith in Christ Jesus and about your love for all God's people.
Your faith and love come from the hope that is kept safe for you in heaven. You already heard about this hope when you heard the true message, the good news.
This good news came to you, and it is spreading all over the world, growing and changing lives — just as it has done in you since the day you heard it and understood God's grace as it really is.
You learned it from Epaphras, our dear fellow servant. He is a faithful worker for Christ on our behalf,
and he told us about the love the Spirit has given you.
So, since the day we heard about you, we have not stopped praying for you. We ask God to fill you with the knowledge of what he wants, and with all the wisdom and understanding the Spirit gives.
Then you will live in a way that honors the Lord and pleases him in every way: doing all kinds of good, and getting to know God better and better.
We pray that God will make you strong with his own glorious power, so that you will have great patience and not give up, and so that you will be full of joy.
And we pray that you will thank the Father, who has made you able to share in what he has kept for his people in the kingdom of light.
God rescued us from the power of darkness and brought us into the kingdom of his dear Son.
Through the Son we are set free; our sins are forgiven.
The Son is the image of God, whom no one can see. The Son is over all creation.

For through the Son, God made everything: things in heaven and on earth, things you can see and things you cannot see, whether thrones or powers or rulers or authorities. God made everything through the Son and for the Son.
The Son was there before anything else, and the Son holds everything together.
And the Son is the head of the body, which is the church. He is the beginning, the first to rise from death, so that in everything he would be first.
For God was pleased to have all of himself live in the Son.
And through the Son, God chose to bring everything back to himself — things on earth and things in heaven. God made peace through the blood of Christ on the cross.
At one time you were far away from God. In your minds you were his enemies because of the evil things you did.
But now God has brought you back to himself through Christ's death, through the body of Christ. He did this to bring you into his presence holy, with no fault and nothing against you —

if you keep going in the faith, firm and steady, and do not move away from the hope you found in the good news. This is the good news you heard, the news that has been preached to everyone under heaven. And I, Paul, have become a servant of this good news.
Now I am glad about what I suffer for you. In my own body I am completing what is still needed of Christ's sufferings, for the sake of his body, which is the church.
I became a servant of the church when God gave me the job of bringing his full message to you.
This message is the secret that was hidden for ages and generations, but now is made known to God's people.
God wanted them to know how rich and glorious this secret is for all peoples. And the secret is this: Christ lives in you. He is your hope of glory.
So we tell everyone about Christ. We warn each person and teach each person with all wisdom, so that we can bring each person to God fully grown in Christ.
This is the work I am doing. I work hard at it, with all the strength that Christ powerfully gives me.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: I want you to know how hard I am working for you, and for the people in Laodicea, and for everyone who has never met me in person.
I want them to be encouraged in their hearts and joined together in love. I want them to have the full riches of complete understanding, so that they truly know God's secret, which is Christ himself.
In Christ are hidden all the treasures of wisdom and knowledge.
I tell you this so that no one will fool you with smooth talk.
For even though I am far away from you in body, I am with you in spirit. And I am glad to see how well-ordered you are and how firm your faith in Christ is.
So then, just as you accepted Christ Jesus as Lord, keep living in him.
Keep your roots deep in Christ. Build your life on him. Stay strong in the faith you were taught, and overflow with thankfulness.
Be careful that no one traps you with empty, tricky ideas that come from human thinking and from the basic powers of this world, and not from Christ.
For everything that God is lives fully in Christ, in his body.
And you have everything you need in Christ, who is the head over every ruler and power.
In Christ you also went through a kind of circumcision — not one done by human hands. It is the removing of your sinful self, the circumcision that Christ does.
When you were baptized, you were buried with Christ. And you were raised to new life with him, because you believed in the power of God, who raised Christ from death.
You were dead because of your sins and because your sinful self was not yet cut away. But God made you alive together with Christ. God forgave us all our sins.
There was a record of the rules we had broken, a record that stood against us. God wiped it out and took it away, nailing it to the cross.
And God stripped the rulers and powers of their weapons. He shamed them in public, leading them as defeated prisoners by the cross.
So do not let anyone judge you about what you eat or drink, or about a religious feast, a New Moon festival, or a Sabbath day.
These were only a shadow of the things that were coming; the real thing is Christ.

Do not let anyone rob you of your prize. Some people enjoy false humility and the worship of angels. They go on and on about visions they have seen, and their human thinking makes them proud for no reason.
They have lost their hold on Christ, the Head. From the Head the whole body is fed and held together by its joints, and it grows as God makes it grow.
You died with Christ and were set free from the basic powers of this world. So why do you act as if you still belong to the world? Why do you obey rules like these:
Do not handle! Do not taste! Do not touch!?
All these things are meant to be used up and thrown away. They are only human commands and teachings.
These rules look wise, with their self-made religion, false humility, and harsh treatment of the body. But they have no value for stopping the wrong desires of the body.

3.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So then, since you were raised to new life with Christ, set your hearts on the things above, where Christ is sitting at God's right hand.
Think about the things above, not about earthly things.
For you died, and your life is now hidden with Christ in God.
Christ is your life. When Christ appears, then you also will appear with him in glory.
So put to death whatever belongs to your earthly, sinful self: sexual sin, anything unclean, wrong desire, and greed, which is the same as worshiping idols.
Because of these things, God's anger comes on those who disobey him.
You used to live this way, when your life was full of these things.
But now you must get rid of all of them: anger, bad temper, hate, insults, and dirty talk from your mouth.
Do not lie to one another. You have taken off your old self and the things it used to do,
and you have put on a new self. This new self keeps being made fresh, to know God and to become like the God who made it.
Here it does not matter if you are Greek or Jew, circumcised or not, an outsider, a wild tribesman, a slave or free. Christ is everything, and Christ is in everything.
So then, as God's chosen people, holy and dearly loved, clothe yourselves with a tender heart, kindness, a humble mind, gentleness, and patience.
Put up with one another, and forgive one another if anyone has a complaint against someone. Forgive just as the Lord forgave you.
And over all these things put on love, which holds everything together perfectly.
Let the peace of Christ rule in your hearts, since you were called to peace as one body. And be thankful.
Let the message of Christ live in you fully. Teach and warn one another with all wisdom, and sing to God with thankful hearts — psalms, hymns, and songs from the Spirit.
And whatever you do, in word or action, do it all in the name of the Lord Jesus, giving thanks to God the Father through him.
Wives, follow the lead of your husbands, as is right in the Lord.
Husbands, love your wives and do not be harsh with them.
Children, obey your parents in everything, for this pleases the Lord.
Fathers, do not be so hard on your children that they lose heart.
Servants, obey your earthly masters in everything. Do not work only when they are watching, to win their favor. Instead, work with an honest heart, because you respect the Lord.
Whatever you do, do it with all your heart, as if you were working for the Lord and not for people.
Remember that the Lord will reward you with what he has promised his people. It is the Lord Christ you are serving.
Anyone who does wrong will be paid back for the wrong he did, and God treats everyone the same.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Masters, treat your servants in a fair and right way, because you know that you also have a Master in heaven.
Keep on praying. Stay awake as you pray, and give thanks.
And pray for us too, that God will open a door for our message, so that we can tell the secret of Christ. It is for this that I am in prison.
Pray that I will make it clear, as I should.
Be wise in the way you act toward those who are not believers. Make good use of every chance you have.
Let your words always be kind, and seasoned, like food with salt, so that you will know how to answer everyone.
Tychicus will tell you all about how I am doing. He is a dear brother, a faithful worker, and a fellow servant in the Lord.
I am sending him to you for this very reason: so that you may know how we are, and so that he may encourage your hearts.
He is coming with Onesimus, our faithful and dear brother, who is one of you. They will tell you everything that is happening here.
Aristarchus, who is in prison with me, sends you greetings. So does Mark, the cousin of Barnabas. (You have already been told: if Mark comes to you, welcome him.)
Jesus, who is also called Justus, also greets you. These are the only Jewish believers who work with me for God's kingdom, and they have been a comfort to me.
Epaphras, who is one of you and a servant of Christ Jesus, sends you greetings. He always prays hard for you, asking that you will stand firm — fully grown and sure of everything God wants.
I can tell you that he works hard for you and for the people in Laodicea and Hierapolis.
Luke, our dear friend the doctor, and Demas send you greetings.
Give my greetings to the brothers and sisters in Laodicea, and to Nympha and the church that meets in her house.
After this letter is read to you, make sure it is also read in the church of the Laodiceans, and that you also read the letter coming from Laodicea.
Tell Archippus: Be sure to finish the work the Lord gave you to do.
I, Paul, write this greeting with my own hand. Remember that I am in prison. May grace be with you. Amen.

1.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Paul, an apostle. I was not sent by people or chosen by any person. I was sent by Jesus Christ and by God the Father, who raised Jesus from death.
All the brothers and sisters here with me join me in writing. To the churches in Galatia:
May God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give you grace and peace.
Jesus gave himself for our sins to rescue us from this present evil world. He did this because God our Father wanted it.
May God have glory forever and ever. Amen.
I am shocked that you are turning away so quickly from the One who called you by the grace of Christ. You are turning to a different “good news” —
which is not good news at all. Some people are confusing you and trying to twist the good news about Christ.
But even if we, or an angel from heaven, were to preach a different “good news” from the one we preached to you, let that person be cursed.
I have said it before, and I say it again: if anyone preaches to you a “good news” different from the one you received, let that person be cursed.
Am I now trying to win people's approval, or God's? Am I trying to please people? If I were still trying to please people, I would not be a servant of Christ.
Brothers and sisters, I want you to know that the good news I preached does not come from any human being.
I did not receive it from a person, and no one taught it to me. Jesus Christ himself showed it to me.
You have heard how I lived before, in the Jewish religion. I attacked God's church without mercy and tried to destroy it.
In the Jewish religion I was ahead of many people my own age, because I worked so hard to follow the traditions of my ancestors.
But God had set me apart even before I was born, and he called me by his grace. When God was pleased to
show his Son to me, so that I could preach about him among people who are not Jews, I did not ask any human being for advice.
I did not even go up to Jerusalem to see those who were apostles before me. Instead, I went away to Arabia, and later I returned to Damascus.
Three years later I went up to Jerusalem to meet Peter, and I stayed with him fifteen days.
I did not see any of the other apostles, except James, the Lord's brother.
I promise you, before God, that what I am writing to you is true. I am not lying.
Later I went to the regions of Syria and Cilicia.
The churches of Christ in Judea did not know me by sight.
They only kept hearing people say: The man who used to attack us is now preaching the faith he once tried to destroy.
And they praised God because of me.

2.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Fourteen years later, I went up to Jerusalem again, this time with Barnabas. I also took Titus with me.
I went because God showed me I should go. I explained to them the good news that I preach among the people who are not Jews. I spoke privately with the leaders, so that my work would not be wasted.
Yet they did not even force Titus, who was with me, to be circumcised, even though he was a Greek.
This question came up because some false brothers had slipped in among us. They came secretly to spy on the freedom we have in Christ Jesus, so that they could make us slaves again.
But we did not give in to them, not even for a moment, so that the truth of the good news would stay with you.
As for the leaders who seemed important — whatever they were makes no difference to me, because God does not judge by how important a person seems — those leaders added nothing to my message.
Instead, they saw that God had trusted me to take the good news to people who are not Jews, just as he had trusted Peter to take it to the Jews.
For God, who worked through Peter as an apostle to the Jews, also worked through me as an apostle to the other nations.

James, Peter, and John, who were seen as pillars of the church, recognized the grace God had given me. So they shook hands with me and Barnabas as partners. We would go to the other nations, and they would go to the Jews.
They asked only one thing: that we keep remembering the poor — the very thing I was eager to do.
